commentary: As all letters have the letter A for their first, so the world has the eternal God for its first.
en:
A, as its first of letters, every speech maintains;
The 'Primal Deity' is first through all the world's domains.
ta:
அகர முதல எழுத்தெல்லாம் ஆதி
  பகவன் முதற்றே உலகு.

commentary: What Profit have those derived from learning, who worship not the good feet of Him who is possessed of pure knowledge ?
en:
No fruit have men of all their studied lore,
Save they the 'Purely Wise One's' feet adore.
ta:
கற்றதனால் ஆய பயனென்கொல் வாலறிவன்
  நற்றாள் தொழாஅர் எனின்.

commentary: They who are united to the glorious feet of Him who occupies swiftly the flower of the mind, shall flourish in the highest of worlds (heaven).
en:
His feet, 'Who o'er the full-blown flower hath past,' who gain
In bliss long time shall dwell above this earthly plain.
ta:
மலர்மிசை ஏகினான் மாணடி சேர்ந்தார்
  நிலமிசை நீடுவாழ் வார்.

commentary: To those who meditate the feet of Him who is void of desire or aversion, evil shall never come.
en:
His foot, 'Whom want affects not, irks not grief,' who gain
Shall not, through every time, of any woes complain.
ta:
வேண்டுதல் வேண்டாமை இலானடி சேர்ந்தார்க்கு
  யாண்டும் இடும்பை இல.

commentary: The two-fold deeds that spring from darkness shall not adhere to those who delight in the true praise of God.
en:
The men, who on the 'King's' true praised delight to dwell,
Affects not them the fruit of deeds done ill or well.
ta:
இருள்சேர் இருவினையும் சேரா இறைவன்
  பொருள்சேர் புகழ்புரிந்தார் மாட்டு.

commentary: Those shall long proposer who abide in the faultless way of Him who has destroyed the five desires of the senses.
en:
Long live they blest, who 've stood in path from falsehood freed;
His, 'Who quenched lusts that from the sense-gates five proceed'.
ta:
பொறிவாயில் ஐந்தவித்தான் பொய்தீர் ஒழுக்க
  நெறிநின்றார் நீடுவாழ் வார்.

commentary: Anxiety of mind cannot be removed, except from those who are united to the feet of Him who is incomparable.
en:
Unless His foot, 'to Whom none can compare,' men gain,
'Tis hard for mind to find relief from anxious pain.
ta:
தனக்குவமை இல்லாதான் தாள்சேர்ந்தார்க் கல்லால்
  மனக்கவலை மாற்றல் அரிது.

commentary: None can swim the sea of vice, but those who are united to the feet of that gracious Being who is a sea of virtue.
en:
Unless His feet 'the Sea of Good, the Fair and Bountiful,' men gain,
'Tis hard the further bank of being's changeful sea to attain.
ta:
அறவாழி அந்தணன் தாள்சேர்ந்தார்க் கல்லால்
  பிறவாழி நீந்தல் அரிது.

commentary: The head that worships not the feet of Him who is possessed of eight attributes, is as useless as a sense without the power of sensation.
en:
Before His foot, 'the Eight-fold Excellence,' with unbent head,
Who stands, like palsied sense, is to all living functions dead.
ta:
கோளில் பொறியின் குணமிலவே எண்குணத்தான்
  தாளை வணங்காத் தலை.

commentary: None can swim the great sea of births but those who are united to the feet of God.
en:
They swim the sea of births, the 'Monarch's' foot who gain;
None others reach the shore of being's mighty main.
ta:
பிறவிப் பெருங்கடல் நீந்துவர் நீந்தார்
  இறைவன் அடிசேரா தார்.

commentary: By the continuance of rain the world is preserved in existence; it is therefore worthy to be called ambrosia.
en:
The world its course maintains through life that rain unfailing gives;
Thus rain is known the true ambrosial food of all that lives.
ta:
வான்நின்று உலகம் வழங்கி வருதலால்
  தான்அமிழ்தம் என்றுணரற் பாற்று.

commentary: Rain produces good food, and is itself food.
en:
The rain makes pleasant food for eaters rise;
As food itself, thirst-quenching draught supplies.
ta:
துப்பார்க்குத் துப்பாய துப்பாக்கித் துப்பார்க்குத்
  துப்பாய தூஉம் மழை.

commentary: If the cloud, withholding rain, deceive (our hopes) hunger will long distress the sea-girt spacious world.
en:
If clouds, that promised rain, deceive, and in the sky remain,
Famine, sore torment, stalks o'er earth's vast ocean-girdled plain.
ta:
விண்இன்று பொய்ப்பின் விரிநீர் வியனுலகத்து
  உள்நின்று உடற்றும் பசி.

commentary: If the abundance of wealth imparting rain diminish, the labour of the plough must cease.
en:
If clouds their wealth of waters fail on earth to pour,
The ploughers plough with oxen's sturdy team no more.
ta:
ஏரின் உழாஅர் உழவர் புயல்என்னும்
  வாரி வளங்குன்றிக் கால்.

commentary: Rain by its absence ruins men; and by its existence restores them to fortune.
en:
'Tis rain works all: it ruin spreads, then timely aid supplies;
As, in the happy days before, it bids the ruined rise.
ta:
கெடுப்பதூஉம் கெட்டார்க்குச் சார்வாய்மற் றாங்கே
  எடுப்பதூஉம் எல்லாம் மழை.

commentary: If no drop falls from the clouds, not even the green blade of grass will be seen.
en:
If from the clouds no drops of rain are shed.
'Tis rare to see green herb lift up its head.
ta:
விசும்பின் துளிவீழின் அல்லால்மற் றாங்கே
  பசும்புல் தலைகாண்பு அரிது.

commentary: Even the wealth of the wide sea will be diminished, if the cloud that has drawn (its waters) up gives them not back again (in rain).
en:
If clouds restrain their gifts and grant no rain,
The treasures fail in ocean's wide domain.
ta:
நெடுங்கடலும் தன்நீர்மை குன்றும் தடிந்தெழிலி
  தான்நல்கா தாகி விடின்.

commentary: If the heaven dry up, neither yearly festivals, nor daily worship will be offered in this world, to the celestials.
en:
If heaven grow dry, with feast and offering never more,
Will men on earth the heavenly ones adore.
ta:
சிறப்பொடு பூசனை செல்லாது வானம்
  வறக்குமேல் வானோர்க்கும் ஈண்டு.

commentary: If rain fall not, penance and alms-deeds will not dwell within this spacious world.
en:
If heaven its watery treasures ceases to dispense,
Through the wide world cease gifts, and deeds of 'penitence'.
ta:
தானம் தவம்இரண்டும் தங்கா வியன்உலகம்
  வானம் வழங்கா தெனின்.

commentary: If it be said that the duties of life cannot be discharged by any person without water, so without rain there cannot be the flowing of water.
en:
When water fails, functions of nature cease, you say;
Thus when rain fails, no men can walk in 'duty's ordered way'.
ta:
நீர்இன்று அமையாது உலகெனின் யார்யார்க்கும்
  வான்இன்று அமையாது ஒழுக்கு.

commentary: The end and aim of all treatise is to extol beyond all other excellence, the greatness of those who, while abiding in the rule of conduct peculiar to their state, have abandoned all desire.
en:
The settled rule of every code requires, as highest good,
Their greatness who, renouncing all, true to their rule have stood.
ta:
ஒழுக்கத்து நீத்தார் பெருமை விழுப்பத்து
  வேண்டும் பனுவல் துணிவு.

commentary: To describe the measure of the greatness of those who have forsaken the two-fold desires, is like counting the dead.
en:
As counting those that from the earth have passed away,
'Tis vain attempt the might of holy men to say.
ta:
துறந்தார் பெருமை துணைக்கூறின் வையத்து
  இறந்தாரை எண்ணிக்கொண் டற்று.

commentary: The greatness of those who have discovered the properties of both states of being, and renounced the world, shines forth on earth (beyond all others).
en:
Their greatness earth transcends, who, way of both worlds weighed,
In this world take their stand, in virtue's robe arrayed.
ta:
இருமை வகைதெரிந்து ஈண்டுஅறம் பூண்டார்
  பெருமை பிறங்கிற்று உலகு.

commentary: He who guides his five senses by the hook of wisdom will be a seed in the world of heaven.
en:
He, who with firmness, curb the five restrains,
Is seed for soil of yonder happy plains.
ta:
உரனென்னும் தோட்டியான் ஓரைந்தும் காப்பான்
  வரனென்னும் வைப்பிற்கோர் வித்தது.

commentary: Indra, the king of the inhabitants of the spacious heaven, is himself, a sufficient proof of the strength of him who has subdued his five senses.
en:
Their might who have destroyed 'the five', shall soothly tell
Indra, the lord of those in heaven's wide realms that dwell.
ta:
ஐந்தவித்தான் ஆற்றல் அகல்விசும்பு ளார்கோமான்
  இந்திரனே சாலுங் கரி.

commentary: The great will do those things which is difficult to be done; but the mean cannot do them.
en:
Things hard in the doing will great men do;
Things hard in the doing the mean eschew.
ta:
செயற்கரிய செய்வார் பெரியர் சிறியர்
  செயற்கரிய செய்கலா தார்.

commentary: The world is within the knowledge of him who knows the properties of taste, sight, touch, hearing and smell.
en:
Taste, light, touch, sound, and smell: who knows the way
Of all the five,- the world submissive owns his sway.
ta:
சுவைஒளி ஊறுஓசை நாற்றமென ஐந்தின்
  வகைதெரிவான் கட்டே உலகு.

commentary: The hidden words of the men whose words are full of effect, will shew their greatness to the world.
en:
The might of men whose word is never vain,
The 'secret word' shall to the earth proclaim.
ta:
நிறைமொழி மாந்தர் பெருமை நிலத்து
  மறைமொழி காட்டி விடும்.

commentary: The anger of those who have ascended the mountain of goodness, though it continue but for a moment, cannot be resisted.
en:
The wrath 'tis hard e'en for an instant to endure,
Of those who virtue's hill have scaled, and stand secure.
ta:
குணமென்னும் குன்றேறி நின்றார் வெகுளி
  கணமேயும் காத்தல் அரிது.

commentary: The virtuous are truly called Anthanar; because in their conduct towards all creatures they are clothed in kindness.
en:
Towards all that breathe, with seemly graciousness adorned they live;
And thus to virtue's sons the name of 'Anthanar' men give,
ta:
அந்தணர் என்போர் அறவோர்மற் றெவ்வுயிர்க்கும்
  செந்தண்மை பூண்டொழுக லான்.

commentary: Virtue will confer heaven and wealth; what greater source of happiness can man possess ?
en:
It yields distinction, yields prosperity; what gain
Greater than virtue can a living man obtain?
ta:
சிறப்புஈனும் செல்வமும் ஈனும் அறத்தினூஉங்கு
  ஆக்கம் எவனோ உயிர்க்கு.

commentary: There can be no greater source of good than (the practice of) virtue; there can be no greater source of evil than the forgetfulness of it.
en:
No greater gain than virtue aught can cause;
No greater loss than life oblivious of her laws.
ta:
அறத்தினூஉங்கு ஆக்கமும் இல்லை அதனை
  மறத்தலின் ஊங்கில்லை கேடு.

commentary: As much as possible, in every way, incessantly practise virtue.
en:
To finish virtue's work with ceaseless effort strive,
What way thou may'st, where'er thou see'st the work may thrive.
ta:
ஒல்லும் வகையான் அறவினை ஓவாதே
  செல்லும்வாய் எல்லாஞ் செயல்.

commentary: Let him who does virtuous deeds be of spotless mind; to that extent is virtue; all else is vain show.
en:
Spotless be thou in mind! This only merits virtue's name;
All else, mere pomp of idle sound, no real worth can claim.
ta:
மனத்துக்கண் மாசிலன் ஆதல் அனைத்தறன்
  ஆகுல நீர பிற.

commentary: That conduct is virtue which is free from these four things, viz, malice, desire, anger and bitter speech.
en:
'Tis virtue when, his footsteps sliding not through envy, wrath,
Lust, evil speech-these four, man onwards moves in ordered path.
ta:
அழுக்காறு அவாவெகுளி இன்னாச்சொல் நான்கும்
  இழுக்கா இயன்றது அறம்.

commentary: Defer not virtue to another day; receive her now; and at the dying hour she will be your undying friend.
en:
Do deeds of virtue now. Say not, 'To-morrow we'll be wise';
Thus, when thou diest, shalt thou find a help that never dies.
ta:
அன்றறிவாம் என்னாது அறஞ்செய்க மற்றது
  பொன்றுங்கால் பொன்றாத் துணை.

commentary: The fruit of virtue need not be described in books; it may be inferred from seeing the bearer of a palanquin and the rider therein.
en:
Needs not in words to dwell on virtue's fruits: compare
The man in litter borne with them that toiling bear!
ta:
அறத்தாறு இதுவென வேண்டா சிவிகை
  பொறுத்தானோடு ஊர்ந்தான் இடை.

commentary: If one allows no day to pass without some good being done, his conduct will be a stone to block up the passage to other births.
en:
If no day passing idly, good to do each day you toil,
A stone it will be to block the way of future days of moil.
ta:
வீழ்நாள் படாஅமை நன்றாற்றின் அஃதொருவன்
  வாழ்நாள் வழியடைக்கும் கல்.

commentary: Only that pleasure which flows from domestic virtue is pleasure; all else is not pleasure, and it is without praise.
en:
What from virtue floweth, yieldeth dear delight;
All else extern, is void of glory's light.
ta:
அறத்தான் வருவதே இன்பம் மற்றெல்லாம்
  புறத்த புகழும் இல.

commentary: That is virtue which each ought to do, and that is vice which each should shun.
en:
'Virtue' sums the things that should be done;
'Vice' sums the things that man should shun.
ta:
செயற்பால தோரும் அறனே ஒருவற்கு
  உயற்பால தோரும் பழி.

commentary: He will be called a (true) householder, who is a firm support to the virtuous of the three orders in their good path.
en:
The men of household virtue, firm in way of good, sustain
The other orders three that rule professed maintain.
ta:
இல்வாழ்வான் என்பான் இயல்புடைய மூவர்க்கும்
  நல்லாற்றின் நின்ற துணை.

commentary: He will be said to flourish in domestic virtue who aids the forsaken, the poor, and the dead.
en:
To anchorites, to indigent, to those who've passed away,
The man for household virtue famed is needful held and stay.
ta:
துறந்தார்க்கும் துவ்வாதவர்க்கும் இறந்தார்க்கும் இல்வாழ்வான்
  என்பான் துணை.

commentary: The chief (duty of the householder) is to preserve the five-fold rule (of conduct) towards the manes, the Gods, his guests, his relations and himself.
en:
The manes, God, guests kindred, self, in due degree,
These five to cherish well is chiefest charity.
ta:
தென்புலத்தார் தெய்வம் விருந்தொக்கல் தானென்றாங்கு
  ஐம்புலத்தாறு ஓம்பல் தலை.

commentary: His descendants shall never fail who, living in the domestic state, fears vice (in the acquisition of property) and shares his food (with others).
en:
Who shares his meal with other, while all guilt he shuns,
His virtuous line unbroken though the ages runs.
ta:
பழியஞ்சிப் பாத்தூண் உடைத்தாயின் வாழ்க்கை
  வழியெஞ்சல் எஞ்ஞான்றும் இல்.

commentary: If the married life possess love and virtue, these will be both its duty and reward.
en:
If love and virtue in the household reign,
This is of life the perfect grace and gain.
ta:
அன்பும் அறனும் உடைத்தாயின் இல்வாழ்க்கை
  பண்பும் பயனும் அது.

commentary: What will he who lives virtuously in the domestic state gain by going into the other, (ascetic) state ?
en:
If man in active household life a virtuous soul retain,
What fruit from other modes of virtue can he gain?
ta:
அறத்தாற்றின் இல்வாழ்க்கை ஆற்றின் புறத்தாற்றில்
  போஒய்ப் பெறுவ எவன்.

commentary: Among all those who labour (for future happiness) he is greatest who lives well in the household state.
en:
In nature's way who spends his calm domestic days,
'Mid all that strive for virtue's crown hath foremost place.
ta:
இயல்பினான் இல்வாழ்க்கை வாழ்பவன் என்பான்
  முயல்வாருள் எல்லாம் தலை.

commentary: The householder who, not swerving from virtue, helps the ascetic in his way, endures more than those who endure penance.
en:
Others it sets upon their way, itself from virtue ne'er declines;
Than stern ascetics' pains such life domestic brighter shines.
ta:
ஆற்றின் ஒழுக்கி அறனிழுக்கா இல்வாழ்க்கை
  நோற்பாரின் நோன்மை உடைத்து.

commentary: The marriage state is truly called virtue. The other state is also good, if others do not reproach it.
en:
The life domestic rightly bears true virtue's name;
That other too, if blameless found, due praise may claim.
ta:
அறனென்ப் பட்டதே இல்வாழ்க்கை அஃதும்
  பிறன்பழிப்ப தில்லாயின் நன்று.

commentary: He who on earth has lived in the conjugal state as he should live, will be placed among the Gods who dwell in heaven.
en:
Who shares domestic life, by household virtues graced,
Shall, mid the Gods, in heaven who dwell, be placed.
ta:
வையத்துள் வாழ்வாங்கு வாழ்பவன் வான்உநற்யும்
  தெய்வத்துள் வைக்கப் படும்.

commentary: She who has the excellence of home virtues, and can expend within the means of her husband, is a help in the domestic state.
en:
As doth the house beseem, she shows her wifely dignity;
As doth her husband's wealth befit, she spends: help - meet is she.
ta:
மனைக்தக்க மாண்புடையள் ஆகித்தற் கொண்டான்
  வளத்தக்காள் வாழ்க்கைத் துணை.

commentary: If the wife be devoid of domestic excellence, whatever (other) greatness be possessed, the conjugal state, is nothing.
en:
If household excellence be wanting in the wife,
Howe'er with splendour lived, all worthless is the life.
ta:
மனைமாட்சி இல்லாள்கண் இல்லாயின் வாழ்க்கை
  எனைமாட்சித் தாயினும் இல்.

commentary: If his wife be eminent (in virtue), what does (that man) not possess ? If she be without excellence, what does (he) possess ?
en:
There is no lack within the house, where wife in worth excels,
There is no luck within the house, where wife dishonoured dwells.
ta:
இல்லதென் இல்லவள் மாண்பானால் உள்ளதென்
  இல்லவள் மாணாக் கடை.

commentary: What is more excellent than a wife, if she possess the stability of chastity ?
en:
If woman might of chastity retain,
What choicer treasure doth the world contain?
ta:
பெண்ணின் பெருந்தக்க யாவுள கற்பென்னும்
  திண்மைஉண் டாகப் பெறின்.

commentary: If she, who does not worship God, but who rising worships her husband, say, "let it rain," it will rain.
en:
No God adoring, low she bends before her lord;
Then rising, serves: the rain falls instant at her word!
ta:
தெய்வம் தொழாஅள் கொழுநன் தொழுதெழுவாள்
  பெய்யெனப் பெய்யும் மழை.

commentary: She is a wife who unweariedly guards herself, takes care of her husband, and preserves an unsullied fame.
en:
Who guards herself, for husband's comfort cares, her household's fame,
In perfect wise with sleepless soul preserves, -give her a woman's name.
ta:
தற்காத்துத் தற்கொண்டாற் பேணித் தகைசான்ற
  சொற்காத்துச் சோர்விலாள் பெண்.

commentary: What avails the guard of a prison ? The chief guard of a woman is her chastity.
en:
Of what avail is watch and ward?
Honour's woman's safest guard.
ta:
சிறைகாக்கும் காப்பெவன் செய்யும் மகளிர்
  நிறைகாக்கும் காப்பே தலை.

commentary: If women shew reverence to their husbands, they will obtain great excellence in the world where the gods flourish.
en:
If wife be wholly true to him who gained her as his bride,
Great glory gains she in the world where gods bliss abide.
ta:
பெற்றாற் பெறின்பெறுவர் பெண்டிர் பெருஞ்சிறப்புப்
  புத்தேளிர் வாழும் உலகு.

commentary: The man whose wife seeks not the praise (of chastity) cannot walk with lion-like stately step, before those who revile them.
en:
Who have not spouses that in virtue's praise delight,
They lion-like can never walk in scorner's sight.
ta:
புகழ்புரிந்த இல்லிலோர்க்கு இல்லை இகழ்வார்முன்
  ஏறுபோல் பீடு நடை.

commentary: The excellence of a wife is the good of her husband; and good children are the jewels of that goodness.
en:
The house's 'blessing', men pronounce the house-wife excellent;
The gain of blessed children is its goodly ornament.
ta:
மங்கலம் என்ப மனைமாட்சி மற்றுஅதன்
  நன்கலம் நன்மக்கட் பேறு.

commentary: Among all the benefits that may be acquired, we know no greater benefit than the acquisition of intelligent children.
en:
Of all that men acquire, we know not any greater gain,
Than that which by the birth of learned children men obtain.
ta:
பெறுமவற்றுள் யாமறிவது இல்லை அறிவறிந்த
  மக்கட்பேறு அல்ல பிற.

commentary: The evils of the seven births shall not touch those who abtain children of a good disposition, free from vice.
en:
Who children gain, that none reproach, of virtuous worth,
No evils touch them, through the sev'n-fold maze of birth.
ta:
எழுபிறப்பும் தீயவை தீண்டா பழிபிறங்காப்
  பண்புடை மக்கட் பெறின்.

commentary: Men will call their sons their wealth, because it flows to them through the deeds which they (sons) perform on their behalf.
en:
'Man's children are his fortune,' say the wise;
From each one's deeds his varied fortunes rise.
ta:
தம்பொருள் என்பதம் மக்கள் அவர்பொருள்
  தம்தம் வினையான் வரும்.

commentary: The rice in which the little hand of their children has dabbled will be far sweeter (to the parent) than ambrosia.
en:
Than God's ambrosia sweeter far the food before men laid,
In which the little hands of children of their own have play'd.
ta:
அமிழ்தினும் ஆற்ற இனிதேதம் மக்கள்
  சிறுகை அளாவிய கூழ்.

commentary: The touch of children gives pleasure to the body, and the hearing of their words, pleasure to the ear.
en:
To patent sweet the touch of children dear;
Their voice is sweetest music to his ear.
ta:
மக்கள்மெய் தீண்டல் உடற்கின்பம் மற்றுஅவர்
  சொற்கேட்டல் இன்பம் செவிக்கு.

commentary: "The pipe is sweet, the lute is sweet," say those who have not heard the prattle of their own children.
en:
'The pipe is sweet,' 'the lute is sweet,' by them't will be averred,
Who music of their infants' lisping lips have never heard.
ta:
குழல்இனிது யாழ்இனிது என்பதம் மக்கள்
  மழலைச்சொல் கேளா தவர்.

commentary: The benefit which a father should confer on his son is to give him precedence in the assembly of the learned.
en:
Sire greatest boon on son confers, who makes him meet,
In councils of the wise to fill the highest seat.
ta:
தந்தை மகற்காற்று நன்றி அவையத்து
  முந்தி இருப்பச் செயல்.

commentary: That their children should possess knowledge is more pleasing to all men of this great earth than to themselves.
en:
Their children's wisdom greater than their own confessed,
Through the wide world is sweet to every human breast.
ta:
தம்மின்தம் மக்கள் அறிவுடைமை மாநிலத்து
  மன்னுயிர்க் கெல்லாம் இனிது.

commentary: The mother who hears her son called "a wise man" will rejoice more than she did at his birth.
en:
When mother hears him named 'fulfill'd of wisdom's lore,'
Far greater joy she feels, than when her son she bore.
ta:
ஈன்ற பொழுதின் பெரிதுவக்கும் தன்மகனைச்
  சான்றோன் எனக்கேட்ட தாய்.

commentary: (So to act) that it may be said "by what great penance did his father beget him," is the benefit which a son should render to his father.
en:
To sire, what best requital can by grateful child be done?
To make men say, 'What merit gained the father such a son?'
ta:
மகன்தந்தைக்கு ஆற்றும் உதவி இவன்தந்தை
  என்நோற்றான் கொல்எனும் சொல்.

commentary: Is there any fastening that can shut in love ? Tears of the affectionate will publish the love that is within.
en:
And is there bar that can even love restrain?
The tiny tear shall make the lover's secret plain.
ta:
அன்பிற்கும் உண்டோ அடைக்குந்தாழ் ஆர்வலர்
  புன்கணீர் பூசல் தரும்.

commentary: Those who are destitute of love appropriate all they have to themselves; but those who possess love consider even their bones to belong to others.
en:
The loveless to themselves belong alone;
The loving men are others' to the very bone.
ta:
அன்பிலார் எல்லாம் தமக்குரியர் அன்புடையார்
  என்பும் உரியர் பிறர்க்கு.

commentary: They say that the union of soul and body in man is the fruit of the union of love and virtue (in a former birth).
en:
Of precious soul with body's flesh and bone,
The union yields one fruit, the life of love alone.
ta:
அன்போடு இயைந்த வழக்கென்ப ஆருயிர்க்கு
  என்போடு இயைந்த தொடர்பு.

commentary: Love begets desire: and that (desire) begets the immeasureable excellence of friendship.
en:
From love fond yearning springs for union sweet of minds;
And that the bond of rare excelling friendship binds.
ta:
அன்புஈனும் ஆர்வம் உடைமை அதுஈனும்
  நண்பு என்னும் நாடாச் சிறப்பு.

commentary: They say that the felicity which those who, after enjoying the pleasure (of the conjugal state) in this world, obtain in heaven is the result of their domestic state imbued with love.
en:
Sweetness on earth and rarest bliss above,
These are the fruits of tranquil life of love.
ta:
அன்புற்று அமர்ந்த வழக்கென்ப வையகத்து
  இன்புற்றார் எய்தும் சிறப்பு.

commentary: The ignorant say that love is an ally to virtue only, but it is also a help to get out of vice.
en:
The unwise deem love virtue only can sustain,
It also helps the man who evil would restrain.
ta:
அறத்திற்கே அன்புசார் பென்ப அறியார்
  மறத்திற்கும் அஃதே துணை.

commentary: Virtue will burn up the soul which is without love, even as the sun burns up the creature which is without bone, i.e. worms.
en:
As sun's fierce ray dries up the boneless things,
So loveless beings virtue's power to nothing brings.
ta:
என்பி லதனை வெயில்போலக் காயுமே
  அன்பி லதனை அறம்.

commentary: The domestic state of that man whose mind is without love is like the flourishing of a withered tree upon the parched desert.
en:
The loveless soul, the very joys of life may know,
When flowers, in barren soil, on sapless trees, shall blow.
ta:
அன்பகத் தில்லா உயிர்வாழ்க்கை வன்பாற்கண்
  வற்றல் மரந்தளிர்த் தற்று.

commentary: Of what avail are all the external members (of the body) to those who are destitute of love, the internal member.
en:
Though every outward part complete, the body's fitly framed;
What good, when soul within, of love devoid, lies halt and maimed?
ta:
புறத்துறுப் பெல்லாம் எவன்செய்யும் யாக்கை
  அகத்துறுப்பு அன்பி லவர்க்கு.

commentary: That body alone which is inspired with love contains a living soul: if void of it, (the body) is bone overlaid with skin.
en:
Bodies of loveless men are bony framework clad with skin;
Then is the body seat of life, when love resides within.
ta:
அன்பின் வழியது உயிர்நிலை அஃதிலார்க்கு
  என்புதோல் போர்த்த உடம்பு.

commentary: The whole design of living in the domestic state and laying up (property) is (to be able) to exercise the benevolence of hospitality.
en:
All household cares and course of daily life have this in view.
Guests to receive with courtesy, and kindly acts to do.
ta:
இருந்தோம்பி இல்வாழ்வ தெல்லாம் விருந்தோம்பி
  வேளாண்மை செய்தற் பொருட்டு.

commentary: It is not fit that one should wish his guests to be outside (his house) even though he were eating the food of immortality.
en:
Though food of immortality should crown the board,
Feasting alone, the guests without unfed, is thing abhorred.
ta:
விருந்து புறத்ததாத் தானுண்டல் சாவா
  மருந்தெனினும் வேண்டற்பாற் றன்று.

commentary: The domestic life of the man that daily entertains the guests who come to him shall not be laid waste by poverty.
en:
Each day he tends the coming guest with kindly care;
Painless, unfailing plenty shall his household share.
ta:
வருவிருந்து வைகலும் ஓம்புவான் வாழ்க்கை
  பருவந்து பாழ்படுதல் இன்று.

commentary: Lakshmi with joyous mind shall dwell in the house of that man who, with cheerful countenance, entertains the good as guests.
en:
With smiling face he entertains each virtuous guest,
'Fortune' with gladsome mind shall in his dwelling rest.
ta:
அகனமர்ந்து செய்யாள் உறையும் முகனமர்ந்து
  நல்விருந்து ஓம்புவான் இல்.

commentary: Is it necessary to sow the field of the man who, having feasted his guests, eats what may remain ?
en:
Who first regales his guest, and then himself supplies,
O'er all his fields, unsown, shall plenteous harvests rise.
ta:
வித்தும் இடல்வேண்டும் கொல்லோ விருந்தோம்பி
  மிச்சில் மிசைவான் புலம்.

commentary: He who, having entertained the guests that have come, looks out for others who may yet come, will be a welcome guest to the inhabitants of heaven.
en:
The guest arrived he tends, the coming guest expects to see;
To those in heavenly homes that dwell a welcome guest is he.
ta:
செல்விருந்து ஓம்பி வருவிருந்து பார்த்திருப்பான்
  நல்வருந்து வானத் தவர்க்கு.

commentary: The advantages of benevolence cannot be measured; the measure (of the virtue) of the guests (entertained) is the only measure.
en:
To reckon up the fruit of kindly deeds were all in vain;
Their worth is as the worth of guests you entertain.
ta:
இனைத்துணைத் தென்பதொன் றில்லை விருந்தின்
  துணைத்துணை வேள்விப் பயன்.

commentary: Those who have taken no part in the benevolence of hospitality shall (at length lament) saying, "we have laboured and laid up wealth and are now without support."
en:
With pain they guard their stores, yet 'All forlorn are we,' they'll cry,
Who cherish not their guests, nor kindly help supply.
ta:
பரிந்தோம்பிப் பற்றற்றேம் என்பர் விருந்தோம்பி
  வேள்வி தலைப்படா தார்.

commentary: That stupidity which excercises no hospitality is poverty in the midst of wealth. It is the property of the stupid.
en:
To turn from guests is penury, though worldly goods abound;
'Tis senseless folly, only with the senseless found.
ta:
உடைமையுள் இன்மை விருந்தோம்பல் ஓம்பா
  மடமை மடவார்கண் உண்டு.

commentary: As the Anicham flower fades in smelling, so fades the guest when the face is turned away.
en:
The flower of 'Anicha' withers away, If you do but its fragrance inhale;
If the face of the host cold welcome convey, The guest's heart within him will fail.
ta:
மோப்பக் குழையும் அனிச்சம் முகந்திரிந்து
  நோக்கக் குநழ்யும் விருந்து.

commentary: Sweet words are those which imbued with love and free from deceit flow from the mouth of the virtuous.
en:
Pleasant words are words with all pervading love that burn;
Words from his guileless mouth who can the very truth discern.
ta:
இன்சொலால் ஈரம் அளைஇப் படிறுஇலவாம்
  செம்பொருள் கண்டார்வாய்ச் சொல்.

commentary: Sweet speech, with a cheerful countenance is better than a gift made with a joyous mind.
en:
A pleasant word with beaming smile,s preferred,
Even to gifts with liberal heart conferred.
ta:
அகன்அமர்ந்து ஈதலின் நன்றே முகனமர்ந்து
  இன்சொலன் ஆகப் பெறின்.

commentary: Sweet speech, flowing from the heart (uttered) with a cheerful countenance and a sweet look, is true virtue.
en:
With brightly beaming smile, and kindly light of loving eye,
And heart sincere, to utter pleasant words is charity.
ta:
முகத்தான் அமர்ந்து இனிதுநோக்கி அகத்தானாம்
  இன்சொ லினதே அறம்.

commentary: Sorrow-increasing poverty shall not come upon those who use towards all, pleasure-increasing sweetness of speech.
en:
The men of pleasant speech that gladness breathe around,
Through indigence shall never sorrow's prey be found.
ta:
துன்புறூஉம் துவ்வாமை இல்லாகும் யார்மாட்டும்
  இன்புறூஉம் இன்சொ லவர்க்கு.

commentary: Humility and sweetness of speech are the ornaments of man; all others are not (ornaments).
en:
Humility with pleasant speech to man on earth,
Is choice adornment; all besides is nothing worth.
ta:
பணிவுடையன் இன்சொலன் ஆதல் ஒருவற்கு
  அணியல்ல மற்றுப் பிற.

commentary: If a man, while seeking to speak usefully, speaks also sweetly, his sins will diminish and his virtue increase.
en:
Who seeks out good, words from his lips of sweetness flow;
In him the power of vice declines, and virtues grow.
ta:
அல்லவை தேய அறம்பெருகும் நல்லவை
  நாடி இனிய சொலின்

commentary: That speech which, while imparting benefits ceases not to please, will yield righteousness (for this world) and merit (for the next world).
en:
The words of sterling sense, to rule of right that strict adhere,
To virtuous action prompting, blessings yield in every sphere.
ta:
நயன்ஈன்று நன்றி பயக்கும் பயன்ஈன்று
  பண்பின் தலைப்பிரியாச் சொல்.

commentary: Sweet speech, free from harm to others, will give pleasure both in this world and in the next.
en:
Sweet kindly words, from meanness free, delight of heart,
In world to come and in this world impart.
ta:
சிறுமையுவு நீங்கிய இன்சொல் மறுமையும்
  இம்மையும் இன்பம் தரும்.

commentary: Why does he use harsh words, who sees the pleasure which sweet speech yields ?
en:
Who sees the pleasure kindly speech affords,
Why makes he use of harsh, repellant words?
ta:
இன்சொல் இனிதீன்றல் காண்பான் எவன்கொலோ
  வன்சொல் வழங்கு வது.

commentary: To say disagreeable things when agreeable are at hand is like eating unripe fruit when there is ripe.
en:
When pleasant words are easy, bitter words to use,
Is, leaving sweet ripe fruit, the sour unripe to choose.
ta:
இனிய உளவாக இன்னாத கூறல்
  கனிஇருப்பக் காய்கவர்ந் தற்று.

commentary: (The gift of) heaven and earth is not an equivalent for a benefit which is conferred where none had been received.
en:
Assistance given by those who ne'er received our aid,
Is debt by gift of heaven and earth but poorly paid.
ta:
செய்யாமல் செய்த உதவிக்கு வையகமும்
  வானகமும் ஆற்றல் அரிது.

commentary: A favour conferred in the time of need, though it be small (in itself), is (in value) much larger than the world.
en:
A timely benefit, -though thing of little worth,
The gift itself, -in excellence transcends the earth.
ta:
காலத்தி னாற்செய்த நன்றி சிறிதெனினும்
  ஞாலத்தின் மாணப் பெரிது.

commentary: If we weigh the excellence of a benefit which is conferred without weighing the return, it is larger than the sea.
en:
Kindness shown by those who weigh not what the return may be:
When you ponder right its merit, 'Tis vaster than the sea.
ta:
பயன்தூக்கார் செய்த உதவி நயன்தூக்கின்
  நன்மை கடலின் பெரிது.

commentary: Though the benefit conferred be as small as a millet seed, those who know its advantage will consider it as large as a palmyra fruit.
en:
Each benefit to those of actions' fruit who rightly deem,
Though small as millet-seed, as palm-tree vast will seem.
ta:
தினைத்துணை நன்றி செயினும் பனைத்துணையாக்
  கொள்வர் பயன்தெரி வார்.

commentary: The benefit itself is not the measure of the benefit; the worth of those who have received it is its measure.
en:
The kindly aid's extent is of its worth no measure true;
Its worth is as the worth of him to whom the act you do.
ta:
உதவி வரைத்தன்று உதவி உதவி
  செயப்பட்டார் சால்பின் வரைத்து.

commentary: Forsake not the friendship of those who have been your staff in adversity. Forget not be benevolence of the blameless.
en:
Kindness of men of stainless soul remember evermore!
Forsake thou never friends who were thy stay in sorrow sore!
ta:
மறவற்க மாசற்றார் கேண்மை துறவற்க
  துன்பத்துள் துப்பாயார் நட்பு.

commentary: (The wise) will remember throughout their seven-fold births the love of those who have wiped away their affliction.
en:
Through all seven worlds, in seven-fold birth, Remains in mem'ry of the wise.
Friendship of those who wiped on earth, The tears of sorrow from their eyes.
ta:
எழுமை எழுபிறப்பும் உள்ளுவர் தங்கண்
  விழுமந் துடைத்தவர் நட்பு.

commentary: It is not good to forget a benefit; it is good to forget an injury even in the very moment (in which it is inflicted).
en:
'Tis never good to let the thought of good things done thee pass away;
Of things not good, 'tis good to rid thy memory that very day.
ta:
நன்றி மறப்பது நன்றன்று நன்றல்லது
  அன்றே மறப்பது நன்று.

commentary: Though one inflict an injury great as murder, it will perish before the thought of one benefit (formerly) conferred.
en:
Effaced straightway is deadliest injury,
By thought of one kind act in days gone by.
ta:
கொன்றன்ன இன்னா செயினும் அவர்செய்த
  ஒன்றுநன்று உள்ளக் கெடும்.

commentary: He who has killed every virtue may yet escape; there is no escape for him who has killed a benefit.
en:
Who every good have killed, may yet destruction flee;
Who 'benefit' has killed, that man shall ne'er 'scape free!
ta:
எந்நன்றி கொன்றார்க்கும் உய்வுண்டாம் உய்வில்லை
  செய்ந்நன்றி கொன்ற மகற்கு.

commentary: That equity which consists in acting with equal regard to each of (the three) divisions of men [enemies, strangers and friends] is a pre-eminent virtue.
en:
If justice, failing not, its quality maintain,
Giving to each his due, -'tis man's one highest gain.
ta:
தகுதி எனவொன்று நன்றே பகுதியால்
  பாற்பட்டு ஒழுகப் பெறின்.

commentary: The wealth of the man of rectitude will not perish, but will bring happiness also to his posterity.
en:
The just man's wealth unwasting shall endure,
And to his race a lasting joy ensure.
ta:
செப்பம் உடையவன் ஆக்கஞ் சிதைவின்றி
  எச்சத்திற் கேமாப்பு உடைத்து.

commentary: Forsake in the very moment (of acquisition) that gain which, though it should bring advantage, is without equity.
en:
Though only good it seem to give, yet gain
By wrong acquired, not e'en one day retain!
ta:
நன்றே தரினும் நடுவிகந்தாம் ஆக்கத்தை
  அன்றே யொழிய விடல்.

commentary: The worthy and unworthy may be known by the existence or otherwise of good offsprings.
en:
Who just or unjust lived shall soon appear:
By each one's offspring shall the truth be clear.
ta:
தக்கார் தகவிலர் என்பது அவரவர்
  எச்சத்தாற் காணப்ப படும்.

commentary: Loss and gain come not without cause; it is the ornament of the wise to preserve evenness of mind (under both).
en:
The gain and loss in life are not mere accident;
Just mind inflexible is sages' ornament.
ta:
கேடும் பெருக்கமும் இல்லல்ல நெஞ்சத்துக்
  கோடாமை சான்றோர்க் கணி.

commentary: Let him whose mind departing from equity commits sin well consider thus within himself, "I shall perish."
en:
If, right deserting, heart to evil turn,
Let man impending ruin's sign discern!
ta:
கெடுவல்யான் என்பது அறிகதன் நெஞ்சம்
  நடுவொரீஇ அல்ல செயின்.

commentary: The great will not regard as poverty the low estate of that man who dwells in the virtue of equity.
en:
The man who justly lives, tenacious of the right,
In low estate is never low to wise man's sight.
ta:
கெடுவாக வையாது உலகம் நடுவாக
  நன்றிக்கண் தங்கியான் தாழ்வு.

commentary: To incline to neither side, but to rest impartial as the even-fixed scale is the ornament of the wise.
en:
To stand, like balance-rod that level hangs and rightly weighs,
With calm unbiassed equity of soul, is sages' praise.
ta:
சமன்செய்து சீர்தூக்குங் கோல்போல் அமைந்தொருபால்
  கோடாமை சான்றோர்க் கணி.

commentary: Freedom from obliquity of speech is rectitude, if there be (corresponding) freedom from bias of mind.
en:
Inflexibility in word is righteousness,
If men inflexibility of soul possess.
ta:
சொற்கோட்டம் இல்லது செப்பம் ஒருதலையா
  உட்கோட்டம் இன்மை பெறின்.

commentary: The true merchandize of merchants is to guard and do by the things of others as they do by their own.
en:
As thriving trader is the trader known,
Who guards another's interests as his own.
ta:
வாணிகம் செய்வார்க்கு வாணிகம் பேணிப்
  பிறவும் தமபோல் செயின்.

commentary: Self-control will place (a man) among the Gods; the want of it will drive (him) into the thickest darkness (of hell).
en:
Control of self does man conduct to bliss th' immortals share;
Indulgence leads to deepest night, and leaves him there.
ta:
அடக்கம் அமரருள் உய்க்கும் அடங்காமை
  ஆரிருள் உய்த்து விடும்.

commentary: Let self-control be guarded as a treasure; there is no greater source of good for man than that.
en:
Guard thou as wealth the power of self-control;
Than this no greater gain to living soul!
ta:
காக்க பொருளா அடக்கத்தை ஆக்கம்
  அதனினூஉங் கில்லை உயிர்க்கு.

commentary: Knowing that self-control is knowledge, if a man should control himself, in the prescribed course, such self-control will bring him distinction among the wise.
en:
If versed in wisdom's lore by virtue's law you self restrain.
Your self-repression known will yield you glory's gain.
ta:
செறிவறிந்து சீர்மை பயக்கும் அறிவறிந்து
  ஆற்றின் அடங்கப் பெறின்.

commentary: More lofty than a mountain will be the greatness of that man who without swerving from his domestic state, controls himself.
en:
In his station, all unswerving, if man self subdue,
Greater he than mountain proudly rising to the view.
ta:
நிலையின் திரியாது அடங்கியான் தோற்றம்
  மலையினும் மாணப் பெரிது.

commentary: Humility is good in all; but especially in the rich it is (the excellence of) higher riches.
en:
To all humility is goodly grace; but chief to them
With fortune blessed, -'tis fortune's diadem.
ta:
எல்லார்க்கும் நன்றாம் பணிதல் அவருள்ளும்
  செல்வர்க்கே செல்வம் தகைத்து.

commentary: Should one throughout a single birth, like a tortoise keep in his five senses, the fruit of it will prove a safe-guard to him throughout the seven-fold births.
en:
Like tortoise, who the five restrains
In one, through seven world bliss obtains.
ta:
ஒருநம்யுள் ஆமைபோல் ஐந்தடக்கல் ஆற்றின்
  எழுநம்யும் ஏமாப் புடைத்து.

commentary: Whatever besides you leave unguarded, guard your tongue; otherwise errors of speech and the consequent misery will ensue.
en:
Whate'er they fail to guard, o'er lips men guard should keep;
If not, through fault of tongue, they bitter tears shall weep.
ta:
யாகாவா ராயினும் நாகாக்க காவாக்கால்
  சோகாப்பர் சொல்லிழுக்குப் பட்டு.

commentary: If a man's speech be productive of a single evil, all the good by him will be turned into evil.
en:
Though some small gain of good it seem to bring,
The evil word is parent still of evil thing.
ta:
ஒன்றானுந் தீச்சொல் பொருட்பயன் உண்டாயின்
  நன்றாகா தாகி விடும்.

commentary: The wound which has been burnt in by fire may heal, but a wound burnt in by the tongue will never heal.
en:
In flesh by fire inflamed, nature may thoroughly heal the sore;
In soul by tongue inflamed, the ulcer healeth never more.
ta:
தீயினாற் சுட்டபுண் உள்ளாறும் ஆறாதே
  நாவினாற் சுட்ட வடு.

commentary: Virtue, seeking for an opportunity, will come into the path of that man who, possessed of learning and self-control, guards himself against anger.
en:
Who learns restraint, and guards his soul from wrath,
Virtue, a timely aid, attends his path.
ta:
கதங்காத்துக் கற்றடங்கல் ஆற்றுவான் செவ்வி
  அறம்பார்க்கும் ஆற்றின் நுழைந்து.

commentary: Propriety of conduct leads to eminence, it should therefore be preserved more carefully than life.
en:
'Decorum' gives especial excellence; with greater care
'Decorum' should men guard than life, which all men share.
ta:
ஒழுக்கம் விழுப்பந் தரலான் ஒழுக்கம்
  உயிரினும் ஓம்பப் படும்.

commentary: Let propriety of conduct be laboriously preserved and guarded; though one know and practise and excel in many virtues, that will be an eminent aid.
en:
Searching, duly watching, learning, 'decorum' still we find;
Man's only aid; toiling, guard thou this with watchful mind.
ta:
பரிந்தோம்பிக் காக்க ஒழுக்கம் தெரிந்தோம்பித்
  தேரினும் அஃதே துணை.

commentary: Propriety of conduct is true greatness of birth, and impropriety will sink into a mean birth.
en:
'Decorum's' true nobility on earth;
'Indecorum's' issue is ignoble birth.
ta:
ஒழுக்கம் உடைமை குடிமை இழுக்கம்
  இழிந்த பிறப்பாய் விடும்.

commentary: A Brahman though he should forget the Vedas may recover it by reading; but, if he fail in propriety of conduct even his high birth will be destroyed.
en:
Though he forget, the Brahman may regain his Vedic lore;
Failing in 'decorum due,' birthright's gone for evermore.
ta:
மறப்பினும் ஓத்துக் கொளலாகும் பார்ப்பான்
  பிறப்பொழுக்கங் குன்றக் கெடும்.

commentary: Just as the envious man will be without wealth, so will the man of destitute of propriety of conduct be without greatness.
en:
The envious soul in life no rich increase of blessing gains,
So man of 'due decorum' void no dignity obtains.
ta:
அழுக்கா றுடையான்கண் ஆக்கம்போன்று இல்லை
  ஒழுக்க மிலான்கண் உயர்வு.

commentary: Those firm in mind will not slacken in their observance of the proprieties of life, knowing, as they do, the misery that flows from the transgression from them.
en:
The strong of soul no jot abate of 'strict decorum's' laws,
Knowing that 'due decorum's' breach foulest disgrace will cause.
ta:
ஒழுக்கத்தின் ஒல்கார் உரவோர் இழுக்கத்தின்
  ஏதம் படுபாக் கறிந்து.

commentary: From propriety of conduct men obtain greatness; from impropriety comes insufferable disgrace.
en:
'Tis source of dignity when 'true decorum' is preserved;
Who break 'decorum's' rules endure e'en censures undeserved.
ta:
ஒழுக்கத்தின் எய்துவர் மேன்மை இழுக்கத்தின்
  எய்துவர் எய்தாப் பழி.

commentary: Propriety of conduct is the seed of virtue; impropriety will ever cause sorrow.
en:
'Decorum true' observed a seed of good will be;
'Decorum's breach' will sorrow yield eternally.
ta:
நன்றிக்கு வித்தாகும் நல்லொழுக்கம் தீயொழுக்கம்
  என்றும் இடும்பை தரும்.

commentary: Those who study propriety of conduct will not speak evil, even forgetfully.
en:
It cannot be that they who 'strict decorum's' law fulfil,
E'en in forgetful mood, should utter words of ill.
ta:
ஒழுக்க முடையவர்க்கு ஒல்லாவே தீய
  வழுக்கியும் வாயாற் சொலல்.

commentary: Those who know not how to act agreeably to the world, though they have learnt many things, are still ignorant.
en:
Who know not with the world in harmony to dwell,
May many things have learned, but nothing well.
ta:
உலகத்தோடு ஒட்ட ஒழுகல் பலகற்றும்
  கல்லார் அறிவிலா தார்

commentary: The folly of desiring her who is the property of another will not be found in those who know (the attributes of) virtue and (the rights of) property.
en:
Who laws of virtue and possession's rights have known,
Indulge no foolish love of her by right another's own.
ta:
பிறன்பொருளாள் பெட்டொழுகும் பேதைமை ஞாலத்து
  அறம்பொருள் கண்டார்கண் இல்.

commentary: Among all those who stand on the outside of virtue, there are no greater fools than those who stand outside their neighbour's door.
en:
No fools, of all that stand from virtue's pale shut out,
Like those who longing lurk their neighbour's gate without.
ta:
அறன்கடை நின்றாருள் எல்லாம் பிறன்கடை
  நின்றாரின் பேதையார் இல்.

commentary: Certainly they are no better than dead men who desire evil towards the wife of those who undoubtingly confide in them.
en:
They're numbered with the dead, e'en while they live, -how otherwise?
With wife of sure confiding friend who evil things devise.
ta:
விளிந்தாரின் வேறல்லர் மன்ற தெளிந்தாரில்
  தீமை புரிந்துதொழுகு வார்.

commentary: However great one may be, what does it avail if, without at all considering his guilt, he goes unto the wife of another ?
en:
How great soe'er they be, what gain have they of life,
Who, not a whit reflecting, seek a neighbour's wife.
ta:
எனைத்துணையர் ஆயினும் என்னாம் தினைத்துணையும்
  தேரான் பிறனில் புகல்.

commentary: He who thinks lightly of going into the wife of another acquires guilt that will abide with him imperishably and for ever.
en:
'Mere triflel' saying thus, invades the home, so he ensures.
A gain of guilt that deathless aye endures.
ta:
எளிதென இல்லிறப்பான் எய்துமெஞ் ஞான்றும்
  விளியாது நிற்கும் பழி.

commentary: Hatred, sin, fear, disgrace; these four will never leave him who goes in to his neighbour's wife.
en:
Who home ivades, from him pass nevermore,
Hatred and sin, fear, foul disgrace; these four.
ta:
பகைபாவம் அச்சம் பழியென நான்கும்
  இகவாவாம் இல்லிறப்பான் கண்.

commentary: He who desires not the womanhood of her who should walk according to the will of another will be praised as a virtuous house-holder.
en:
Who sees the wife, another's own, with no desiring eye
In sure domestic bliss he dwelleth ever virtuously.
ta:
அறனியலான் இல்வாழ்வான் என்பான் பிறனியலாள்
  பெண்மை நயவா தவன்.

commentary: That noble manliness which looks not at the wife of another is the virtue and dignity of the great.
en:
Manly excellence, that looks not on another's wife,
Is not virtue merely, 'tis full 'propriety' of life.
ta:
பிறன்மனை நோக்காத பேராண்மை சான்றோர்க்கு
  அறனொன்றோ ஆன்ற வொழுக்கு.

commentary: Is it asked, "who are those who shall obtain good in this world surrounded by the terror-producing sea ?" Those who touch not the shoulder of her who belongs to another.
en:
Who 're good indeed, on earth begirt by ocean's gruesome tide?
The men who touch not her that is another's bride.
ta:
நலக்குரியார் யாரெனின் நாமநீர் வைப்பின்
  பிறர்க்குரியாள் தோள்தோயா தார்.

commentary: Though a man perform no virtuous deeds and commit (every) vice, it will be well if he desire not the womanhood of her who is within the limit (of the house) of another.
en:
Though virtue's bounds he pass, and evil deeds hath wrought;
At least, 'tis good if neighbour's wife he covet not.
ta:
அறன்வரையான் அல்ல செயினும் பிறன்வரையாள்
  பெண்மை நயவாமை நன்று.

commentary: To bear with those who revile us, just as the earth bears up those who dig it, is the first of virtues.
en:
As earth bears up the men who delve into her breast,
To bear with scornful men of virtues is the best.
ta:
அகழ்வாரைத் தாங்கும் நிலம்போலத் தம்மை
  இகழ்வார்ப் பொறுத்தல் தலை.

commentary: Bear with reproach even when you can retaliate; but to forget it will be still better than that.
en:
Forgiving trespasses is good always;
Forgetting them hath even higher praise;
ta:
பொறுத்தல் இறப்பினை என்றும் அதனை
  மறத்தல் அதனினும் நன்று.

commentary: To neglect hospitality is poverty of poverty. To bear with the ignorant is might of might.
en:
The sorest poverty is bidding guest unfed depart;
The mightiest might to bear with men of foolish heart.
ta:
இன்நம்யுள் இன்மை விருந்தொரால் வன்மையுள்
  வன்மை மடவார்ப் பொறை.

commentary: If you desire that greatness should never leave, you preserve in your conduct the exercise of patience.
en:
Seek'st thou honour never tarnished to retain;
So must thou patience, guarding evermore, maintain.
ta:
நிறையுடைமை நீங்காமை வேண்டின் பொற்யுடைமை
  போற்றி யொழுகப் படும்.

commentary: (The wise) will not at all esteem the resentful. They will esteem the patient just as the gold which they lay up with care.
en:
Who wreak their wrath as worthless are despised;
Who patiently forbear as gold are prized.
ta:
ஒறுத்தாரை ஒன்றாக வையாரே வைப்பர்
  பொறுத்தாரைப் பொன்போற் பொதிந்து.

commentary: The pleasure of the resentful continues for a day. The praise of the patient will continue until (the final destruction of) the world.
en:
Who wreak their wrath have pleasure for a day;
Who bear have praise till earth shall pass away.
ta:
ஒறுத்தார்க்கு ஒருநாளை இன்பம் பொறுத்தார்க்குப்
  பொன்றுந் துணையும் புகழ்.

commentary: Though others inflict injuries on you, yet compassionating the evil (that will come upon them) it will be well not to do them anything contrary to virtue.
en:
Though others work thee ill, thus shalt thou blessing reap;
Grieve for their sin, thyself from vicious action keep!
ta:
திறனல்ல தற்பிறர் செய்யினும் நோநொந்து
  அறனல்ல செய்யாமை நன்று.

commentary: Let a man by patience overcome those who through pride commit excesses.
en:
With overweening pride when men with injuries assail,
By thine own righteous dealing shalt thou mightily prevail.
ta:
மிகுதியான் மிக்கவை செய்தாரைத் தாந்தம்
  தகுதியான் வென்று விடல்.

commentary: Those who bear with the uncourteous speech of the insolent are as pure as the ascetics.
en:
They who transgressors' evil words endure
With patience, are as stern ascetics pure.
ta:
துறந்தாரின் தூய்மை உடையர் இறந்தார்வாய்
  இன்னாச்சொல் நோற்கிற் பவர்.

commentary: Those who endure abstinence from food are great, next to those who endure the uncourteous speech of others.
en:
Though 'great' we deem the men that fast and suffer pain,
Who others' bitter words endure, the foremost place obtain.
ta:
உண்ணாது நோற்பார் பெரியர் பிறர்சொல்லும்
  இன்னாச்சொல் நோற்பாரின் பின்.

commentary: Let a man esteem that disposition which is free from envy in the same manner as propriety of conduct.
en:
As 'strict decorum's' laws, that all men bind,
Let each regard unenvying grace of mind.
ta:
ஒழுக்காறாக் கொள்க ஒருவன்தன் நெஞ்சத்து
  அழுக்காறு இலாத இயல்பு.

commentary: Amongst all attainable excellences there is none equal to that of being free from envy towords others.
en:
If man can learn to envy none on earth,
'Tis richest gift, -beyond compare its worth.
ta:
விழுப்பேற்றின் அஃதொப்பது இல்லையார் மாட்டும்
  அழுக்காற்றின் அன்மை பெறின்.

commentary: Of him who instead of rejoicing in the wealth of others, envies it, it will be said "he neither desires virtue not wealth."
en:
Nor wealth nor virtue does that man desire 'tis plain,
Whom others' wealth delights not, feeling envious pain.
ta:
அறன்ஆக்கம் வேண்டாதான் என்பான் பிறனாக்கம்
  பேணாது அழுக்கறுப் பான்.

commentary: (The wise) knowing the misery that comes from transgression will not through envy commit unrighteous deeds.
en:
The wise through envy break not virtue's laws,
Knowing ill-deeds of foul disgrace the cause.
ta:
அழுக்காற்றின் அல்லவை செய்யார் இழுக்காற்றின்
  ஏதம் படுபாக்கு அறிந்து.

commentary: To those who cherish envy that is enough. Though free from enemies that (envy) will bring destruction.
en:
Envy they have within! Enough to seat their fate!
Though foemen fail, envy can ruin consummate.
ta:
அழுக்காறு உடையார்க்கு அதுசாலும் ஒன்னார்
  வழுக்காயும் கேடீன் பது.

commentary: He who is envious at a gift (made to another) will with his relations utterly perish destitute of food and rainment.
en:
Who scans good gifts to others given with envious eye,
His kin, with none to clothe or feed them, surely die.
ta:
கொடுப்பது அழுக்கறுப்பான் சுற்றம் உடுப்பதூஉம்
  உண்பதூஉம் இன்றிக் கெடும்.

commentary: Lakshmi envying (the prosperity) of the envious man will depart and introduce him to her sister.
en:
From envious man good fortune's goddess turns away,
Grudging him good, and points him out misfortune's prey.
ta:
அவ்வித்து அழுக்காறு உடையானைச் செய்யவள்
  தவ்வையைக் காட்டி விடும்.

commentary: Envy will destroy (a man's) wealth (in his world) and drive him into the pit of fire (in the world to come.)
en:
Envy, embodied ill, incomparable bane,
Good fortune slays, and soul consigns to fiery pain.
ta:
அழுக்காறு எனஒரு பாவி திருச்செற்றுத்
  தீயுழி உய்த்து விடும்.

commentary: The wealth of a man of envious mind and the poverty of the righteous will be pondered.
en:
To men of envious heart, when comes increase of joy,
Or loss to blameless men, the 'why' will thoughtful hearts employ.
ta:
அவ்விய நெஞ்சத்தான் ஆக்கமும் செவ்வியான்
  கேடும் நினைக்கப் படும்.

commentary: Never have the envious become great; never have those who are free from envy been without greatness.
en:
No envious men to large and full felicity attain;
No men from envy free have failed a sure increase to gain.
ta:
அழுக்கற்று அகன்றாரும் இல்லை அஃதுஇல்லார்
  பெருக்கத்தில் தீர்ந்தாரும் இல்.

commentary: If a man departing from equity covet the property (of others), at that very time will his family be destroyed and guilt be incurred.
en:
With soul unjust to covet others' well-earned store,
Brings ruin to the home, to evil opes the door.
ta:
நடுவின்றி நன்பொருள் வெஃகின் குடிபொன்றிக்
  குற்றமும் ஆங்கே தரும்.

commentary: Those who blush at the want of equity will not commit disgraceful acts through desire of the profit that may be gained.
en:
Through lust of gain, no deeds that retribution bring,
Do they, who shrink with shame from every unjust thing.
ta:
படுபயன் வெஃகிப் பழிப்படுவ செய்யார்
  நடுவன்மை நாணு பவர்.

commentary: Those who desire the higher pleasures (of heaven) will not act unjustly through desire of the trifling joy. (in this life.)
en:
No deeds of ill, misled by base desire,
Do they, whose souls to other joys aspire.
ta:
சிற்றின்பம் வெஃகி அறனல்ல செய்யாரே
  மற்றின்பம் வேண்டு பவர்.

commentary: The wise who have conquered their senses and are free from crime, will not covet (the things of others), with the thought "we are destitute."
en:
Men who have conquered sense, with sight from sordid vision freed,
Desire not other's goods, e'en in the hour of sorest need.
ta:
இலமென்று வெஃகுதல் செய்யார் புலம்வென்ற
  புன்மையில் காட்சி யவர்.

commentary: What is the advantage of extensive and accurate knowledge if a man through covetousness act senselessly towards all ?
en:
What gain, though lore refined of amplest reach he learn,
His acts towards all mankind if covetous desire to folly turn?
ta:
அஃகி அகன்ற அறிவென்னாம் யார்மாட்டும்
  வெஃகி வெறிய செயின்.

commentary: If he, who through desire of the virtue of kindness abides in the domestic state i.e., the path in which it may be obtained, covet (the property of others) and think of evil methods (to obtain it), he will perish.
en:
Though, grace desiring, he in virtue's way stand strong,
He's lost who wealth desires, and ponders deeds of wrong.
ta:
அருள்வெஃகி ஆற்றின்கண் நின்றான் பொருள்வெஃகிப்
  பொல்லாத சூழக் கெடும்.

commentary: Desire not the gain of covetousness. In the enjoyment of its fruits there is no glory.
en:
Seek not increase by greed of gain acquired;
That fruit matured yields never good desired.
ta:
வேண்டற்க வெஃகியாம் ஆக்கம் விளைவயின்
  மாண்டற் கரிதாம் பயன்.

commentary: If it is weighed, "what is the indestructibility of wealth," it is freedom from covetousness.
en:
What saves prosperity from swift decline?
Absence of lust to make another's cherished riches thine!
ta:
அஃகாமை செல்வத்திற்கு யாதெனின் வெஃகாமை
  வேண்டும் பிறன்கைப் பொருள்.

commentary: Lakshmi, knowing the manner (in which she may approach) will immediately come to those wise men who, knowing that it is virtue, covet not the property of others.
en:
Good fortune draws anigh in helpful time of need,
To him who, schooled in virtue, guards his soul from greed.
ta:
அறனறிந்து வெஃகா அறிவுடையார்ச் சேரும்
  திறன்அறிந் தாங்கே திரு.

commentary: To covet (the wealth of another) regardless of consequences will bring destruction. That greatness (of mind) which covets not will give victory.
en:
From thoughtless lust of other's goods springs fatal ill,
Greatness of soul that covets not shall triumph still.
ta:
இறலீனும் எண்ணாது வெஃகின் விறல்ஈனும்
  வேண்டாமை என்னுஞ் செருக்கு.

commentary: Though one do not even speak of virtue and live in sin, it will be well if it be said of him "he does not backbite."
en:
Though virtuous words his lips speak not, and all his deeds are ill.
If neighbour he defame not, there's good within him still.
ta:
அறங்கூறான் அல்ல செயினும் ஒருவன்
  புறங்கூறான் என்றல் இனிது.

commentary: To smile deceitfully (in another's presence) after having reviled him to his destruction (behind his back) is a greater evil than the commission of (every other) sin and the destruction of (every) virtue.
en:
Than he who virtue scorns, and evil deeds performs, more vile,
Is he that slanders friend, then meets him with false smile.
ta:
அறனழீஇ அல்லவை செய்தலின் தீதே
  புறனழீஇப் பொய்த்து நகை.

commentary: Death rather than life will confer upon the deceitful backbiter the profit which (the treatises on) virtue point out.
en:
'Tis greater gain of virtuous good for man to die,
Than live to slander absent friend, and falsely praise when nigh.
ta:
புறங்கூறிப் பொய்த்துயிர் வாழ்தலின் சாதல்
  அறங்கூற்றும் ஆக்கத் தரும்.

commentary: Though you speak without kindness before another's face speak not in his absence words which regard not the evil subsequently resulting from it.
en:
In presence though unkindly words you speak, say not
In absence words whose ill result exceeds your thought.
ta:
கண்ணின்று கண்ணறச் சொல்லினும் சொல்லற்க
  முன்னின்று பின்நோக்காச் சொல்.

commentary: The emptiness of that man's mind who (merely) praises virtue will be seen from the meanness of reviling another behind his back.
en:
The slanderous meanness that an absent friend defames,
'This man in words owns virtue, not in heart,' proclaims.
ta:
அறஞ்சொல்லும் நெஞ்சத்தான் அன்மை புறஞ்சொல்லும்
  புன்மையாற் காணப் படும்.

commentary: The character of the faults of that man who publishes abroad the faults of others will be sought out and published.
en:
Who on his neighbours' sins delights to dwell,
The story of his sins, culled out with care, the world will tell.
ta:
பிறன்பழி கூறுவான் தன்பழி யுள்ளும்
  திறன்தெரிந்து கூறப் படும்.

commentary: Those who know not to live in friendship with amusing conversation will by back-biting estrange even their relatives.
en:
With friendly art who know not pleasant words to say,
Speak words that sever hearts, and drive choice friends away.
ta:
பகச்சொல்லிக் கேளிர்ப் பிரிப்பர் நகச்சொல்லி
  நட்பாடல் தேற்றா தவர்.

commentary: What will those not do to strangers whose nature leads them to publish abroad the faults of their intimate friends ?
en:
Whose nature bids them faults of closest friends proclaim
What mercy will they show to other men's good name?
ta:
துன்னியார் குற்றமும் தூற்றும் மரபினார்
  என்னைகொல் ஏதிலார் மாட்டு.

commentary: The world through charity supports the weight of those who reproach others observing their absence.
en:
'Tis charity, I ween, that makes the earth sustain their load.
Who, neighbours' absence watching, tales or slander tell abroad.
ta:
அறன்நோக்கி ஆற்றுங்கொல் வையம் புறன்நோக்கிப்
  புன்சொல் உரைப்பான் பொறை.

commentary: If they observed their own faults as they observe the faults of others, would any evil happen to men ?
en:
If each his own, as neighbours' faults would scan,
Could any evil hap to living man?
ta:
ஏதிலார் குற்றம்போல் தங்குற்றங் காண்கிற்பின்
  தீதுண்டோ மன்னும் உயிர்க்கு.

commentary: He who to the disgust of many speaks useless things will be despised by all.
en:
Words without sense, while chafe the wise,
Who babbles, him will all despise.
ta:
பல்லார் முனியப் பயனில சொல்லுவான்
  எல்லாரும் எள்ளப் படும்.

commentary: To speak useless things in the presence of many is a greater evil than to do unkind things towards friends.
en:
Words without sense, where many wise men hear, to pour
Than deeds to friends ungracious done offendeth more.
ta:
பயனில பல்லார்முன் சொல்லல் நயனில
  நட்டார்கண் செய்தலிற் றீது.

commentary: That conversation in which a man utters forth useless things will say of him "he is without virtue."
en:
Diffusive speech of useless words proclaims
A man who never righteous wisdom gains.
ta:
நயனிலன் என்பது சொல்லும் பயனில
  பாரித் துரைக்கும் உரை.

commentary: The words devoid of profit or pleasure which a man speaks will, being inconsistent with virtue, remove him from goodness.
en:
Unmeaning, worthless words, said to the multitude,
To none delight afford, and sever men from good.
ta:
நயன்சாரா நன்மையின் நீக்கும் பயன்சாராப்
  பண்பில்சொல் பல்லா ரகத்து.

commentary: If the good speak vain words their eminence and excellence will leave them.
en:
Gone are both fame and boasted excellence,
When men of worth speak of words devoid of sense.
ta:
சீர்மை சிறப்பொடு நீங்கும் பயனில
  நீர்மை யுடையார் சொலின்.

commentary: Call not him a man who parades forth his empty words. Call him the chaff of men.
en:
Who makes display of idle words' inanity,
Call him not man, -chaff of humanity!
ta:
பயனில்சொல் பராட்டு வானை மகன்எனல்
  மக்கட் பதடி யெனல்.

commentary: Let the wise if they will, speak things without excellence; it will be well for them not to speak useless things.
en:
Let those who list speak things that no delight afford,
'Tis good for men of worth to speak no idle word.
ta:
நயனில சொல்லினுஞ் சொல்லுக சான்றோர்
  பயனில சொல்லாமை நன்று.

commentary: The wise who seek after rare pleasures will not speak words that have not much weight in them.
en:
The wise who weigh the worth of every utterance,
Speak none but words of deep significance.
ta:
அரும்பயன் ஆயும் அறிவினார் சொல்லார்
  பெரும்பயன் இல்லாத சொல்.

commentary: Those wise men who are without faults and are freed from ignorance will not even forgetfully speak things that profit not.
en:
The men of vision pure, from wildering folly free,
Not e'en in thoughtless hour, speak words of vanity.
ta:
பொருள்தீர்ந்த பொச்சாந்துஞ் சொல்லார் மருள்தீர்ந்த
  மாசறு காட்சி யவர்.

commentary: Speak what is useful, and speak not useless words.
en:
If speak you will, speak words that fruit afford,
If speak you will, speak never fruitless word.
ta:
சொல்லுக சொல்லிற் பயனுடைய சொல்லற்க
  சொல்லிற் பயனிலாச் சொல்.

commentary: Those who have experience of evil deeds will not fear, but the excellent will fear the pride of sin.
en:
With sinful act men cease to feel the dread of ill within,
The excellent will dread the wanton pride of cherished sin.
ta:
தீவினையார் அஞ்சார் விழுமியார் அஞ்சுவர்
  தீவினை என்னும் செருக்கு.

commentary: Because evil produces evil, therefore should evil be feared more than fire.
en:
Since evils new from evils ever grow,
Evil than fire works out more dreaded woe.
ta:
தீயவை தீய பயத்தலால் தீயவை
  தீயினும் அஞ்சப் படும்.

commentary: To do no evil to enemies will be called the chief of all virtues.
en:
Even to those that hate make no return of ill;
So shalt thou wisdom's highest law, 'tis said, fulfil.
ta:
அறிவினுள் எல்லாந் தலையென்ப தீய
  செறுவார்க்கும் செய்யா விடல்.

commentary: Even though forgetfulness meditate not the ruin of another. Virtue will meditate the ruin of him who thus meditates.
en:
Though good thy soul forget, plot not thy neighbour's fall,
Thy plans shall 'virtue's Power' by ruin to thyself forestall.
ta:
மறந்தும் பிறன்கேடு சூழற்க சூழின்
  அறஞ்சூழம் சூழ்ந்தவன் கேடு.

commentary: Commit not evil, saying, "I am poor": if you do, you will become poorer still.
en:
Make not thy poverty a plea for ill;
Thy evil deeds will make thee poorer still.
ta:
இலன்என்று தீயவை செய்யற்க செய்யின்
  இலனாகும் மற்றும் பெயர்த்து.

commentary: Let him not do evil to others who desires not that sorrows should pursue him.
en:
What ranks as evil spare to do, if thou would'st shun
Affliction sore through ill to thee by others done.
ta:
தீப்பால தான்பிறர்கண் செய்யற்க நோய்ப்பால
  தன்னை அடல்வேண்டா தான்.

commentary: However great be the enmity men have incurred they may still live. The enmity of sin will incessantly pursue and kill.
en:
From every enmity incurred there is to 'scape, a way;
The wrath of evil deeds will dog men's steps, and slay.
ta:
எனைப்பகை யுற்றாரும் உய்வர் வினைப்பகை
  வீயாது பின்சென்று அடும்.

commentary: Destruction will dwell at the heels of those who commit evil even as their shadow that leaves them not.
en:
Man's shadow dogs his steps where'er he wends;
Destruction thus on sinful deeds attends.
ta:
தீயவை செய்தார் கெடுதல் நிழல்தன்னை
  வீயாது அஇஉறைந் தற்று.

commentary: If a man love himself, let him not commit any sin however small.
en:
Beware, if to thyself thyself is dear,
Lest thou to aught that ranks as ill draw near!
ta:
தன்னைத்தான் காதல னாயின் எனைத்தொன்றும்
  துன்னற்க தீவினைப் பால்.

commentary: Know ye that he is freed from destruction who commits no evil, going to neither side of the right path.
en:
The man, to devious way of sin that never turned aside,
From ruin rests secure, whatever ills betide.
ta:
அருங்கேடன் என்பது அறிக மருங்கோடித்
  தீவினை செய்யான் எனின்.

commentary: Benevolence seeks not a return. What does the world give back to the clouds ?
en:
Duty demands no recompense; to clouds of heaven,
By men on earth, what answering gift is given?
ta:
கைம்மாறு வேண்டா கடப்பாடு மாரிமாட்டு
  என்ஆற்றுங் கொல்லோ உலகு.

commentary: All the wealth acquired with perseverance by the worthy is for the exercise of benevolence.
en:
The worthy say, when wealth rewards their toil-spent hours,
For uses of beneficence alone 'tis ours.
ta:
தாளாற்றித் தந்த பொருளெல்லாம் தக்கார்க்கு
  வேளாண்மை செய்தற் பொருட்டு.

commentary: It is difficult to obtain another good equal to benevolence either in this world or in that of the gods.
en:
To 'due beneficence' no equal good we know,
Amid the happy gods, or in this world below.
ta:
புத்தே ளுலகத்தும் ஈண்டும் பெறலரிதே
  ஒப்புரவின் நல்ல பிற.

commentary: He truly lives who knows (and discharges) the proper duties (of benevolence). He who knows them not will be reckoned among the dead.
en:
Who knows what's human life's befitting grace,
He lives; the rest 'mongst dead men have their place.
ta:
ஒத்த தறவோன் உயிர்வாழ்வான் மற்றையான்
  செத்தாருள் வைக்கப் படும்.

commentary: The wealth of that man of eminent knowledge who desires to exercise the benevolence approved of by the world, is like the full waters of a city-tank.
en:
The wealth of men who love the 'fitting way,' the truly wise,
Is as when water fills the lake that village needs supplies.
ta:
ஊருணி நீர்நிறைந் தற்றே உலகவாம்
  பேரறி வாளன் திரு.

commentary: The wealth of a man (possessed of the virtue) of benevolence is like the ripening of a fruitful tree in the midst of a town.
en:
A tree that fruits in th' hamlet's central mart,
Is wealth that falls to men of liberal heart.
ta:
பயன்மரம் உள்ளூர்ப் பழுத்தற்றால் செல்வம்
  நயனுடை யான்கண் படின்.

commentary: If wealth be in the possession of a man who has the great excellence (of benevolence), it is like a tree which as a medicine is an infallible cure for disease.
en:
Unfailing tree that healing balm distils from every part,
Is ample wealth that falls to him of large and noble heart.
ta:
மருந்தாகித் தப்பா மரத்தற்றால் செல்வம்
  பெருந்தகை யான்கண் படின்.

commentary: The wise who know what is duty will not scant their benevolence even when they are without wealth.
en:
E'en when resources fall, they weary not of 'kindness due,'-
They to whom Duty's self appears in vision true.
ta:
இடனில் பருவத்தும் ஒப்புரவிற்கு ஒல்கார்
  கடனறி காட்சி யவர்.

commentary: The poverty of a benevolent man, is nothing but his inability to exercise the same.
en:
The kindly-hearted man is poor in this alone,
When power of doing deeds of goodness he finds none.
ta:
நயனுடையான் நல்கூர்ந்தா னாதல் செயும்நீர
  செய்யாது அமைகலா வாறு.

commentary: If it be said that loss will result from benevolence, such loss is worth being procured even by the sale of one's self.
en:
Though by 'beneficence,' the loss of all should come,
'Twere meet man sold himself, and bought it with the sum.
ta:
ஒப்புரவி னால்வரும் கேடெனின் அஃதொருவன்
  விற்றுக்கோள் தக்க துடைத்து.

commentary: To give to the destitute is true charity. All other gifts have the nature of (what is done for) a measured return.
en:
Call that a gift to needy men thou dost dispense,
All else is void of good, seeking for recompense.
ta:
வறியார்க்கொன்று ஈவதே ஈகைமற் றெல்லாம்
  குறியெதிர்ப்பை நீர துடைத்து.

commentary: To beg is evil, even though it were said that it is a good path (to heaven). To give is good, even though it were said that those who do so cannot obtain heaven.
en:
Though men declare it heavenward path, yet to receive is ill;
Though upper heaven were not, to give is virtue still.
ta:
நல்லாறு எனினும் கொளல்தீது மேலுலகம்
  இல்லெனினும் ஈதலே நன்று.

commentary: (Even in a low state) not to adopt the mean expedient of saying "I have nothing," but to give, is the characteristic of the mad of noble birth.
en:
'I've nought' is ne'er the high-born man's reply;
He gives to those who raise themselves that cry.
ta:
இலனென்னும் எவ்வம் உரையாமை ஈதல்
  குலனுடையான் கண்ணே யுள.

commentary: To see men begging from us in disagreeable, until we see their pleasant countenance.
en:
The suppliants' cry for aid yields scant delight,
Until you see his face with grateful gladness bright.
ta:
இன்னாது இரக்கப் படுதல் இரந்தவர்
  இன்முகங் காணும் அளவு.

commentary: The power of those who perform penance is the power of enduring hunger. It is inferior to the power of those who remove the hunger (of others).
en:
'Mid devotees they're great who hunger's pangs sustain,
Who hunger's pangs relieve a higher merit gain.
ta:
ஆற்றுவார் ஆற்றல் பசிஆற்றல் அப்பசியை
  மாற்றுவார் ஆற்றலின் பின்.

commentary: The removal of the killing hunger of the poor is the place for one to lay up his wealth.
en:
Let man relieve the wasting hunger men endure;
For treasure gained thus finds he treasure-house secure.
ta:
அற்றார் அழிபசி தீர்த்தல் அஃதொருவன்
  பெற்றான் பொருள்வைப் புழி.

commentary: The fiery disease of hunger shall never touch him who habitually distributes his food to others.
en:
Whose soul delights with hungry men to share his meal,
The hand of hunger's sickness sore shall never feel.
ta:
பாத்தூண் மரீஇ யவனைப் பசியென்னும்
  தீப்பிணி தீண்டல் அரிது.

commentary: Do the hard-eyed who lay up and lose their possessions not know the happiness which springs from the pleasure of giving ?
en:
Delight of glad'ning human hearts with gifts do they not know.
Men of unpitying eye, who hoard their wealth and lose it so?
ta:
ஈத்துவக்கும் இன்பம் அறியார்கொல் தாமுடைமை
  வைத்திழக்கும் வன்க ணவர்.

commentary: Solitary and unshared eating for the sake of filling up one's own riches is certainly much more unpleasant than begging.
en:
They keep their garners full, for self alone the board they spread;-
'Tis greater pain, be sure, than begging daily bread!
ta:
இரத்தலின் இன்னாது மன்ற நிரப்பிய
  தாமே தமியர் உணல்.

commentary: Nothing is more unpleasant than death: yet even that is pleasant where charity cannot be exercised.
en:
'Tis bitter pain to die, 'Tis worse to live.
For him who nothing finds to give!
ta:
சாதலின் இன்னாத தில்லை இனிததூஉம்
  ஈதல் இயையாக் கடை.

commentary: Give to the poor and live with praise. There is no greater profit to man than that.
en:
See that thy life the praise of generous gifts obtain;
Save this for living man exists no real gain.
ta:
ஈதல் இசைபட வாழ்தல் அதுவல்லது
  ஊதியம் இல்லை உயிர்க்கு.

commentary: Whatsoever is spoken in the world will abide as praise upon that man who gives alms to the poor.
en:
The speech of all that speak agrees to crown
The men that give to those that ask, with fair renown.
ta:
உரைப்பார் உரைப்பவை எல்லாம் இரப்பார்க்கொன்று
  ஈவார்மேல் நிற்கும் புகழ்.

commentary: There is nothing that stands forth in the world imperishable, except fame, exalted in solitary greatness.
en:
Save praise alone that soars on high,
Nought lives on earth that shall not die.
ta:
ஒன்றா உலகத்து உயர்ந்த புகழல்லால்
  பொன்றாது நிற்பதொன் றில்.

commentary: If one has acquired extensive fame within the limits of this earth, the world of the Gods will no longer praise those sages who have attained that world.
en:
If men do virtuous deeds by world-wide ample glory crowned,
The heavens will cease to laud the sage for other gifts renowned.
ta:
நிலவரை நீள்புகழ் ஆற்றின் புலவரைப்
  போற்றாது புத்தேள் உலகு.

commentary: Prosperity to the body of fame, resulting in poverty to the body of flesh and the stability to the former arising from the death of the latter, are achievable only by the wise.
en:
Loss that is gain, and death of life's true bliss fulfilled,
Are fruits which only wisdom rare can yield.
ta:
நத்தம்போல் கேடும் உளதாகும் சாக்காடும்
  வித்தகர்க் கல்லால் அரிது.

commentary: If you are born (in this world), be born with qualities conductive to fame. From those who are destitute of them it will be better not to be born.
en:
If man you walk the stage, appear adorned with glory's grace;
Save glorious you can shine, 'twere better hide your face.
ta:
தோன்றின் புகழொடு தோன்றுக அஃதிலார்
  தோன்றலின் தோன்றாமை நன்று.

commentary: Why do those who cannot live with praise, grieve those who despise them, instead of grieving themselves for their own inability.
en:
If you your days will spend devoid of goodly fame,
When men despise, why blame them? You've yourself to blame.
ta:
புகழ்பட வாழாதார் தந்நோவார் தம்மை
  இகழ்வாரை நோவது எவன்.

commentary: Not to beget fame will be esteemed a disgrace by the wise in this world.
en:
Fame is virtue's child, they say; if, then,
You childless live, you live the scorn of men.
ta:
வசையென்ப வையத்தார்க் கெல்லாம் இசையென்னும்
  எச்சம் பெறாஅ விடின்.

commentary: The ground which supports a body without fame will diminish in its rich produce.
en:
The blameless fruits of fields' increase will dwindle down,
If earth the burthen bear of men without renown.
ta:
வசையிலா வண்பயன் குன்றும் இசையிலா
  யாக்கை பொறுத்த நிலம்.

commentary: Those live who live without disgrace. Those who live without fame live not.
en:
Who live without reproach, them living men we deem;
Who live without renown, live not, though living men they seem.
ta:
வசையொழிய வாழ்வாரே வாழ்வார் இசையொழிய
  வாழ்வாரே வாழா தவர்.

commentary: The wealth of kindness is wealth of wealth, in as much as the wealth of property is possessed by the basest of men.
en:
Wealth 'mid wealth is wealth 'kindliness';
Wealth of goods the vilest too possess.
ta:
அருட்செல்வம் செல்வத்துள் செல்வம் பொருட்செல்வம்
  பூரியார் கண்ணும் உள.

commentary: (Stand) in the good path, consider, and be kind. Even considering according to the conflicting tenets of the different sects, kindness will be your best aid, (in the acquisition of heavenly bliss.)
en:
The law of 'grace' fulfil, by methods good due trial made,
Though many systems you explore, this is your only aid.
ta:
நல்லாற்றாள் நாடி அருளாள்க பல்லாற்றால்
  தேரினும் அஃதே துணை.

commentary: They will never enter the world of darkness and wretchedness whose minds are the abode of kindness.
en:
They in whose breast a 'gracious kindliness' resides,
See not the gruesome world, where darkness drear abides.
ta:
அருள்சேர்ந்த நெஞ்சினார்க் கில்லை இருள்சேர்ந்த
  இன்னா உலகம் புகல்.

commentary: (The wise) say that the evils, which his soul would dread, will never come upon the man who exercises kindness and protects the life (of other creatures)
en:
Who for undying souls of men provides with gracious zeal,
In his own soul the dreaded guilt of sin shall never feel.
ta:
மன்னுயிர் ஓம்பி அருளாள்வார்க்கு இல்லென்ப
  தன்னுயிர் அஞ்சும் வினை.

commentary: This great rich earth over which the wind blows, is a witness that sorrow never comes upon the kind-hearted.
en:
The teeming earth's vast realm, round which the wild winds blow,
Is witness, men of 'grace' no woeful want shall know.
ta:
அல்லல் அருளாள்வார்க்கு இல்லை வளிவழங்கும்
  மல்லன்மா ஞாலங் கரி.

commentary: (The wise) say that those who neglect kindness and practise cruelties, neglected virtue (in their former birth), and forgot (the sorrows which they must suffer.)
en:
Gain of true wealth oblivious they eschew,
Who 'grace' forsake, and graceless actions do.
ta:
பொருள்நீங்கிப் பொச்சாந்தார் என்பர் அருள்நீங்கி
  அல்லவை செய்தொழுகு வார்.

commentary: As this world is not for those who are without wealth, so that world is not for those who are without kindness.
en:
As to impoverished men this present world is not;
The 'graceless' in you world have neither part nor lot.
ta:
அருளில்லார்க்கு அவ்வுலகம் இல்லை பொருளில்லார்க்கு
  இவ்வுலகம் இல்லாகி யாங்கு.

commentary: Those who are without wealth may, at some future time, become prosperous; those who are destitute of kindness are utterly destitute; for them there is no change.
en:
Who lose the flower of wealth, when seasons change, again may bloom;
Who lose 'benevolence', lose all; nothing can change their doom.
ta:
பொருளற்றார் பூப்பர் ஒருகால் அருளற்றார்
  அற்றார்மற் றாதல் அரிது.

commentary: If you consider, the virtue of him who is without kindness is like the perception of the true being by him who is without wisdom.
en:
When souls unwise true wisdom's mystic vision see,
The 'graceless' man may work true works of charity.
ta:
தெருளாதான் மெய்ப்பொருள் கண்டற்றால் தேரின்
  அருளாதான் செய்யும் அறம்.

commentary: When a man is about to rush upon those who are weaker than himself, let him remember how he has stood (trembling) before those who are stronger than himself.
en:
When weaker men you front with threat'ning brow,
Think how you felt in presence of some stronger foe.
ta:
வலியார்முன் தன்னை நினைக்கதான் தன்னின்
  மெலியார்மேல் செல்லு மிடத்து.

commentary: How can he be possessed of kindness, who to increase his own flesh, eats the flesh of other creatures.
en:
How can the wont of 'kindly grace' to him be known,
Who other creatures' flesh consumes to feed his own?
ta:
தன்னூன் பெருக்கற்குத் தான்பிறிது ஊனுண்பான்
  எங்ஙனம் ஆளும் அருள்.

commentary: As those possess no property who do not take care of it, so those possess no kindness who feed on flesh.
en:
No use of wealth have they who guard not their estate;
No use of grace have they with flesh who hunger sate.
ta:
பொருளாட்சி போற்றாதார்க்கு இல்லை அருளாட்சி
  ஆங்கில்லை ஊன்தின் பவர்க்கு.

commentary: Like the (murderous) mind of him who carries a weapon (in his hand), the mind of him who feasts with pleasure on the body of another (creature), has no regard for goodness.
en:
Like heart of them that murderous weapons bear, his mind,
Who eats of savoury meat, no joy in good can find.
ta:
படைகொண்டார் நெஞ்சம்போல் நன்னூக்காது ஒன்றன்
  உடல்சுவை உண்டார் மனம்.

commentary: If it be asked what is kindness and what its opposite, the answer would be preservation and destruction of life; and therefore it is not right to feed on the flesh (obtained by taking away life).
en:
'What's grace, or lack of grace'? 'To kill' is this, that 'not to kill';
To eat dead flesh can never worthy end fulfil.
ta:
ருளல்லது யாதெனின் கொல்லாமை கோறல்
  பொருளல்லது அவ்வூன் தினல்.

commentary: Not to eat flesh contributes to the continuance of life; therefore if a man eat flesh, hell will not open its mouth (to let him escape out, after he has once fallen in).
en:
If flesh you eat not, life's abodes unharmed remain;
Who eats, hell swallows him, and renders not again.
ta:
உண்ணாமை உள்ளது உயிர்நிலை ஊனுண்ண
  அண்ணாத்தல் செய்யாது அளறு.

commentary: If the world does not destroy life for the purpose of eating, then no one would sell flesh for the sake of money.
en:
'We eat the slain,' you say, by us no living creatures die;
Who'd kill and sell, I pray, if none came there the flesh to buy?
ta:
தினற்பொருட்டால் கொல்லாது உலகெனின் யாரும்
  விலைப்பொருட்டால் ஊன்றருவா ரில்.

commentary: If men should come to know that flesh is nothing but the unclean ulcer of a body, let them abstain from eating it.
en:
With other beings' ulcerous wounds their hunger they appease;
If this they felt, desire to eat must surely cease.
ta:
உண்ணாமை வேண்டும் புலாஅல் பிறிதொன்றன்
  புண்ணது உணர்வார்ப் பெறின்.

commentary: The wise, who have freed themselves from mental delusion, will not eat the flesh which has been severed from an animal.
en:
Whose souls the vision pure and passionless perceive,
Eat not the bodies men of life bereave.
ta:
செயிரின் தலைப்பிரிந்த காட்சியார் உண்ணார்
  உயிரின் தலைப்பிரிந்த ஊன்.

commentary: Not to kill and eat (the flesh of) an animal, is better than the pouring forth of ghee etc., in a thousand sacrifices.
en:
Than thousand rich oblations, with libations rare,
Better the flesh of slaughtered beings not to share.
ta:
அவிசொரிந் தாயிரம் வேட்டலின் ஒன்றன்
  உயிர்செகுத் துண்ணாமை நன்று.

commentary: All creatures will join their hands together, and worship him who has never taken away life, nor eaten flesh.
en:
Who slays nought,- flesh rejects- his feet before
All living things with clasped hands adore.
ta:
கொல்லான் புலாலை மறுத்தானைக் கைகூப்பி
  எல்லா உயிருந் தொழும்.
