commentary: The nature of religious discipline consists, in the endurance (by the ascetic) of the sufferings which it brings on himself, and in abstaining from giving pain to others.
en:
To bear due penitential pains, while no offence
He causes others, is the type of 'penitence'.
ta:
உற்றநோய் நோன்றல் உயிர்க்குறுகண் செய்யாமை
  அற்றே தவத்திற் குரு.

commentary: Austerities can only be borne, and their benefits enjoyed, by those who have practised them (in a former birth); it will be useless for those who have not done so, to attempt to practise them (now).
en:
To 'penitents' sincere avails their 'penitence';
Where that is not, 'tis but a vain pretence.
ta:
தவமும் தவமுடையார்க்கு ஆகும் அதனை
  அஃதிலார் மேற்கொள் வது.

commentary: It is to provide food etc, for the ascetics who have abandoned (the desire of earthly possessions) that other persons have forgotten (to practise) austerity ?
en:
Have other men forgotten 'penitence' who strive
To earn for penitents the things by which they live?
ta:
துறந்தார்க்குத் துப்புரவு வேண்டி மறந்தார்கொல்
  மற்றை யவர்கள் தவம்.

commentary: If (the ascetic) desire the destruction of his enemies, or the aggrandizement of his friends, it will be effected by (the power of) his austerities.
en:
Destruction to his foes, to friends increase of joy.
The 'penitent' can cause, if this his thoughts employ.
ta:
ஒன்னார்த் தெறலும் உவந்தாரை ஆக்கலும்
  எண்ணின் தவத்தான் வரும்.

commentary: Religious dislipline is practised in this world, because it secures the attainment of whatever one may wish to enjoy (in the world to come).
en:
That what they wish may, as they wish, be won,
By men on earth are works of painful 'penance' done.
ta:
வேண்டிய வேண்டியாங் கெய்தலால் செய்தவம்
  ஈண்டு முயலப் படும்.

commentary: Those discharge their duty who perform austerities; all others accomplish their own destruction, through the entanglement of the desire (of riches and sensual pleasure).
en:
Who works of 'penance' do, their end attain,
Others in passion's net enshared, toil but in vain.
ta:
தவஞ்செய்வார் தங்கருமஞ் செய்வார்மற் றல்லார்
  அவஞ்செய்வார் ஆசையுட் பட்டு.

commentary: Just as gold is purified as heated in the fire, will those shine, who have endured the burning of pain (in frequent austerities).
en:
The hotter glows the fining fire, the gold the brighter shines;
The pain of penitence, like fire, the soul of man refines.
ta:
சுடச்சுடரும் பொன்போல் ஒளிவிடும் துன்பஞ்
  சுடச்சுட நோற்கிற் பவர்க்கு.

commentary: All other creatures will worship him who has attained the control of his own soul.
en:
Who gains himself in utter self-control,
Him worships every other living soul.
ta:
தன்னுயிர் தான்அறப் பெற்றானை ஏனைய
  மன்னுயி ரெல்லாந் தொழும்.

commentary: Those who have attained the power which religious discipline confers, will be able also to pass the limit of Yama, (the God of death).
en:
E'en over death the victory he may gain,
If power by penance won his soul obtain.
ta:
கூற்றம் குதித்தலும் கைகூடும் நோற்றலின்
  ஆற்றல் தலைப்பட் டவர்க்குல்.

commentary: Because there are few who practise austerity and many who do not, there are many destitute and few rich in this world.
en:
The many all things lack! The cause is plain,
The 'penitents' are few. The many shun such pain.
ta:
இலர்பல ராகிய காரணம் நோற்பார்
  சிலர்பலர் நோலா தவர்.

commentary: The five elements (of his body) will laugh within him at the feigned conduct of the deceitful minded man.
en:
Who with deceitful mind in false way walks of covert sin,
The five-fold elements his frame compose, decide within.
ta:
வஞ்ச மனத்தான் படிற்றொழுக்கம் பூதங்கள்
  ஐந்தும் அகத்தே நகும்.

commentary: What avails an appearance (of sanctity) high as heaven, if his mind suffers (the indulgence) of conscious sin.
en:
What gain, though virtue's semblance high as heaven his fame exalt,
If heart dies down through sense of self-detected fault?
ta:
வானுயர் தோற்றம் எவன்செய்யும் தன்னெஞ்சம்
  தான்அறி குற்றப் படின்.

commentary: The assumed appearance of power, by a man who has no power (to restrain his senses and perform austerity), is like a cow feeding on grass covered with a tiger's skin.
en:
As if a steer should graze wrapped round with tiger's skin,
Is show of virtuous might when weakness lurks within.
ta:
வலியில் நிலைமையான் வல்லுருவம் பெற்றம்
  புலியின்தோல் போர்த்துமேய்ந் தற்று.

commentary: He who hides himself under the mask of an ascetic and commits sins, like a sportsman who conceals himself in the thicket to catch birds.
en:
'Tis as a fowler, silly birds to snare, in thicket lurks.
When, clad in stern ascetic garb, one secret evil works.
ta:
தவமறைந்து அல்லவை செய்தல் புதல்மறைந்து
  வேட்டுவன் புள்சிமிழ்த் தற்று.

commentary: The false conduct of those who say they have renounced all desire will one day bring them sorrows that will make them cry out, "Oh! what have we done, what have we done."
en:
'Our souls are free,' who say, yet practise evil secretly,
'What folly have we wrought!' by many shames o'er-whelmed, shall cry.
ta:
பற்றற்றேம் என்பார் படிற்றொழுக்கம் எற்றெற்றென்று
  ஏதம் பலவுந் தரும்.

commentary: Amongst living men there are none so hard-hearted as those who without to saking (desire) in their heart, falsely take the appearance of those who have forsaken (it).
en:
In mind renouncing nought, in speech renouncing every tie,
Who guileful live,- no men are found than these of 'harder eye'.
ta:
நெஞ்சின் துறவார் துறந்தார்போல் வஞ்சித்து
  வாழ்வாரின் வன்கணார் இல்.

commentary: (The world) contains persons whose outside appears (as fair) as the (red) berry of the Abrus, but whose inside is as black as the nose of that berry.
en:
Outward, they shine as 'kunri' berry's scarlet bright;
Inward, like tip of 'kunri' bead, as black as night.
ta:
புறங்குன்றி கண்டனைய ரேனும் அகங்குன்றி
  முக்கிற் கரியார் உடைத்து.

commentary: There are many men of masked conduct, who perform their ablutions, and (make a show) of greatness, while their mind is defiled (with guilt).
en:
Many wash in hollowed waters, living lives of hidden shame;
Foul in heart, yet high upraised of men in virtuous fame.
ta:
மனத்தது மாசாக மாண்டார் நீராடி
  மறைந்தொழுகு மாந்தர் பலர்.

commentary: As, in its use, the arrow is crooked, and the curved lute is straight, so by their deeds, (and not by their appearance) let (the uprightness or crookedness of) men be estimated.
en:
Cruel is the arrow straight, the crooked lute is sweet,
Judge by their deeds the many forms of men you meet.
ta:
கணைகொடிது யாழ்கோடு செவ்விதுஆங் கன்ன
  வினைபடு பாலால் கொளல்.

commentary: There is no need of a shaven crown, nor of tangled hair, if a man abstain from those deeds which the wise have condemned.
en:
What's the worth of shaven head or tresses long,
If you shun what all the world condemns as wrong?
ta:
மழித்தலும் நீட்டலும் வேண்டா உலகம்
  பழித்தது ஒழித்து விடின்.

commentary: Let him, who desires not to be despised, keep his mind from (the desire of) defrauding another of the smallest thing.
en:
Who seeks heaven's joys, from impious levity secure,
Let him from every fraud preserve his spirit pure.
ta:
எள்ளாமை வேண்டுவான் என்பான் எனைத்தொன்றும்
  கள்ளாமை காக்கதன் நெஞ்சு.

commentary: Even the thought (of sin) is sin; think not then of crafiily stealing the property of another.
en:
'Tis sin if in the mind man but thought conceive;
'By fraud I will my neighbour of his wealth bereave.'
ta:
உள்ளத்தால் உள்ளலும் தீதே பிறன்பொருளைக்
  கள்ளத்தால் கள்வேம் எனல்.

commentary: The property, which is acquired by fraud, will entirely perish, even while it seems to increase.
en:
The gain that comes by fraud, although it seems to grow
With limitless increase, to ruin swift shall go.
ta:
களவினால் ஆகிய ஆக்கம் அளவிறந்து
  ஆவது போலக் கெடும்.

commentary: The eager desire of defrauding others will, when it brings forth its fruit, produce undying sorrow.
en:
The lust inveterate of fraudful gain,
Yields as its fruit undying pain.
ta:
களவின்கண் கன்றிய காதல் விளைவின்கண்
  வீயா விழுமம் தரும்.

commentary: The study of kindness and the exercise of benevolence is not with those who watch for another's forgetfulness, though desire of his property.
en:
'Grace' is not in their thoughts, nor know they kind affection's power,
Who neighbour's goods desire, and watch for his unguarded hour.
ta:
அருள்கருதி அன்புடைய ராதல் பொருள்கருதிப்
  பொச்சாப்புப் பார்ப்பார்கண் இல்.

commentary: They cannot walk steadfastly, according to rule, who eagerly desire to defraud others.
en:
They cannot walk restrained in wisdom's measured bound,
In whom inveterate lust of fraudful gain is found.
ta:
அளவின்கண் நின்றொழுகல் ஆற்றார் களவின்கண்
  கன்றிய காத லவர்.

commentary: That black-knowledge which is called fraud, is not in those who desire that greatness which is called rectitude.
en:
Practice of fraud's dark cunning arts they shun,
Who long for power by 'measured wisdom' won.
ta:
களவென்னும் காரறி வாண்மை அளவென்னும்
  ஆற்றல் புரிந்தார்கண்ட இல்.

commentary: Deceit dwells in the mind of those who are conversant with fraud, even as virtue in the minds of those who are conversant with rectitude.
en:
As virtue dwells in heart that 'measured wisdom' gains;
Deceit in hearts of fraudful men established reigns.
ta:
அளவற஧ந்தார் நெஞ்சத் தறம்போல நிற்கும்
  களவறிந்தார் நெஞ்சில் கரவு.

commentary: Those, who are acquainted with nothing but fraud, will perish in the very commission of transgression.
en:
Who have no lore save that which fraudful arts supply,
Acts of unmeasured vice committing straightway die.
ta:
அளவல்ல செய்தாங்கே வீவர் களவல்ல
  மற்றைய தேற்றா தவர்.

commentary: Even their body will fail the fraudulent; but even the world of the gods will not fail those who are free from fraud.
en:
The fraudful forfeit life and being here below;
Who fraud eschew the bliss of heavenly beings know.
ta:
கள்வார்க்குத் தள்ளும் உயிர்நிலை கள்வார்க்குத்
  தள்ளாது புத்தே ளுளகு.

commentary: Truth is the speaking of such words as are free from the least degree of evil (to others).
en:
You ask, in lips of men what 'truth' may be;
'Tis speech from every taint of evil free.
ta:
வாய்மை எனப்படுவது யாதெனின் யாதொன்றும்
  தீமை இலாத சொலல்.

commentary: Even falsehood has the nature of truth, if it confer a benefit that is free from fault.
en:
Falsehood may take the place of truthful word,
If blessing, free from fault, it can afford.
ta:
பொய்மையும் வாய்மை யிடத்த புரைதீர்ந்த
  நன்மை பயக்கும் எனின்.

commentary: Let not a man knowingly tell a lie; for after he has told the lie, his mind will burn him (with the memory of his guilt).
en:
Speak not a word which false thy own heart knows
Self-kindled fire within the false one's spirit glows.
ta:
தன்நெஞ் சறிவது பொய்யற்க பொய்த்தபின்
  தன்நெஞ்சே தன்னைச் சுடும்.

commentary: He who, in his conduct, preserves a mind free from deceit, will dwell in the minds of all men.
en:
True to his inmost soul who lives,- enshrined
He lives in souls of all mankind.
ta:
உள்ளத்தாற் பொய்யா தொழுகின் உலகத்தார்
  உள்ளத்து ளெல்லாம் உளன்.

commentary: He, who speaks truth with all his heart, is superior to those who make gifts and practise austerities.
en:
Greater is he who speaks the truth with full consenting mind.
Than men whose lives have penitence and charity combined.
ta:
மனத்தொடு வாய்மை மொழியின் தவத்தொடு
  தானஞ்செய் வாரின் தலை.

commentary: There is no praise like the praise of never uttering a falsehood: without causing any suffering, it will lead to every virtue.
en:
No praise like that of words from falsehood free;
This every virtue yields spontaneously.
ta:
பொய்யாமை அன்ன புகழில்லை எய்யாமை
  எல்லா அறமுந் தரும்.

commentary: If a man has the power to abstain from falsehood, it will be well with him, even though he practise no other virtue.
en:
If all your life be utter truth, the truth alone,
'Tis well, though other virtuous acts be left undone.
ta:
பொய்யாமை பொய்யாமை ஆற்றின் அறம்பிற
  செய்யாமை செய்யாமை நன்று.

commentary: Purity of body is produced by water and purity of mind by truthfulness.
en:
Outward purity the water will bestow;
Inward purity from truth alone will flow.
ta:
புறள்தூய்மை நீரான் அமையும் அகந்தூய்மை
  வாய்மையால் காணப் படும்.

commentary: All lamps of nature are not lamps; the lamp of truth is the lamp of the wise.
en:
Every lamp is not a lamp in wise men's sight;
That's the lamp with truth's pure radiance bright.
ta:
எல்லா விளக்கும் விளக்கல்ல சான்றோர்க்குப்
  பொய்யா விளக்கே விளக்கு.

commentary: Amidst all that we have seen (described) as real (excellence), there is nothing so good as truthfulness.
en:
Of all good things we've scanned with studious care,
There's nought that can with truthfulness compare.
ta:
யாமெய்யாக் கண்டவற்றுள் இல்லை எனைத்தொன்றும்
  வாய்மையின் நல்ல பிற.

commentary: He restrains his anger who restrains it when it can injure; when it cannot injure, what does it matter whether he restrain it, or not ?
en:
Where thou hast power thy angry will to work, thy wrath restrain;
Where power is none, what matter if thou check or give it rein?
ta:
செல்லிடத்துக் காப்பான் சினங்காப்பான் அல்லிடத்துக்
  காக்கின்என் காவாக்கா

commentary: Anger is bad, even when it cannot injure; when it can injure; there is no greater evil.
en:
Where power is none to wreak thy wrath, wrath importent is ill;
Where thou hast power thy will to work, 'tis greater, evil still.
ta:
செல்லா இடத்துச் சினந்தீது செல்லிடத்தும்
  இல்அதனின் தீய பிற.

commentary: Forget anger towards every one, as fountains of evil spring from it.
en:
If any rouse thy wrath, the trespass straight forget;
For wrath an endless train of evils will beget.
ta:
மறத்தல் வெகுளியை யார்மாட்டும் தீய
  பிறத்தல் அதனான் வரும்.

commentary: Is there a greater enemy than anger, which kills both laughter and joy ?
en:
Wrath robs the face of smiles, the heart of joy,
What other foe to man works such annoy?
ta:
நகையும் உவகையும் கொல்லும் சினத்தின்
  பகையும் உளவோ பிற.

commentary: If a man would guard himself, let him guard against anger; if he do not guard it, anger will kill him.
en:
If thou would'st guard thyself, guard against wrath alway;
'Gainst wrath who guards not, him his wrath shall slay.
ta:
தன்னைத்தான் காக்கின் சினங்காக்க காவாக்கால்
  தன்னையே கொல்லுஞ் சினம்.

commentary: The fire of anger will burn up even the pleasant raft of friendship.
en:
Wrath, the fire that slayeth whose draweth near,
Will burn the helpful 'raft' of kindred dear.
ta:
சினமென்னும் சேர்ந்தாரைக் கொல்லி இனமென்னும்
  ஏமப் புணையைச் சுடும்.

commentary: Destruction will come upon him who ragards anger as a good thing, as surely as the hand of him who strikes the ground will not fail.
en:
The hand that smites the earth unfailing feels the sting;
So perish they who nurse their wrath as noble thing.
ta:
சினத்தைப் பொருளென்று கொண்டவன் கேடு
  நிலத்தறைந்தான் கைபிழையா தற்று.

commentary: Though one commit things against you as painful (to bear) as if a bundle of fire had been thrust upon you, it will be well, to refrain, if possible, from anger.
en:
Though men should work thee woe, like touch of tongues of fire.
'Tis well if thou canst save thy soul from burning ire.
ta:
இணர்எரி தோய்வன்ன இன்னா செயினும்
  புணரின் வெகுளாமை நன்று.

commentary: If a man never indulges anger in his heart, he will at once obtain whatever he has thought of.
en:
If man his soul preserve from wrathful fires,
He gains with that whate'er his soul desires.
ta:
உள்ளிய தெல்லாம் உடனெய்தும் உள்ளத்தால்
  உள்ளான் வெகுளி எனின்.

commentary: Those, who give way to excessive anger, are no better than dead men; but those, who are freed from it, are equal to those who are freed (from death).
en:
Men of surpassing wrath are like the men who've passed away;
Who wrath renounce, equals of all-renouncing sages they.
ta:
இறந்தார் இறந்தார் அனையர் சினத்தைத்
  துறந்தார் துறந்தார் துணை.

commentary: It is the determination of the spotless not to cause sorrow to others, although they could (by so causing) obtain the wealth which confers greatness.
en:
Though ill to neighbour wrought should glorious pride of wealth secure,
No ill to do is fixed decree of men in spirit pure.
ta:
சிறப்பீனும் செல்வம் பெறினும் பிறர்க்குஇன்னா
  செய்யாமை மாசற்றார் கோள்.

commentary: It is the determination of the spotless not to do evil, even in return, to those who have cherished enmity and done them evil.
en:
Though malice work its worst, planning no ill return, to endure,
And work no ill, is fixed decree of men in spirit pure.
ta:
கறுத்துஇன்னா செய்தவக் கண்ணும் மறுத்தின்னா
  செய்யாமை மாசற்றார் கோள்.

commentary: In an ascetic inflict suffering even on those who hate him, when he has not done them any evil, it will afterwards give him irretrievable sorrow.
en:
Though unprovoked thy soul malicious foes should sting,
Retaliation wrought inevitable woes will bring.
ta:
செய்யாமல் செற்றார்க்கும் இன்னாத செய்தபின்
  உய்யா விழுமந் தரும்.

commentary: The (proper) punishment to those who have done evil (to you), is to put them to shame by showing them kindness, in return and to forget both the evil and the good done on both sides.
en:
To punish wrong, with kindly benefits the doers ply;
Thus shame their souls; but pass the ill unheeded by.
ta:
இன்னாசெய் தாரை ஒறுத்தல் அவர்நாண
  நன்னயஞ் செய்து விடல்.

commentary: What benefit has he derived from his knowledge, who does not endeavour to keep off pain from another as much as from himself ?
en:
From wisdom's vaunted lore what doth the learner gain,
If as his own he guard not others' souls from pain?
ta:
அறிவினான் ஆகுவ துண்டோ பிறிதின்நோய்
  தந்நோய்போல் போற்றாக் கடை.

commentary: Let not a man consent to do those things to another which, he knows, will cause sorrow.
en:
What his own soul has felt as bitter pain,
From making others feel should man abstain.
ta:
இன்னா எனத்தான் உணர்ந்தவை துன்னாமை
  வேண்டும் பிறன்கண் செயல்.

commentary: It is the chief of all virtues not knowingly to do any person evil, even in the lowest degree, and at any time.
en:
To work no wilful woe, in any wise, through all the days,
To any living soul, is virtue's highest praise.
ta:
எனைத்தானும் எஞ்ஞான்றும் யார்க்கும் மனத்தானாம்
  மாணாசெய் யாமை தலை.

commentary: Why does a man inflict upon other creatures those sufferings, which he has found by experience are sufferings to himself ?
en:
Whose soul has felt the bitter smart of wrong, how can
He wrongs inflict on ever-living soul of man?
ta:
தன்னுயிர்ககு ஏன்னாமை தானறிவான் என்கொலோ
  மன்னுயிர்க்கு இன்னா செயல்.

commentary: If a man inflict sorrow upon others in the morning, it will come upon him unsought in the very evening.
en:
If, ere the noontide, you to others evil do,
Before the eventide will evil visit you.
ta:
பிறர்க்கின்னா முற்பகல் செய்யின் தமக்குஇன்னா
  பிற்பகல் தாமே வரும்.

commentary: Sorrow will come upon those who cause pain to others; therfore those, who desire to be free from sorrow, give no pain to others.
en:
O'er every evil-doer evil broodeth still;
He evil shuns who freedom seeks from ill.
ta:
நோயெல்லாம் நோய்செய்தார் மேலவாம் நோய்செய்யார்
  நோயின்மை வேண்டு பவர்.

commentary: Never to destroy life is the sum of all virtuous conduct. The destruction of life leads to every evil.
en:
What is the work of virtue? 'Not to kill';
For 'killing' leads to every work of ill.
ta:
அறவினை யாதெனின் கொல்லாமை கோறல்
  பிறவினை எல்லாந் தரும்.

commentary: The chief of all (the virtues) which authors have summed up, is the partaking of food that has been shared with others, and the preservation of the mainfold life of other creatures.
en:
Let those that need partake your meal; guard every-thing that lives;
This the chief and sum of lore that hoarded wisdom gives.
ta:
பகுத்துண்டு பல்லுயிர் ஓம்புதல் நூலோர்
  தொகுத்தவற்றுள் எல்லாந் தலை.

commentary: Not to destroy life is an incomparably (great) good next to it in goodness ranks freedom from falsehood.
en:
Alone, first of goods things, is 'not to slay';
The second is, no untrue word to say.
ta:
ஒன்றாக நல்லது கொல்லாமை மற்றதன்
  பின்சாரப் பொய்யாமை நன்று.

commentary: Good path is that which considers how it may avoid killing any creature.
en:
You ask, What is the good and perfect way?
'Tis path of him who studies nought to slay.
ta:
நல்லாறு எனப்படுவது யாதெனின் யாதொன்றும்
  கொல்லாமை சூழும் நெறி.

commentary: Of all those who, fearing the permanence of earthly births, have abandoned desire, he is the chief who, fearing (the guilt of) murder, considers how he may avoid the destruction of life.
en:
Of those who 'being' dread, and all renounce, the chief are they,
Who dreading crime of slaughter, study nought to slay.
ta:
நிலைஅஞ்சி நீத்தாருள் எல்லாம் கொலைஅஞ்சிக்
  கொல்லாமை சூழ்வான் தலை.

commentary: Yama, the destroyer of life, will not attack the life of him, who acts under the determination of never destroying life.
en:
Ev'n death that life devours, their happy days shall spare,
Who law, 'Thou shall not kill', uphold with reverent care.
ta:
கொல்லாமை மேற்கொண் டொழுகுவான் வாழ்நாள்மேல்
  செல்லாது உயிருண்ணுங் கூற்று.

commentary: Let no one do that which would destroy the life of another, although he should by so doing, lose his own life.
en:
Though thine own life for that spared life the price must pay,
Take not from aught that lives gift of sweet life away.
ta:
தன்னுயிர் நீப்பினும் செய்யற்க தான்பிறிது
  இன்னுயிர் நீக்கும் வினை.

commentary: The advantage which might flow from destroying life in sacrifice, is dishonourable to the wise (who renounced the world), even although it should be said to be productive of great good.
en:
Though great the gain of good should seem, the wise
Will any gain by staughter won despise.
ta:
நன்றாகும் ஆக்கம் பெரிதெனினும் சான்றோர்க்குக்
  கொன்றாகும் ஆக்கங் கடை.

commentary: Men who destroy life are base men, in the estimation of those who know the nature of meanness.
en:
Whose trade is 'killing', always vile they show,
To minds of them who what is vileness know.
ta:
கொலைவினைய ராகிய மாக்கள் புலைவினையர்
  புன்மை தெரிவா ரகத்து.

commentary: (The wise) will say that men of diseased bodies, who live in degradation and in poverty, are those who separated the life from the body of animals (in a former birth).
en:
Who lead a loathed life in bodies sorely pained,
Are men, the wise declare, by guilt of slaughter stained.
ta:
உயிர் உடம்பின் நீக்கியார் என்ப
  செயிர் உடம்பின் செல்லாத்தீ வாழ்க்கை
  யவர்.

commentary: That ignorance which considers those things to be stable which are not so, is dishonourable (to the wise).
en:
Lowest and meanest lore, that bids men trust secure,
In things that pass away, as things that shall endure!
ta:
நில்லாத வற்றை நிலையின என்றுணரும்
  புல்லறி வாண்மை கடை.

commentary: The acquisition of wealth is like the gathering together of an assembly for a theatre; its expenditure is like the breaking up of that assembly.
en:
As crowds round dancers fill the hall, is wealth's increase;
Its loss, as throngs dispersing, when the dances cease.
ta:
கூத்தாட்டு அவைக்குழாத் தற்றே பெருஞ்செல்வம்
  போக்கும் அதுவிளிந் தற்று.

commentary: Wealth is perishable; let those who obtain it immediately practise those (virtues) which are imperishable.
en:
Unenduring is all wealth; if you wealth enjoy,
Enduring works in working wealth straightway employ.
ta:
அற்கா இயல்பிற்றுச் செல்வம் அதுபெற்றால்
  அற்குப ஆங்கே செயல்.

commentary: Time, which shows itself (to the ignorant) as if it were something (real) is in the estimation of the wise (only) a saw which cuts down life.
en:
As 'day' it vaunts itself; well understood, 'tis knife',
That daily cuts away a portion from thy life.
ta:
நாளென ஒன்றுபோற் காட்டி உயிர்ஈரும்
  வாளது உணர்வார்ப் பெறின்.

commentary: Let virtuous deeds be done quickly, before the biccup comes making the tongue silent.
en:
Before the tongue lie powerless, 'mid the gasp of gurgling breath,
Arouse thyself, and do good deeds beyond the power of death.
ta:
நாச்செற்று விக்குள்மேல் வாராமுன் நல்வினை
  மேற்சென்று செய்யப் படும்

commentary: This world possesses the greatness that one who yesterday was is not today.
en:
Existing yesterday, today to nothing hurled!-
Such greatness owns this transitory world.
ta:
நெருநல் உளனொருவன் இன்றில்லை என்னும்
  பெருமை உடைத்துஇவ் வுலகு.

commentary: Innumerable are the thoughts which occupy the mind of (the unwise), who know not that they shall live another moment.
en:
Who know not if their happy lives shall last the day,
In fancies infinite beguile the hours away!
ta:
ஒருபொழுதும் வாழ்வது அறியார் கருதுப
  கோடியும் அல்ல பல.

commentary: The love of the soul to the body is like (the love of) a bird to its egg which it flies away from and leaves empty.
en:
Birds fly away, and leave the nest deserted bare;
Such is the short-lived friendship soul and body share.
ta:
குடம்பை தனித்துஒழியப் புள்பறந் தற்றே
  உடம்பொடு உயிரிடை நட்பு.

commentary: Death is like sleep; birth is like awaking from it.
en:
Death is sinking into slumbers deep;
Birth again is waking out of sleep.
ta:
உறங்கு வதுபோலுஞ் சாக்காடு உறங்கி
  விழிப்பது போலும் பிறப்பு.

commentary: It seems as if the soul, which takes a temporary shelter in a body, had not attained a home.
en:
The soul in fragile shed as lodger courts repose:-
Is it because no home's conclusive rest it knows?
ta:
புக்கில் அமைந்தின்று கொல்லோ உடம்பினுள்
  துச்சில் இருந்த உயிர்க்கு.

commentary: Whatever thing, a man has renounced, by that thing; he cannot suffer pain.
en:
From whatever, aye, whatever, man gets free,
From what, aye, from that, no more of pain hath he!
ta:
யாதனின் யாதனின் நீங்கியான் நோதல்
  அதனின் அதனின் இலன்.

commentary: After a man has renounced (all things), there will still be many things in this world (which he may enjoy); if he should desire them, let him, while it is time abandon. (the world).
en:
'Renunciation' made- ev'n here true pleasures men acquire;
'Renounce' while time is yet, if to those pleasures you aspire.
ta:
வேண்டின்உண் டாகத் துறக்க துறந்தபின்
  ஈண்டுஇயற் பால பல.

commentary: Let the five senses be destroyed; and at the same time, let everything be abandoned that (the ascetic) has (formerly) desired.
en:
'Perceptions of the five' must all expire;-
Relinquished in its order each desire
ta:
அடல்வேண்டும் ஐந்தன் புலத்தை விடல்வேண்டும்
  வேண்டிய வெல்லாம் ஒருங்கு.

commentary: To be altogether destitute is the proper condition of those who perform austerities; if they possess anything, it will change (their resolution) and bring them back to their confused state.
en:
'Privation absolute' is penance true;
'Possession' brings bewilderment anew.
ta:
இயல்பாகும் நோன்பிற்கொன்று இன்மை உடைமை
  மயலாகும் மற்றும் பெயர்த்து.

commentary: What means the addition of other things those who are attempting to cut off (future) births, when even their body is too much (for them).
en:
To those who sev'rance seek from being's varied strife,
Flesh is burthen sore; what then other bonds of life?
ta:
மற்றும் தொடர்ப்பாடு எவன்கொல் பிறப்பறுக்கல்
  உற்றார்க்கு உடம்பும் மிகை.

commentary: He who destroys the pride which says "I", "mine" will enter a world which is difficult even to the Gods to attain.
en:
Who kills conceit that utters 'I' and 'mine',
Shall enter realms above the powers divine.
ta:
யான் எனது என்னும் செருக்கு
  அறுப்பான் வானோர்க்கு உயர்ந்த உலகம்
  புகும்.

commentary: Sorrows will never let go their hold of those who give not up their hold of desire.
en:
Who cling to things that cling and eager clasp,
Griefs cling to them with unrelaxing grasp.
ta:
பற்றி விடாஅ இடும்பைகள் பற்றினைப்
  பற்றி விடாஅ தவர்க்கு.

commentary: Those who have entirely renounced (all things and all desire) have obtained (absorption into God); all others wander in confusion, entangled in the net of (many) births.
en:
Who thoroughly 'renounce' on highest height are set;
The rest bewildered, lie entangled in the net.
ta:
தலைப்பட்டார் தீரத் துறந்தார் மயங்கி
  வலைப்பட்டார் மற்றை யவர்.

commentary: At the moment in which desire has been abandoned, (other) births will be cut off; when that has not been done, instability will be seen.
en:
When that which clings falls off, severed is being's tie;
All else will then be seen as instability.
ta:
பற்றற்ற கண்ணே பிறப்பறுக்கும் மற்று
  நிலையாமை காணப் படும்.

commentary: Desire the desire of Him who is without desire; in order to renounce desire, desire that desire.
en:
Cling thou to that which He, to Whom nought clings, hath bid thee cling,
Cling to that bond, to get thee free from every clinging thing.
ta:
பற்றுக பற்றற்றான் பற்றினை அப்பற்றைப்
  பற்றுக பற்று விடற்கு.

commentary: Inglorious births are produced by the confusion (of mind) which considers those things to be real which are not real.
en:
Of things devoid of truth as real things men deem;-
Cause of degraded birth the fond delusive dream!
ta:
பொருளல்ல வற்றைப் பொருளென்று உணரும்
  மருளானாம் மாணாப் பிறப்பு.

commentary: A clear, undimmed vision of things will deliver its possessors from the darkness of future births, and confer the felicity (of heaven).
en:
Darkness departs, and rapture springs to men who see,
The mystic vision pure, from all delusion free.
ta:
இருள்நீங்கி இன்பம் பயக்கும் மருள்நீங்கி
  மாசறு காட்சி யவர்க்கு.

commentary: Heaven is nearer than earth to those men of purified minds who are freed from from doubt.
en:
When doubts disperse, and mists of error roll
Away, nearer is heav'n than earth to sage's soul.
ta:
ஐயத்தின் நீங்கித் தெளிந்தார்க்கு வையத்தின்
  வானம் நணிய துடைத்து.

commentary: Even those who have all the knowledge which can be attained by the five senses, will derive no benefit from it, if they are without a knowledge of the true nature of things.
en:
Five-fold perception gained, what benefits accrue
To them whose spirits lack perception of the true?
ta:
ஐயுணர்வு எய்தியக் கண்ணும் பயமின்றே
  மெய்யுணர்வு இல்லா தவர்க்கு.

commentary: (True) knowledge is the perception concerning every thing of whatever kind, that that thing is the true thing.
en:
Whatever thing, of whatsoever kind it be,
'Tis wisdom's part in each the very thing to see.
ta:
எப்பொருள் எத்தன்மைத் தாயினும் அப்பொருள்
  மெய்ப்பொருள் காண்பது அறிவு.

commentary: They, who in this birth have learned to know the True Being, enter the road which returns not into this world.
en:
Who learn, and here the knowledge of the true obtain,
Shall find the path that hither cometh not again.
ta:
கற்றீண்டு மெய்ப்பொருள் கண்டார் தலைப்படுவர்
  மற்றீண்டு வாரா நெறி.

commentary: Let it not be thought that there is another birth for him whose mind having thoroughly considered (all it has been taught) has known the True Being.
en:
The mind that knows with certitude what is, and ponders well,
Its thoughts on birth again to other life need not to dwell.
ta:
ஓர்த்துள்ளம் உள்ளது உணரின் ஒருதலையாப்
  பேர்த்துள்ள வேண்டா பிறப்பு.

commentary: True knowledge consists in the removal of ignorance; which is (the cause of) births, and the perception of the True Being who is (the bestower of) heaven.
en:
When folly, cause of births, departs; and soul can view
The truth of things, man's dignity- 'tis wisdom true.
ta:
பிறப்பென்னும் பேதைமை நீங்கச் சிறப்பென்னும்
  செம்பொருள் காண்பது அறிவு.

commentary: He who so lives as to know Him who is the support of all things and abandon all desire, will be freed from the evils which would otherwise cleave to him and destroy (his efforts after absorption).
en:
The true 'support' who knows- rejects 'supports' he sought before-
Sorrow that clings all destroys, shall cling to him no more.
ta:
சார்புணர்ந்து சார்பு கெடஒழுகின் மற்றழித்துச்
  சார்தரா சார்தரு நோய்.

commentary: If the very names of these three things, desire, anger, and confusion of mind, be destroyed, then will also perish the evils (which flow from them).
en:
When lust and wrath and error's triple tyranny is o'er,
Their very names for aye extinct, then pain shall be no more.
ta:
காமம் வெகுளி மயக்கம் இவ்முன்றன்
  நாமம் கெடக்கெடும் நோய்.

commentary: (The wise) say that the seed, which produces unceasing births, at all times, to all creatures, is desire.
en:
The wise declare, through all the days, to every living thing.
That ceaseless round of birth from seed of strong desire doth spring.
ta:
அவாஎன்ப எல்லா உயிர்க்கும் எஞ்ஞான்றும்
  தவாஅப் பிறப்பீனும் வித்து.

commentary: If anything be desired, freedom from births should be desired; that (freedom from births) will be attained by desiring to be without desire.
en:
If desire you feel, freedom from changing birth require!
'I' will come, if you desire to 'scape, set free from all desire.
ta:
வேண்டுங்கால் வேண்டும் பிறவாமை மற்றது
  வேண்டாமை வேண்ட வரும்.

commentary: There is in this world no excellence equal to freedom from desire; and even in that world, there is nothing like it.
en:
No glorious wealth is here like freedom from desire;
To bliss like this not even there can soul aspire.
ta:
வேண்டாமை அன்ன விழுச்செல்வம் ஈண்டில்லை
  ஆண்டும் அஃதொப்பது இல்.

commentary: Purity (of mind) consists in freedom from desire; and that (freedom from desire) is the fruit of the love of truth.
en:
Desire's decease as purity men know;
That, too, from yearning search for truth will grow.
ta:
தூஉய்மை என்பது அவாவின்மை மற்றது
  வாஅய்மை வேண்ட வரும்.

commentary: They are said to be free (from future birth) who are freed from desire; all others (who, whatever else they may be free from, are not freed from desire) are not thus free.
en:
Men freed from bonds of strong desire are free;
None other share such perfect liberty.
ta:
அற்றவர் என்பார் அவாஅற்றார் மற்றையார்
  அற்றாக அற்றது இலர்.

commentary: It is the chief duty of (an ascetic) to watch against desire with (jealous) fear; for it has power to deceive (and destroy) him.
en:
Desire each soul beguiles;
True virtue dreads its wiles.
ta:
அஞ்சுவ தோரும் அறனே ஒருவனை
  வஞ்சிப்ப தோரும் அவா.

commentary: If a man thoroughly cut off all desire, the deeds, which confer immortality, will come to him, in the path in which he seeks them.
en:
Who thoroughly rids his life of passion-prompted deed,
Deeds of unfailing worth shall do, which, as he plans, succeed.
ta:
அவாவினை ஆற்ற அறுப்பின் தவாவினை
  தான்வேண்டு மாற்றான் வரும்.

commentary: There is no sorrow to those who are without desire; but where that is, (sorrow) will incessantly come, more and more.
en:
Affliction is not known where no desires abide;
Where these are, endless rises sorrow's tide.
ta:
அவாஇல்லார்க் கில்லாகுந் துன்பம் அஃதுண்டேல்
  தவாஅது மேன்மேல் வரும்.

commentary: Even while in this body, joy will never depart (from the mind, in which) desire, that sorrow of sorrows, has been destroyed.
en:
When dies away desire, that woe of woes
Ev'n here the soul unceasing rapture knows.
ta:
இன்பம் இடையறா தீண்டும் அவாவென்னும்
  துன்பத்துள் துன்பங் கெடின்.

commentary: The removal of desire, whose nature it is never to be satisfied, will immediately confer a nature that can never be changed.
en:
Drive from thy soul desire insatiate;
Straight'way is gained the moveless blissful state.
ta:
ஆரா இயற்கை அவாநீப்பின் அந்நிலையே
  பேரா இயற்கை தரும்.

commentary: Perseverance comes from a prosperous fate, and idleness from an adverse fate.
en:
Wealth-giving fate power of unflinching effort brings;
From fate that takes away idle remissness springs.
ta:
ஆகூழால் தோன்றும் அசைவின்மை கைப்பொருள்
  போகூழால் தோன்றும் மடி.

commentary: An adverse fate produces folly, and a prosperous fate produces enlarged knowledge.
en:
The fate that loss ordains makes wise men's wisdom foolishness;
The fate that gain bestows with ampler powers will wisdom bless.
ta:
பேதைப் படுக்கும் இழவூழ் அறிவகற்றும்
  ஆகலூழ் உற்றக் கடை.

commentary: Although (a man) may study the most polished treatises, the knowledge which fate has decreed to him will still prevail.
en:
In subtle learning manifold though versed man be,
'The wisdom, truly his, will gain supremacy.
ta:
நுண்ணிய நூல்பல கற்பினும் மற்றுந்தன்
  உண்மை யறிவே மிகும்.

commentary: There are (through fate) two different natures in the world, hence the difference (observable in men) in (their acquisition of) wealth, and in their attainment of knowledge.
en:
Two fold the fashion of the world: some live in fortune's light;
While other some have souls in wisdom's radiance bright.
ta:
இருவேறு உலகத்து இயற்கை திருவேறு
  தெள்ளிய ராதலும் வேறு.

commentary: In the acquisition of property, every thing favourable becomes unfavourable, and (on the other hand) everything unfavourable becomes favourable, (through the power of fate).
en:
All things that good appear will oft have ill success;
All evil things prove good for gain of happiness.
ta:
நல்லவை எல்லாஅந் தீயவாம் தீயவும்
  நல்லவாம் செல்வம் செயற்கு.

commentary: Whatever is not conferred by fate cannot be preserved although it be guarded with most painful care; and that, which fate has made his, cannot be lost, although one should even take it and throw it away.
en:
Things not your own will yield no good, howe'er you guard with pain;
Your own, howe'er you scatter them abroad, will yours remain.
ta:
பரியினும் ஆகாவாம் பாலல்ல உய்த்துச்
  சொரியினும் போகா தம.

commentary: Even those who gather together millions will only enjoy them, as it has been determined by the disposer (of all things).
en:
Save as the 'sharer' shares to each in due degree,
To those who millions store enjoyment scarce can be.
ta:
வகுத்தான் வகுத்த வகையல்லால் கோடி
  தொகுத்தார்க்கு துய்த்தல் அரிது.

commentary: The destitute will renounce desire (and become ascetics), if (fate) do not make them suffer the hindrances to which they are liable, and they pass away.
en:
The destitute with ascetics merit share,
If fate to visit with predestined ills would spare.
ta:
துறப்பார்மன் துப்புர வில்லார் உறற்பால
  ஊட்டா கழியு மெனின்.

commentary: How is it that those, who are pleased with good fortune, trouble themselves when evil comes, (since both are equally the decree of fate) ?
en:
When good things come, men view them all as gain;
When evils come, why then should they complain?
ta:
நன்றாங்கால் நல்லவாக் காண்பவர் அன்றாங்கால்
  அல்லற் படுவ தெவன்.

commentary: What is stronger than fate ? If we think of an expedient (to avert it), it will itself be with us before (the thought).
en:
What powers so great as those of Destiny? Man's skill
Some other thing contrives; but fate's beforehand still.
ta:
ஊழிற் பெருவலி யாவுள மற்றொன்று
  சூழினுந் தான்முந் துறும்.

commentary: He who possesses these six things, an army, a people, wealth, ministers, friends and a fortress, is a lion among kings.
en:
An army, people, wealth, a minister, friends, fort: six things-
Who owns them all, a lion lives amid the kings.
ta:
படைகுடி கூழ்அமைச்சு நட்பரண் ஆறும்
  உடையான் அரசருள் ஏறு.

commentary: Never to fail in these four things, fearlessness, liberality, wisdom, and energy, is the kingly character.
en:
Courage, a liberal hand, wisdom, and energy: these four
Are qualities a king adorn for evermore.
ta:
அஞ்சாமை ஈகை அறிவூக்கம் இந்நான்கும்
  எஞ்சாமை வேந்தர்க் கியல்பு.

commentary: These three things, viz., vigilance, learning, and bravery, should never be wanting in the ruler of a country.
en:
A sleepless promptitude, knowledge, decision strong:
These three for aye to rulers of the land belong.
ta:
தூங்காமை கல்வி துணிவுடைமை இம்மூன்றும்
  நீங்கா நிலனான் பவர்க்கு.

commentary: He is a king who, with manly modesty, swerves not from virtue, and refrains from vice.
en:
Kingship, in virtue failing not, all vice restrains,
In courage failing not, it honour's grace maintains.
ta:
அறனிழுக்கா தல்லவை நீக்கி மறனிழுக்கா
  மானம் உடைய தரசு.

commentary: He is a king who is able to acquire (wealth), to lay it up, to guard, and to distribute it.
en:
A king is he who treasure gains, stores up, defends,
And duly for his kingdom's weal expends.
ta:
இயற்றலும் ஈட்டலுங் காத்தலும் காத்த
  வகுத்தலும் வல்ல தரசு.

commentary: The whole world will exalt the country of the king who is easy of access, and who is free from harsh language.
en:
Where king is easy of access, where no harsh word repels,
That land's high praises every subject swells.
ta:
காட்சிக் கெளியன் கடுஞ்சொல்லன் அல்லனேல்
  மீக்கூறும் மன்னன் நிலம்

commentary: The world will praise and submit itself to the mind of the king who is able to give with affability, and to protect all who come to him.
en:
With pleasant speech, who gives and guards with powerful liberal hand,
He sees the world obedient all to his command.
ta:
இன்சொலால் ஈத்தளிக்க வல்லார்க்குத் தன்சொலால்
  தான்கண் டனைத்திவ் வுலகு.

commentary: That king, will be esteemed a God among men, who performs his own duties, and protects (his subjects).
en:
Who guards the realm and justice strict maintains,
That king as god o'er subject people reigns.
ta:
முறைசெய்து காப்பாற்றும் மன்னவன் மக்கட்கு
  இறையென்று வைக்கப் படும்.

commentary: The whole world will dwell under the umbrella of the king, who can bear words that embitter the ear.
en:
The king of worth, who can words bitter to his ear endure,
Beneath the shadow of his power the world abides secure.
ta:
செவிகைப்பச் சொற்பொறுக்கும் பண்புடை வேந்தன்
  கவிகைக்கீழ்த் தங்கும் உலகு.

commentary: He is the light of kings who has there four things, beneficence, benevolence, rectitude, and care for his people.
en:
Gifts, grace, right sceptre, care of people's weal;
These four a light of dreaded kings reveal.
ta:
கொடையளி செங்கோல் குடியோம்பல் நான்கும்
  உடையானாம் வேந்தர்க் கொளி.

commentary: Let a man learn thoroughly whatever he may learn, and let his conduct be worthy of his learning.
en:
So learn that you may full and faultless learning gain,
Then in obedience meet to lessons learnt remain.
ta:
கற்க கசடறக் கற்பவை கற்றபின்
  நிற்க அதற்குத் தக.

commentary: Letters and numbers are the two eyes of man.
en:
The twain that lore of numbers and of letters give
Are eyes, the wise declare, to all on earth that live.
ta:
எண்ணென்ப ஏனை எழுத்தென்ப இவ்விரண்டும்
  கண்ணென்ப வாழும் உயிர்க்கு.

commentary: The learned are said to have eyes, but the unlearned have (merely) two sores in their face.
en:
Men who learning gain have eyes, men say;
Blockheads' faces pairs of sores display.
ta:
கண்ணுடையர் என்பவர் கற்றோர் முகத்திரண்டு
  புண்ணுடையர் கல்லா தவர்.

commentary: It is the part of the learned to give joy to those whom they meet, and on leaving, to make them think (Oh! when shall we meet them again.)
en:
You meet with joy, with pleasant thought you part;
Such is the learned scholar's wonderous art!
ta:
உவப்பத் தலைக்கூடி உள்ளப் பிரிதல்
  அனைத்தே புலவர் தொழில்.

commentary: The unlearned are inferior to the learned, before whom they stand begging, as the destitute before the wealthy.
en:
With soul submiss they stand, as paupers front a rich man's face;
Yet learned men are first; th'unlearned stand in lowest place.
ta:
உடையார்முன் இல்லார்போல் ஏக்கற்றுங் கற்றார்
  கடையரே கல்லா தவர்.

commentary: Water will flow from a well in the sand in proportion to the depth to which it is dug, and knowledge will flow from a man in proportion to his learning.
en:
In sandy soil, when deep you delve, you reach the springs below;
The more you learn, the freer streams of wisdom flow.
ta:
தொட்டனைத் தூறும் மணற்கேணி மாந்தர்க்குக்
  கற்றனைத் தூறும் அறிவு.

commentary: How is it that any one can remain without learning, even to his death, when (to the learned man) every country is his own (country), and every town his own (town) ?
en:
The learned make each land their own, in every city find a home;
Who, till they die; learn nought, along what weary ways they roam!
ta:
யாதானும் நாடாமால் ஊராமால் என்னொருவன்
  சாந்துணையுங் கல்லாத வாறு.

commentary: The learning, which a man has acquired in one birth, will yield him pleasure during seven births.
en:
The man who store of learning gains,
In one, through seven worlds, bliss attains.
ta:
ஒருமைக்கண் தான்கற்ற கல்வி ஒருவற்கு
  எழுமையும் ஏமாப் புடைத்து.

commentary: The learned will long (for more learning), when they see that while it gives pleasure to themselves, the world also derives pleasure from it.
en:
Their joy is joy of all the world, they see; thus more
The learners learn to love their cherished lore.
ta:
தாமின் புறுவது உலகின் புறக்கண்டு
  காமுறுவர் கற்றறிந் தார்.

commentary: Learning is the true imperishable riches; all other things are not riches.
en:
Learning is excellence of wealth that none destroy;
To man nought else affords reality of joy.
ta:
கேடில் விழுச்செல்வம் கல்வி யொருவற்கு
  மாடல்ல மற்றை யவை.

commentary: To speak in an assembly (of the learned) without fullness of knowledge, is like playing at chess (on a board) without squares.
en:
Like those at draughts would play without the chequered square,
Men void of ample lore would counsels of the learned share.
ta:
அரங்கின்றி வட்டாடி யற்றே நிரம்பிய
  நூலின்றிக் கோட்டி கொளல்.

commentary: The desire of the unlearned to speak (in an assembly), is like a woman without breasts desiring (the enjoyment of ) woman-hood.
en:
Like those who doat on hoyden's undeveloped charms are they,
Of learning void, who eagerly their power of words display.
ta:
கல்லாதான் சொற்கா முறுதல் முலையிரண்டும்
  இல்லாதாள் பெண்காமுற் றற்று.

commentary: The unlearned also are very excellent men, if they know how to keep silence before the learned.
en:
The blockheads, too, may men of worth appear,
If they can keep from speaking where the learned hear!
ta:
கல்லா தவரும் நனிநல்லர் கற்றார்முன்
  சொல்லா திருக்கப் பெறின்.

commentary: Although the natural knowledge of an unlearned man may be very good, the wise will not accept for true knowledge.
en:
From blockheads' lips, when words of wisdom glibly flow,
The wise receive them not, though good they seem to show.
ta:
கல்லாதான் ஒட்பம் கழியநன் றாயினும்
  கொள்ளார் அறிவுடை யார்.

commentary: The self-conceit of an unlearned man will fade away, as soon as he speaks in an assembly (of the learned).
en:
As worthless shows the worth of man unlearned,
When council meets, by words he speaks discerned.
ta:
கல்லா ஒருவன் தகைமை தலைப்பெய்து
  சொல்லாடச் சோர்வு படும்.

commentary: The unlearned are like worthless barren land: all that can be said of them is, that they exist.
en:
'They are': so much is true of men untaught;
But, like a barren field, they yield us nought!
ta:
உளரென்னும் மாத்திரையர் அல்லால் பயவாக்
  களரனையர் கல்லா தவர்.

commentary: The beauty and goodness of one who is destitute of knowledge by the study of great and exquisite works, is like (the beauty and goodness) of a painted earthen doll.
en:
Who lack the power of subtle, large, and penetrating sense,
Like puppet, decked with ornaments of clay, their beauty's vain pretence.
ta:
நுண்மாண் நுழைபுலம் இல்லான் எழில்நலம்
  மண்மாண் புனைபாவை யற்று.

commentary: Wealth, gained by the unlearned, will give more sorrow than the poverty which may come upon the learned.
en:
To men unlearned, from fortune's favour greater-evil springs
Than poverty to men of goodly wisdom brings.
ta:
நல்லார்கண் பட்ட வறுமையின் இன்னாதே
  கல்லார்கண் பட்ட திரு.

commentary: The unlearned, though born in a high caste, are not equal in dignity to the learned; though they may have been born in a low caste.
en:
Lower are men unlearned, though noble be their race,
Than low-born men adorned with learning's grace.
ta:
மேற்பிறந்தா ராயினும் கல்லாதார் கீழ்ப்பிறந்தும்
  கற்றார் அனைத்திலர் பாடு.

commentary: As beasts by the side of men, so are other men by the side of those who are learned in celebrated works.
en:
Learning's irradiating grace who gain,
Others excel, as men the bestial train.
ta:
விலங்கொடு மக்கள் அனையர் இலங்குநூல்
  கற்றாரோடு ஏனை யவர்.

commentary: Wealth (gained) by the ear is wealth of wealth; that wealth is the chief of all wealth.
en:
Wealth of wealth is wealth acquired be ear attent;
Wealth mid all wealth supremely excellent.
ta:
செல்வத்துட் செல்வஞ் செவிச்செல்வம் அச்செல்வம்
  செல்வத்து ளெல்லாந் தலை.

commentary: When there is no food for the ear, give a little also to the stomach.
en:
When 'tis no longer time the listening ear to feed
With trifling dole of food supply the body's need.
ta:
செவுக்குண வில்லாத போழ்து சிறிது
  வயிற்றுக்கும் ஈயப் படும்.

commentary: Those who in this world enjoy instruction which is the food of the ear, are equal to the Gods, who enjoy the food of the sacrifices.
en:
Who feed their ear with learned teachings rare,
Are like the happy gods oblations rich who share.
ta:
செவியுணவிற் கேள்வி யுடையார் அவியுணவின்
  ஆன்றாரோ டொப்பர் நிலத்து.

commentary: Although a man be without learning, let him listen (to the teaching of the learned); that will be to him a staff in adversity.
en:
Though learning none hath he, yet let him hear alway:
In weakness this shall prove a staff and stay.
ta:
கற்றில னாயினுங் கேட்க அஃதொருவற்கு
  ஒற்கத்தின் ஊற்றாந் துணை.

commentary: The words of the good are like a staff in a slippery place.
en:
Like staff in hand of him in slippery ground who strays
Are words from mouth of those who walk in righteous ways.
ta:
இழுக்கல் உடையுழி ஊற்றுக்கோல் அற்றே
  ஒழுக்க முடையார்வாய்ச் சொல்.

commentary: Let a man listen, never so little, to good (instruction), even that will bring him great dignity.
en:
Let each man good things learn, for e'en as he
Shall learn, he gains increase of perfect dignity.
ta:
எனைத்தானும் நல்லவை கேட்க அனைத்தானும்
  ஆன்ற பெருமை தரும்.

commentary: Not even when they have imperfectly understood (a matter), will those men speak foolishly, who have profoundly studied and diligently listened (to instruction).
en:
Not e'en through inadvertence speak they foolish word,
With clear discerning mind who've learning's ample lessons heard.
ta:
பிழைத்துணர்ந்தும் பேதைமை சொல்லா ரிழைத்துணர்ந்
  தீண்டிய கேள்வி யவர்.

commentary: The ear which has not been bored by instruction, although it hears, is deaf.
en:
Where teaching hath not oped the learner's ear,
The man may listen, but he scarce can hear.
ta:
கேட்பினுங் கேளாத் தகையவே கேள்வியால்
  தோட்கப் படாத செவி.

commentary: It is a rare thing to find modesty, a reverend mouth- with those who have not received choice instruction.
en:
'Tis hard for mouth to utter gentle, modest word,
When ears discourse of lore refined have never heard.
ta:
நுணங்கிய கேள்விய ரல்லார் வணங்கிய
  வாயின ராதல் அரிது.

commentary: What does it matter whether those men live or die, who can judge of tastes by the mouth, and not by the ear ?
en:
His mouth can taste, but ear no taste of joy can give!
What matter if he die, or prosperous live?
ta:
செவியிற் சுவையுணரா வாயுணர்வின் மாக்கள்
  அவியினும் வாழினும் என்.

commentary: Wisdom is a weapon to ward off destruction; it is an inner fortress which enemies cannot destroy.
en:
True wisdom wards off woes, A circling fortress high;
Its inner strength man's eager foes Unshaken will defy.
ta:
அறிவற்றங் காக்குங் கருவி செறுவார்க்கும்
  உள்ளழிக்க லாகா அரண்.

commentary: Not to permit the mind to go where it lists, to keep it from evil, and to employ it in good, this is wisdom.
en:
Wisdom restrains, nor suffers mind to wander where it would;
From every evil calls it back, and guides in way of good.
ta:
சென்ற இடத்தால் செலவிடா தீதொரீஇ
  நன்றின்பால் உய்ப்ப தறிவு.

commentary: To discern the truth in every thing, by whomsoever spoken, is wisdom.
en:
Though things diverse from divers sages' lips we learn,
'Tis wisdom's part in each the true thing to discern.
ta:
எப்பொருள் யார்யார்வாய்க் கேட்பினும் அப்பொருள்
  மெய்ப்பொருள் காண்ப தறிவு.

commentary: To speak so as that the meaning may easily enter the mind of the hearer, and to discern the subtlest thought which may lie hidden in the words of others, this is wisdom.
en:
Wisdom hath use of lucid speech, words that acceptance win,
And subtle sense of other men's discourse takes in.
ta:
எண்பொருள வாகச் செலச்சொல்லித் தான்பிறர்வாய்
  நுண்பொருள் காண்ப தறிவு.

commentary: To secure the friendship of the great is true wisdom; it is (also) wisdom to keep (that friendship unchanged, and) not opening and closing (like the lotus flower).
en:
Wisdom embraces frank the world, to no caprice exposed;
Unlike the lotus flower, now opened wide, now petals strictly closed.
ta:
உலகம் தழீஇய தொட்பம் மலர்தலும்
  கூம்பலும் இல்ல தறிவு.

commentary: To live as the world lives, is wisdom.
en:
As dwells the world, so with the world to dwell
In harmony- this is to wisely live and well.
ta:
எவ்வ துறைவது உலகம் உலகத்தோடு
  அவ்வ துறைவ தறிவு.

commentary: The wise are those who know beforehand what will happen; those who do not know this are the unwise.
en:
The wise discern, the foolish fail to see,
And minds prepare for things about to be.
ta:
அறிவுடையார் ஆவ தறிவார் அறிவிலார்
  அஃதறி கல்லா தவர்.

commentary: Not to fear what ought to be feared, is folly; it is the work of the wise to fear what should be feared.
en:
Folly meets fearful ills with fearless heart;
To fear where cause of fear exists is wisdom's part.
ta:
அஞ்சுவ தஞ்சாமை பேதைமை அஞ்சுவது
  அஞ்சல் அறிவார் தொழில்.

commentary: No terrifying calamity will happen to the wise, who (foresee) and guard against coming evils.
en:
The wise with watchful soul who coming ills foresee;
From coming evil's dreaded shock are free.
ta:
எதிரதாக் காக்கும் அறிவினார்க் கில்லை
  அதிர வருவதோர் நோய்.

commentary: Those who possess wisdom, possess every thing; those who have not wisdom, whatever they may possess, have nothing.
en:
The wise is rich, with ev'ry blessing blest;
The fool is poor, of everything possessed.
ta:
அறிவுடையார் எல்லா முடையார் அறிவிலார்
  என்னுடைய ரேனும் இலர்.

commentary: Truly great is the excellence of those (kings) who are free from pride, anger, and lust.
en:
Who arrogance, and wrath, and littleness of low desire restrain,
To sure increase of lofty dignity attain.
ta:
செருக்குஞ் சினமும் சிறுமையும் இல்லார்
  பெருக்கம் பெருமித நீர்த்து.

commentary: Avarice, undignified pride, and low pleasures are faults in a king.
en:
A niggard hand, o'erweening self-regard, and mirth
Unseemly, bring disgrace to men of kingly brith.
ta:
இவறலும் மாண்பிறந்த மானமும் மாணா
  உவகையும் ஏதம் இறைக்கு.

commentary: Those who fear guilt, if they commit a fault small as a millet seed, will consider it to be as large as a palmyra tree.
en:
Though small as millet-seed the fault men deem;
As palm tree vast to those who fear disgrace 'twill seem.
ta:
தினைத்துணையாங் குற்றம் வரினும் பனைத்துணையாக்
  கொள்வர் பழிநாணு வார்.

commentary: Guard against faults as a matter (of great consequence; for) faults are a deadly enemy.
en:
Freedom from faults is wealth; watch heedfully
'Gainst these, for fault is fatal enmity.
ta:
குற்றமே காக்க பொருளாகக் குற்றமே
  அற்றந் த்ரூஉம் பகை.

commentary: The prosperity of him who does not timely guard against faults, will perish like straw before fire.
en:
His joy who guards not 'gainst the coming evil day,
Like straw before the fire shall swift consume away.
ta:
வருமுன்னர்க் காவாதான் வாழ்க்கை எரிமுன்னர்
  வைத்தூறு போலக் கெடும்.

commentary: What fault will remain in the king who has put away his own evils, and looks after the evils of others.
en:
Faultless the king who first his own faults cures, and then
Permits himself to scan faults of other men.
ta:
தன்குற்றம் நீக்கிப் பிறர்குற்றங் காண்கிற்பின்
  என்குற்ற மாகும் இறைக்கு.

commentary: The wealth of the avaricious man, who does not expend it for the purposes for which he ought to expend it will waste away and not continue.
en:
Who leaves undone what should be done, with niggard mind,
His wealth shall perish, leaving not a wrack behind.
ta:
செயற்பால செய்யா திவறியான் செல்வம்
  உயற்பால தன்றிக் கெடும்.

commentary: Griping avarice is not to be reckoned as one among other faults; (it stands alone - greater than all).
en:
The greed of soul that avarice men call,
When faults are summed, is worst of all.
ta:
பற்றுள்ளம் என்னும் இவறன்மை எற்றுள்ளும்
  எண்ணப் படுவதொன் றன்று.

commentary: Let no (one) praise himself, at any time; let him not desire to do useless things.
en:
Never indulge in self-complaisant mood,
Nor deed desire that yields no gain of good.
ta:
வியவற்க எஞ்ஞான்றும் தன்னை நயவற்க
  நன்றி பயவா வினை.

commentary: If (a king) enjoys, privately the things which he desires, the designs of his enemies will be useless.
en:
If, to your foes unknown, you cherish what you love,
Counsels of men who wish you harm will harmless prove.
ta:
காதல காதல் அறியாமை உய்க்கிற்பின்
  ஏதில ஏதிலார் நூல்.

commentary: Let (a king) ponder well its value, and secure the friendship of men of virtue and of mature knowledge.
en:
As friends the men who virtue know, and riper wisdom share,
Their worth weighed well, the king should choose with care.
ta:
அறனறிந்து மூத்த அறிவுடையார் கேண்மை
  திறனறிந்து தேர்ந்து கொளல்.

commentary: Let (a king) procure and kindly care for men who can overcome difficulties when they occur, and guard against them before they happen.
en:
Cherish the all-accomplished men as friends,
Whose skill the present ill removes, from coming ill defends.
ta:
உற்றநோய் நீக்கி உறாஅமை முற்காக்கும்
  பெற்றியார்ப் பேணிக் கொளல்.

commentary: To cherish great men and make them his own, is the most difficult of all difficult things.
en:
To cherish men of mighty soul, and make them all their own,
Of kingly treasures rare, as rarest gift is known.
ta:
அரியவற்று ளெல்லாம் அரிதே பெரியாரைப்
  பேணித் தமராக் கொளல்.

commentary: So to act as to make those men, his own, who are greater than himself is of all powers the highest.
en:
To live with men of greatness that their own excels,
As cherished friends, is greatest power that with a monarch dwells.
ta:
தம்மிற் பெரியார் தமரா ஒழுகுதல்
  வன்மையு ளெல்லாந் தலை.

commentary: As a king must use his ministers as eyes (in managing his kingdom), let him well examine their character and qualifications before he engages them.
en:
The king, since counsellors are monarch's eyes,
Should counsellors select with counsel wise.
ta:
சூழ்வார்கண் ணாக ஒழுகலான் மன்னவன்
  சூழ்வாரைக் சூழ்ந்து கொளல்.

commentary: There will be nothing left for enemies to do, against him who has the power of acting (so as to secure) the fellowship of worthy men.
en:
The king, who knows to live with worthy men allied,
Has nought to fear from any foeman's pride.
ta:
தக்கா ரினத்தனாய்த் தானொழுக வல்லானைச்
  செற்றார் செயக்கிடந்த தில்.

commentary: Who are great enough to destroy him who has servants that have power to rebuke him ?
en:
What power can work his fall, who faithful ministers
Employs, that thunder out reproaches when he errs.
ta:
இடிக்குந் துணையாரை யாள்வரை யாரே
  கெடுக்குந் தகைமை யவர்.

commentary: The king, who is without the guard of men who can rebuke him, will perish, even though there be no one to destroy him.
en:
The king with none to censure him, bereft of safeguards all,
Though none his ruin work, shall surely ruined fall.
ta:
இடிப்பாரை இல்லாத ஏமரா மன்னன்
  கெடுப்பா ரிலானுங் கெடும்.

commentary: There can be no gain to those who have no capital; and in like manner there can be no permanence to those who are without the support of adherents.
en:
Who owns no principal, can have no gain of usury;
Who lacks support of friends, knows no stability.
ta:
முதலிலார்க ஊதிய மில்லை மதலையாஞ்
  சார்பிலார்க் கில்லை நிலை.

commentary: It is tenfold more injurious to abandon the friendship of the good, than to incur the hatred of the many.
en:
Than hate of many foes incurred, works greater woe
Ten-fold, of worthy men the friendship to forego.
ta:
பல்லார் பகைகொளலிற் பத்தடுத்த தீமைத்தே
  நல்லார் தொடர்கை விடல்.

commentary: (True) greatness fears the society of the base; it is only the low - minded who will regard them as friends.
en:
The great of soul will mean association fear;
The mean of soul regard mean men as kinsmen dear.
ta:
சிற்றினம் அஞ்சும் பெருமை சிறுமைதான்
  சுற்றமாச் சூழ்ந்து விடும்.

commentary: As water changes (its nature), from the nature of the soil (in which it flows), so will the character of men resemble that of their associates.
en:
The waters' virtues change with soil through which they flow;
As man's companionship so will his wisdom show.
ta:
நிலத்தியல்பால் நீர்திரிந் தற்றாகும் மாந்தர்க்கு
  இனத்தியல்ப தாகும் அறிவு.

commentary: The power of knowing is from the mind; (but) his character is from that of his associates.
en:
Perceptions manifold in men are of the mind alone;
The value of the man by his companionship is known.
ta:
மனத்தானாம் மாந்தர்க் குணர்ச்சி இனத்தானாம்
  இன்னான் எனப்படுஞ் சொல்.

commentary: Wisdom appears to rest in the mind, but it really exists to a man in his companions.
en:
Man's wisdom seems the offspring of his mind;
'Tis outcome of companionship we find.
ta:
மனத்து ளதுபோலக் காட்டி ஒருவற்கு
  இனத்துள தாகும் அறிவு.

commentary: Chaste company is the staff on which come, these two things, viz, purity of mind and purity of conduct.
en:
Both purity of mind, and purity of action clear,
Leaning no staff of pure companionship, to man draw near.
ta:
மனந்தூய்மை செய்வினை தூய்மை இரண்டும்
  இனந்தூய்மை தூவா வரும்.

commentary: To the pure-minded there will be a good posterity. By those whose associates are pure, no deeds will be done that are not good.
en:
From true pure-minded men a virtuous race proceeds;
To men of pure companionship belong no evil deeds.
ta:
மனந்தூயார்க் கெச்சம்நன் றாகும் இனந்தூயார்க்கு
  இல்லைநன் றாகா வினை.

commentary: Goodness of mind will give wealth, and good society will bring with it all praise, to men.
en:
Goodness of mind to lives of men increaseth gain;
And good companionship doth all of praise obtain.
ta:
மனநலம் மன்னுயிர்க் காக்கம் இனநலம்
  எல்லாப் புகழும் தரும்.

commentary: Although they may have great (natural) goodness of mind, yet good society will tend to strengthen it.
en:
To perfect men, though minds right good belong,
Yet good companionship is confirmation strong.
ta:
மனநலம் நன்குடைய ராயினும் சான்றோர்க்கு
  இனநலம் ஏமாப் புடைத்து.

commentary: Future bliss is (the result) of goodness of mind; and even this acquires strength from the society of the good.
en:
Although to mental goodness joys of other life belong,
Yet good companionship is confirmation strong.
ta:
மனநலத்தின் ஆகும் மறுமைமற் றஃதும்
  இனநலத்தின் ஏமாப் புடைத்து.

commentary: There is no greater help than the company of the good; there is no greater source of sorrow than the company of the wicked.
en:
Than good companionship no surer help we know;
Than bad companionship nought causes direr woe.
ta:
நல்லினத்தி னூங்குந் துணையில்லை தீயினத்தின்
  அல்லற் படுப்பதூஉம் இல்.

commentary: Let a man reflect on what will be lost, what will be acquired and (from these) what will be his ultimate gain, and (then, let him) act.
en:
Expenditure, return, and profit of the deed
In time to come; weigh these- than to the act proceed.
ta:
அழிவதூஉம் ஆவதூஉம் ஆகி வழிபயக்கும்
  ஊதியமும் சூழ்ந்து செயல்.

commentary: There is nothing too difficult to (be attained by) those who, before they act, reflect well themselves, and thoroughly consider (the matter) with chosen friends.
en:
With chosen friends deliberate; next use the private thought;
Then act. By those who thus proceed all works with ease are wrought.
ta:
தெரிந்த இனத்தொடு தேர்ந்தெண்ணிச் செய்வார்க்கு
  அரும்பொருள் யாதொன்றும் இல்.

commentary: Wise men will not, in the hopes of profit, undertake works that will consume their principal.
en:
To risk one's all and lose, aiming at added gain,
Is rash affair, from which the wise abstain.
ta:
ஆக்கம் கருதி முதலிழக்கும் செய்வினை
  ஊக்கார் அறிவுடை யார்.

commentary: Those who fear reproach will not commence anything which has not been (thoroughly considered) and made clear to them.
en:
A work of which the issue is not clear,
Begin not they reproachful scorn who fear.
ta:
தெளிவி லதனைத் தொடங்கார் இளிவென்னும்
  ஏதப்பாடு அஞ்சு பவர்.

commentary: One way to promote the prosperity of an enemy, is (for a king) to set out (to war) without having thoroughly weighed his ability (to cope with its chances).
en:
With plans not well matured to rise against your foe,
Is way to plant him out where he is sure to grow!
ta:
வகையறச் சூழா தெழுதல் பகைவரைப்
  பாத்திப் படுப்பதோ ராறு.

commentary: He will perish who does not what is not fit to do; and he also will perish who does not do what it is fit to do.
en:
'Tis ruin if man do an unbefitting thing;
Fit things to leave undone will equal ruin bring.
ta:
செய்தக்க அல்ல செயக்கெடும் செய்தக்க
  செய்யாமை யானுங் கெடும்.

commentary: Consider, and then undertake a matter; after having undertaken it, to say "We will consider," is folly.
en:
Think, and then dare the deed! Who cry,
'Deed dared, we'll think,' disgraced shall be.
ta:
எண்ணித் துணிக கருமம் துணிந்தபின்
  எண்ணுவம் என்பது இழுக்கு.

commentary: The work, which is not done by suitable methods, will fail though many stand to uphold it.
en:
On no right system if man toil and strive,
Though many men assist, no work can thrive.
ta:
ஆற்றின் வருந்தா வருத்தம் பலர்நின்று
  போற்றினும் பொத்துப் படும்.

commentary: There are failures even in acting well, when it is done without knowing the various dispositions of men.
en:
Though well the work be done, yet one mistake is made,
To habitudes of various men when no regard is paid.
ta:
நன்றாற்ற லுள்ளுந் தவுறுண்டு அவரவர்
  பண்பறிந் தாற்றாக் கடை.

commentary: Let a man reflect, and do things which bring no reproach; the world will not approve, with him, of things which do not become of his position to adopt.
en:
Plan and perform no work that others may despise;
What misbeseems a king the world will not approve as wise.
ta:
எள்ளாத எண்ணிச் செயல்வேண்டும் தம்மோடு
  கொள்ளாத கொள்ளாது உலகு.

commentary: Let (one) weigh well the strength of the deed (he purposes to do), his own strength, the strength of his enemy, and the strength of the allies (of both), and then let him act.
en:
The force the strife demands, the force he owns, the force of foes,
The force of friends; these should he weigh ere to the war he goes.
ta:
வினைவலியும் தன்வலியும் மாற்றான் வலியும்
  துணைவலியும் தூக்கிச் செயல்.

commentary: There is nothing which may not be accomplished by those who, before they attack (an enemy), make themselves acquainted with their own ability, and with whatever else is (needful) to be known, and apply themselves wholly to their object.
en:
Who know what can be wrought, with knowledge of the means, on this,
Their mind firm set, go forth, nought goes with them amiss.
ta:
ஒல்வ தறிவது அறிந்ததன் கண்தங்கிச்
  செல்வார்க்குச் செல்லாதது இல்.

commentary: There are many who, ignorant of their (want of) power (to meet it), have haughtily set out to war, and broken down in the midst of it.
en:
Ill-deeming of their proper powers, have many monarchs striven,
And midmost of unequal conflict fallen asunder riven.
ta:
உடைத்தம் வலியறியார் ஊக்கத்தின் ஊக்கி
  இடைக்கண் முரிந்தார் பலர்.

commentary: He will quickly perish who, ignorant of his own resources flatters himself of his greatness, and does not live in peace with his neighbours.
en:
Who not agrees with those around, no moderation knows,
In self-applause indulging, swift to ruin goes.
ta:
அமைந்தாங் கொழுகான் அளவறியான் தன்னை
  வியந்தான் விரைந்து கெடும்.

commentary: The axle tree of a bandy, loaded only with peacocks' feathers will break, if it be greatly overloaded.
en:
With peacock feathers light, you load the wain;
Yet, heaped too high, the axle snaps in twain.
ta:
பீலிபெய் சாகாடும் அச்சிறும் அப்பண்டஞ்
  சால மிகுத்துப் பெயின்.

commentary: There will be an end to the life of him who, having climbed out to the end of a branch, ventures to go further.
en:
Who daring climbs, and would himself upraise
Beyond the branch's tip, with life the forfeit pays.
ta:
நுனிக்கொம்பர் ஏறினார் அஃதிறந் தூக்கின்
  உயிர்க்கிறுதி ஆகி விடும்.

commentary: Let a man know the measure of his ability (to give), and let him give accordingly; such giving is the way to preserve his property.
en:
With knowledge of the measure due, as virtue bids you give!
That is the way to guard your wealth, and seemly live.
ta:
ஆற்றின் அறவறிந்து ஈக அதுபொருள்
  போற்றி வழங்கு நெறி.

commentary: Even though the income (of a king) be small, it will not cause his (ruin), if his outgoings be not larger than his income.
en:
Incomings may be scant; but yet, no failure there,
If in expenditure you rightly learn to spare.
ta:
ஆகாறு அளவிட்டி தாயினுங் கேடில்லை
  போகாறு அகலாக் கடை.

commentary: The prosperity of him who lives without knowing the measure (of his property), will perish, even while it seems to continue.
en:
Who prosperous lives and of enjoyment knows no bound,
His seeming wealth, departing, nowhere shall be found.
ta:
அளவற஧ந்து வாழாதான் வாழ்க்கை உளபோல
  இல்லாகித் தோன்றாக் கெடும்.

commentary: The measure of his wealth will quickly perish, whose liberality weighs not the measure of his property.
en:
Beneficence that measures not its bound of means,
Will swiftly bring to nought the wealth on which it leans.
ta:
உளவரை தூக்காத ஒப்புர வாண்மை
  வளவரை வல்லைக் கெடும்.

commentary: A crow will overcome an owl in the day time; so the king who would conquer his enemy must have (a suitable) time.
en:
A crow will conquer owl in broad daylight;
The king that foes would crush, needs fitting time to fight.
ta:
பகல்வெல்லும் கூகையைக் காக்கை இகல்வெல்லும்
  வேந்தர்க்கு வேண்டும் பொழுது.

commentary: Acting at the right season, is a cord that will immoveably bind success (to a king).
en:
The bond binds fortune fast is ordered effort made,
Strictly observant still of favouring season's aid.
ta:
பருவத்தோடு ஒட்ட ஒழுகல் திருவினைத்
  தீராமை ஆர்க்குங் கயிறு.

commentary: Is there anything difficult for him to do, who acts, with (the right) instruments at the right time ?
en:
Can any work be hard in very fact,
If men use fitting means in timely act?
ta:
அருவினை யென்ப உளவோ கருவியான்
  காலம் அற஧ந்து செயின்.

commentary: Though (a man) should meditate (the conquest of) the world, he may accomplish it if he acts in the right time, and at the right place.
en:
The pendant world's dominion may be won,
In fitting time and place by action done.
ta:
ஞாலம் கருதினுங் கைகூடுங் காலம்
  கருதி இடத்தாற் செயின்.

commentary: They who thoughtfully consider and wait for the (right) time (for action), may successfully meditate (the conquest of) the world.
en:
Who think the pendant world itself to subjugate,
With mind unruffled for the fitting time must wait.
ta:
காலம் கருதி இருப்பர் கலங்காது
  ஞாலம் கருது பவர்.

commentary: The self-restraint of the energetic (while waiting for a suitable opportunity), is like the drawing back of a fighting-ram in order to butt.
en:
The men of mighty power their hidden energies repress,
As fighting ram recoils to rush on foe with heavier stress.
ta:
ஊக்க முடையான் ஒடுக்கம் பொருதகர்
  தாக்கற்குப் பேருந் தகைத்து.

commentary: The wise will not immediately and hastily shew out their anger; they will watch their time, and restrain it within.
en:
The glorious once of wrath enkindled make no outward show,
At once; they bide their time, while hidden fires within them glow.
ta:
பொள்ளென ஆங்கே புறம்வேரார் காலம்பார்த்து
  உள்வேர்ப்பர் ஒள்ளி யவர்.

commentary: If one meets his enemy, let him show him all respect, until the time for his destruction is come; when that is come, his head will be easily brought low.
en:
If foes' detested form they see, with patience let them bear;
When fateful hour at last they spy,- the head lies there.
ta:
செறுநரைக் காணின் சுமக்க இறுவரை
  காணின் கிழக்காம் தலை.

commentary: If a rare opportunity occurs, while it lasts, let a man do that which is rarely to be accomplished (but for such an opportunity).
en:
When hardest gain of opportunity at last is won,
With promptitude let hardest deed be done.
ta:
எய்தற் கரியது இயைந்தக்கால் அந்நிலையே
  செய்தற் கரிய செயல்.

commentary: At the time when one should use self-control, let him restrain himself like a heron; and, let him like it, strike, when there is a favourable opportunity.
en:
As heron stands with folded wing, so wait in waiting hour;
As heron snaps its prey, when fortune smiles, put forth your power.
ta:
கொக்கொக்க கூம்பும் பருவத்து மற்றதன்
  குத்தொக்க சீர்த்த இடத்து.

commentary: Let not (a king) despise (an enemy), nor undertake any thing (against him), until he has obtained (a suitable) place for besieging him.
en:
Begin no work of war, depise no foe,
Till place where you can wholly circumvent you know.
ta:
தொடங்கற்க எவ்வினையும் எள்ளற்க முற்றும்
  இடங்கண்ட பின்அல் லது.

commentary: Even to those who are men of power and expedients, an attack in connection with a fortification will yield many advantages.
en:
Though skill in war combine with courage tried on battle-field,
The added gain of fort doth great advantage yield.
ta:
முரண்சேர்ந்த மொய்ம்பி னவர்க்கும் அரண்சேர்ந்தாம்
  ஆக்கம் பலவுந் தரும்.

commentary: Even the powerless will become powerful and conquer, if they select a proper field (of action), and guard themselves, while they make war on their enemies.
en:
E'en weak ones mightily prevail, if place of strong defence,
They find, protect themselves, and work their foes offence.
ta:
ஆற்றாரும் ஆற்றி அடுப இடனறிந்து
  போற்றார்கண் போற்றிச் செயின்.

commentary: If they who draw near (to fight) choose a suitable place to approach (their enemy), the latter, will have to relinquish the thought which they once entertained, of conquering them.
en:
The foes who thought to triumph, find their thoughts were vain,
If hosts advance, seize vantage ground, and thence the fight maintain.
ta:
எண்ணியார் எண்ணம் இழப்பர் இடனறிந்து
  துன்னியார் துன்னிச் செயின்.

commentary: In deep water, a crocodile will conquer (all other animals); but if it leave the water, other animals will conquer it.
en:
The crocodile prevails in its own flow of water wide,
If this it leaves, 'tis slain by anything beside.
ta:
நெடும்புனலுள் வெல்லும் முதலை அடும்புனலின்
  நீங்கின் அதனைப் பிற.

commentary: Wide chariots, with mighty wheels, will not run on the ocean; neither will ships that the traverse ocean, move on the earth.
en:
The lofty car, with mighty wheel, sails not o'er watery main,
The boat that skims the sea, runs not on earth's hard plain.
ta:
கடலோடா கால்வல் நெடுந்தேர் கடலோடும்
  நாவாயும் ஓடா நிலத்து.

commentary: You will need no other aid than fearlessness, if you thoroughly reflect (on what you are to do), and select (a suitable) place for your operations.
en:
Save their own fearless might they need no other aid,
If in right place they fight, all due provision made.
ta:
அஞ்சாமை அல்லால் துணைவேண்டா எஞ்சாமை
  எண்ணி இடத்தால் செயின்.

commentary: The power of one who has a large army will perish, if he goes into ground where only a small army can act.
en:
If lord of army vast the safe retreat assail
Of him whose host is small, his mightiest efforts fail.
ta:
சிறுபடையான் செல்லிடம் சேரின் உறுபடையான்
  ஊக்கம் அழிந்து விடும்.

commentary: It is a hazardous thing to attack men in their own country, although they may neither have power nor a good fortress.
en:
Though fort be none, and store of wealth they lack,
'Tis hard a people's homesteads to attack!
ta:
சிறைநலனும் சீரும் இலரெனினும் மாந்தர்
  உறைநிலத்தோடு ஒட்டல் அரிது.

commentary: A fox can kill a fearless, warrior-faced elephant, if it go into mud in which its legs sink down.
en:
The jackal slays, in miry paths of foot-betraying fen,
The elephant of fearless eye and tusks transfixing armed men.
ta:
காலாழ் களரில் நரியடும் கண்ணஞ்சா
  வேலாள் முகத்த களிறு.

commentary: Let (a minister) be chosen, after he has been tried by means of these four things, viz,-his virtue, (love of) money, (love of) sexual pleasure, and tear of (losing) life.
en:
How treats he virtue, wealth and pleasure? How, when life's at stake,
Comports himself? This four-fold test of man will full assurance make.
ta:
அறம்பொருள் இன்பம் உயிரச்சம் நான்கின்
  திறந்தெரிந்து தேறப் படும்.

commentary: (The king's) choice should (fall) on him, who is of good family, who is free from faults, and who has the modesty which fears the wounds (of sin).
en:
Of noble race, of faultless worth, of generous pride
That shrinks from shame or stain; in him may king confide.
ta:
குடிப்பிறந்து குற்றத்தின் நீங்கி வடுப்பரியும்
  நாணுடையான் சுட்டே தெளிவு.

commentary: When even men, who have studied the most difficult works, and who are free from faults, are (carefully) examined, it is a rare thing to find them without ignorance.
en:
Though deeply learned, unflecked by fault, 'tis rare to see,
When closely scanned, a man from all unwisdom free.
ta:
அரியகற்று ஆசற்றார் கண்ணும் தெரியுங்கால்
  இன்மை அரிதே வெளிறு.

commentary: Let (a king) consider (a man's) good qualities, as well as his faults, and then judge (of his character) by that which prevails.
en:
Weigh well the good of each, his failings closely scan,
As these or those prevail, so estimate the man.
ta:
குணம்நாடிக் குற்றமும் நாடி அவற்றுள்
  மிகைநாடி மிக்க கொளல்.

commentary: A man's deeds are the touchstone of his greatness and littleness.
en:
Of greatness and of meanness too,
The deeds of each are touchstone true.
ta:
பெருமைக்கும் ஏனைச் சிறுமைக்கும் தத்தம்
  கருமமே கட்டளைக் கல்.

commentary: Let (a king) avoid choosing men who have no relations; such men have no attachment, and therefore have no fear of crime.
en:
Beware of trusting men who have no kith of kin;
No bonds restrain such men, no shame deters from sin.
ta:
அற்றாரைத் தேறுதல் ஓம்புக மற்றவர்
  பற்றிலர் நாணார் பழி.

commentary: To choose ignorant men, through partiality, is the height of folly.
en:
By fond affection led who trusts in men of unwise soul,
Yields all his being up to folly's blind control.
ta:
காதன்மை கந்தா அறிவறியார்த் தேறுதல்
  பேதைமை எல்லாந் தரும்.

commentary: Sorrow that will not leave even his posterity will come upon him chooses a stranger whose character he has not known.
en:
Who trusts an untried stranger, brings disgrace,
Remediless, on all his race.
ta:
தேரான் பிறனைத் தெளிந்தான் வழிமுறை
  தீரா இடும்பை தரும்.

commentary: Let (a king) choose no one without previous consideration; after he has made his choice, let him unhesitatingly select for each such duties as are appropriate.
en:
Trust no man whom you have not fully tried,
When tested, in his prudence proved confide.
ta:
தேறற்க யாரையும் தேராது தேர்ந்தபின்
  தேறுக தேறும் பொருள்.

commentary: To make choice of one who has not been examined, and to entertain doubts respecting one who has been chosen, will produce irremediable sorrow.
en:
Trust where you have not tried, doubt of a friend to feel,
Once trusted, wounds inflict that nought can heal.
ta:
தேரான் தெளிவும் தெளிந்தான்கண் ஐயுறவும்
  தீரா இடும்பை தரும்.

commentary: He should be employed (by a king), whose nature leads him to choose the good, after having weighed both the evil and the good in any undertaking.
en:
Who good and evil scanning, ever makes the good his joy;
Such man of virtuous mood should king employ.
ta:
நன்மையும் தீமையும் நாடி நலம்புரிந்த
  தன்மையான் ஆளப் படும்.

commentary: Let him do (the king's) work who can enlarge the sources (of revenue), increase wealth and considerately prevent the accidents (which would destroy it).
en:
Who swells the revenues, spreads plenty o'er the land,
Seeks out what hinders progress, his the workman's hand.
ta:
வாரி பெருக்கி வளம்படுத்து உற்றவை
  ஆராய்வான் செய்க வினை.

commentary: Let the choice (of a king) fall upon him who largely possesses these four things, love, knowledge, a clear mind and freedom from covetousness.
en:
A loyal love with wisdom, clearness, mind from avarice free;
Who hath these four good gifts should ever trusted be.
ta:
அன்பறிவு தேற்றம் அவாவின்மை இந்நான்கும்
  நன்குடையான் கட்டே தெளிவு.

commentary: Even when (a king) has tried them in every possible way, there are many men who change, from the nature of the works (in which they may be employed).
en:
Even when tests of every kind are multiplied,
Full many a man proves otherwise, by action tried!
ta:
எனைவகையான் தேறியக் கண்ணும் வினைவகையான்
  வேறாகும் மாந்தர் பலர்.

commentary: (A king's) work can only be accomplished by a man of wisdom and patient endurance; it is not of a nature to be given to one from mere personal attachment.
en:
No specious fav'rite should the king's commission bear,
But he that knows, and work performs with patient care.
ta:
அறிந்தாற்றிச் செய்கிற்பாற்கு அல்லால் வினைதான்
  சிறந்தானென்று ஏவற்பாற் றன்று.

commentary: Let (a king) act, after having considered the agent (whom he is to employ), the deed (he desires to do), and the time which is suitable to it.
en:
Let king first ask, 'Who shall the deed perform?' and 'What the deed?'
Of hour befitting both assured, let every work proceed.
ta:
செய்வானை நாடி வினைநாடிக் காலத்தோடு
  எய்த உணர்ந்து செயல்.

commentary: After having considered, "this man can accomplish this, by these means", let (the king) leave with him the discharge of that duty.
en:
'This man, this work shall thus work out,' let thoughtful king command;
Then leave the matter wholly in his servant's hand.
ta:
இதனை இதனால் இவன்முடிக்கும் என்றாய்ந்து
  அதனை அவன்கண் விடல்.

commentary: Having considered what work a man is fit for, let (the king) employ him in that work.
en:
As each man's special aptitude is known,
Bid each man make that special work his own.
ta:
வினைக்குரிமை நாடிய பின்றை அவனை
  அதற்குரிய னாகச் செயல்.
