commentary: Prosperity will leave (the king) who doubts the friendship of the man who steadily labours in the discharge of his duties.
en:
Fortune deserts the king who ill can bear,
Informal friendly ways of men his tolls who share.
ta:
வினைக்கண் வினையுடையான் கேண்மைவே றாக
  நினைப்பானை நீங்கும் திரு.

commentary: Let a king daily examine the conduct of his servants; if they do not act crookedly, the world will not act crookedly.
en:
Let king search out his servants' deeds each day;
When these do right, the world goes rightly on its way.
ta:
நாடோறும் நாடுக மன்னன் வினைசெய்வான்
  கோடாமை கோடா துலகு.

commentary: Even when (a man's) property is all gone, relatives will act towards him with their accustomed (kindness).
en:
When wealth is fled, old kindness still to show,
Is kindly grace that only kinsmen know.
ta:
பற்றற்ற கண்ணும் பழைமைபா ராட்டுதல்
  சுற்றத்தார் கண்ணே உள.

commentary: If (a man's) relatives remain attached to him with unchanging love, it will be a source of ever-increasing wealth.
en:
The gift of kin's unfailing love bestows
Much gain of good, like flower that fadeless blows.
ta:
விருப்பறாச் சுற்றம் இயையின் அருப்பறா
  ஆக்கம் பலவும் தரும்.

commentary: The wealth of one who does not mingle freely with his relatives, will be like the filling of water in a spacious tank that has no banks.
en:
His joy of life who mingles not with kinsmen gathered round,
Is lake where streams pour in, with no encircling bound.
ta:
அளவளா வில்லாதான் வாழ்க்கை குளவளாக்
  கோடின்றி நீர்நிறைந் தற்று.

commentary: To live surrounded by relatives, is the advantage to be derived from the acquisition of wealth.
en:
The profit gained by wealth's increase,
Is living compassed round by relatives in peace.
ta:
சுற்றத்தால் சுற்றப் படஒழுகல் செல்வந்தான்
  பெற்றத்தால் பெற்ற பயன்.

commentary: He will be surrounded by numerous relatives who manifests generosity and affability.
en:
Who knows the use of pleasant words, and liberal gifts can give,
Connections, heaps of them, surrounding him shall live.
ta:
கொடுத்தலும் இன்சொலும் ஆற்றின் அடுக்கிய
  சுற்றத்தால் சுற்றப் படும்.

commentary: No one, in all the world, will have so many relatives (about him), as he who makes large gift, and does not give way to anger.
en:
Than one who gifts bestows and wrath restrains,
Through the wide world none larger following gains.
ta:
பெருங்கொடையான் பேணான் வெகுளி அவனின்
  மருங்குடையார் மாநிலத்து இல்.

commentary: The crows do not conceal (their prey), but will call out for others (to share with them) while they eat it; wealth will be with those who show a similar disposition (towards their relatives).
en:
The crows conceal not, call their friends to come, then eat;
Increase of good such worthy ones shall meet.
ta:
காக்கை கரவா கரைந்துண்ணும் ஆக்கமும்
  அன்னநீ ரார்க்கே உள.

commentary: Many relatives will live near a king, when they observe that he does not look on all alike, but that he looks on each man according to his merit.
en:
Where king regards not all alike, but each in his degree,
'Neath such discerning rule many dwell happily.
ta:
பொதுநோக்கான் வேந்தன் வரிசையா நோக்கின்
  அதுநோக்கி வாழ்வார் பலர்.

commentary: Those who have been friends and have afterwards forsaken him, will return and join themselves (to him), when the cause of disagreement is not to be found in him.
en:
Who once were his, and then forsook him, as before
Will come around, when cause of disagreement is no more.
ta:
தமராகிக் தற்றுறந்தார் சுற்றம் அமராமைக்
  காரணம் இன்றி வரும்.

commentary: When one may have left him, and for some cause has returned to him, let the king fulfil the object (for which he has come back) and thoughtfully receive him again.
en:
Who causeless went away, then to return, for any cause, ask leave;
The king should sift their motives well, consider, and receive!
ta:
உழைப்பிரிந்து காரணத்தின் வந்தானை வேந்தன்
  இழைத்திருந்து எண்ணிக் கொளல்.

commentary: More evil than excessive anger, is forgetfulness which springs from the intoxication of great joy.
en:
'Tis greater ill, it rapture of o'erweening gladness to the soul
Bring self-forgetfulness than if transcendent wrath control.
ta:
இறந்த வெகுளியின் தீதே சிறந்த
  உவகை மகிழ்ச்சியிற் சோர்வு.

commentary: Forgetfulness will destroy fame, even as constant poverty destroys knowledge.
en:
Perpetual, poverty is death to wisdom of the wise;
When man forgets himself his glory dies!
ta:
பொச்சாப்புக் கொல்லும் புகழை அறிவினை
  நிச்ச நிரப்புக்கொன் றாங்கு.

commentary: Thoughtlessness will never acquire fame; and this tenet is upheld by all treatises in the world.
en:
'To self-oblivious men no praise'; this rule
Decisive wisdom sums of every school.
ta:
பொச்சாப்பார்க் கில்லை புகழ்மை அதுஉலகத்து
  எப்பால்நூ லோர்க்கும் துணிவு.

commentary: Just as the coward has no defence (by whatever fortifications ha may be surrounded), so the thoughtless has no good (whatever advantages he may possess).
en:
'To cowards is no fort's defence'; e'en so
The self-oblivious men no blessing know.
ta:
அச்ச முடையார்க்கு அரணில்லை ஆங்கில்லை
  பொச்சாப் புடையார்க்கு நன்கு.

commentary: The thoughtless man, who provides not against the calamities that may happen, will afterwards repent for his fault.
en:
To him who nought foresees, recks not of anything,
The after woe shall sure repentance bring.
ta:
முன்னுறக் காவாது இழுக்கியான் தன்பிழை
  பின்னூறு இரங்கி விடும்.

commentary: There is nothing comparable with the possession of unfailing thoughtfulness at all times; and towards all persons.
en:
Towards all unswerving, ever watchfulness of soul retain,
Where this is found there is no greater gain.
ta:
இழுக்காமை யார்மாட்டும் என்றும் வழுக்காமை
  வாயின் அதுவொப்பது இல்.

commentary: There is nothing too difficult to be accomplished, if a man set about it carefully, with unflinching endeavour.
en:
Though things are arduous deemed, there's nought may not be won,
When work with mind's unslumbering energy and thought is done.
ta:
அரியஎன்று ஆகாத இல்லைபொச் சாவாக்
  கருவியால் போற்றிச் செயின்.

commentary: Let (a man) observe and do these things which have been praised (by the wise); if he neglects and fails to perform them, for him there will be no (happiness) throughout the seven births.
en:
Let things that merit praise thy watchful soul employ;
Who these despise attain through sevenfold births no joy.
ta:
புகழ்ந்தவை போற்றிச் செயல்வேண்டும் செய்யாது
  இகழ்ந்தார்க்கு எழுமையும் இல்.

commentary: Let (a king) think of those who have been ruined by neglect, when his mind is elated with joy.
en:
Think on the men whom scornful mind hath brought to nought,
When exultation overwhelms thy wildered thought.
ta:
இகழ்ச்சியின் கெட்டாரை உள்ளுக தாந்தம்
  மகிழ்ச்சியின் மைந்துறும் போழ்து.

commentary: It is easy for (one) to obtain whatever he may think of, if he can again think of it.
en:
'Tis easy what thou hast in mind to gain,
If what thou hast in mind thy mind retain.
ta:
உள்ளியது எய்தல் எளிதுமன் மற்றுந்தான்
  உள்ளியது உள்ளப் பெறின்.

commentary: To examine into (the crimes which may be committed), to show no favour (to any one), to desire to act with impartiality towards all, and to inflict (such punishments) as may be wisely resolved on, constitute rectitude.
en:
Search out, to no one favour show; with heart that justice loves
Consult, then act; this is the rule that right approves.
ta:
ஓர்ந்துகண் ணோடாது இறைபுரிந்து யார்மாட்டும்
  தேர்ந்துசெய் வஃதே முறை.

commentary: When there is rain, the living creation thrives; and so when the king rules justly, his subjects thrive.
en:
All earth looks up to heav'n whence raindrops fall;
All subjects look to king that ruleth all.
ta:
வானோக்கி வாழும் உலகெல்லாம் மன்னவன்
  கோல்நோக்கி வாழுங் குடி.

commentary: The sceptre of the king is the firm support of the Vedas of the Brahmin, and of all virtues therein described.
en:
Learning and virtue of the sages spring,
From all-controlling sceptre of the king.
ta:
அந்தணர் நூற்கும் அறத்திற்கும் ஆதியாய்
  நின்றது மன்னவன் கோல்.

commentary: The world will constantly embrace the feet of the great king who rules over his subjects with love.
en:
Whose heart embraces subjects all, lord over mighty land
Who rules, the world his feet embracing stands.
ta:
குடிதழீஇக் கோலோச்சும் மாநில மன்னன்
  அடிதழீஇ நிற்கும் உலகு.

commentary: Rain and plentiful crops will ever dwell together in the country of the king who sways his sceptre with justice.
en:
Where king, who righteous laws regards, the sceptre wields,
There fall the showers, there rich abundance crowns the fields.
ta:
இயல்புளிக் கோலோச்சும் மன்னவன் நாட்ட
  பெயலும் விளையுளும் தொக்கு.

commentary: It is not the javelin that gives victory, but the king's sceptre, if it do no injustice.
en:
Not lance gives kings the victory,
But sceptre swayed with equity.
ta:
வேலன்று வென்றி தருவது மன்னவன்
  கோலதூஉங் கோடா தெனின்.

commentary: The king defends the whole world; and justice, when administered without defect, defends the king.
en:
The king all the whole realm of earth protects;
And justice guards the king who right respects.
ta:
இறைகாக்கும் வையகம் எல்லாம் அவனை
  முறைகாக்கும் முட்டாச் செயின்.

commentary: The king who gives not facile audience (to those who approach him), and who does not examine and pass judgment (on their complaints), will perish in disgrace.
en:
Hard of access, nought searching out, with partial hand
The king who rules, shall sink and perish from the land.
ta:
எண்பதத்தான் ஓரா முறைசெய்யா மன்னவன்
  தண்பதத்தான் தானே கெடும்.

commentary: In guarding his subjects (against injury from others), and in preserving them himself; to punish crime is not a fault in a king, but a duty.
en:
Abroad to guard, at home to punish, brings
No just reproach; 'tis work assigned to kings.
ta:
குடிபுறங் காத்தோம்பிக் குற்றம் கடிதல்
  வடுவன்று வேந்தன் தொழில்.

commentary: For a king to punish criminals with death, is like pulling up the weeds in the green corn.
en:
By punishment of death the cruel to restrain,
Is as when farmer frees from weeds the tender grain.
ta:
கொலையிற் கொடியாரை வேந்தொறுத்தல் பைங்கூழ்
  களைகட் டதனொடு நேர்.

commentary: The king who gives himself up to oppression and acts unjustly (towards his subjects) is more cruel than the man who leads the life of a murderer.
en:
Than one who plies the murderer's trade, more cruel is the king
Who all injustice works, his subjects harassing.
ta:
கொலைமேற்கொண் டாரிற் கொடிதே அலைமேற்கொண்டு
  அல்லவை செய்தொழுகும் வேந்து.

commentary: The request (for money) of him who holds the sceptre is like the word of a highway robber who stands with a weapon in hand and says "give up your wealth".
en:
As 'Give' the robber cries with lance uplift,
So kings with sceptred hand implore a gift.
ta:
வேலொடு நின்றான் இடுவென் றதுபோலும்
  கோலொடு நின்றான் இரவு.

commentary: The country of the king who does not daily examine into the wrongs done and distribute justice, will daily fall to ruin.
en:
Who makes no daily search for wrongs, nor justly rules, that king
Doth day by day his realm to ruin bring.
ta:
நாடொறும் நாடி முறைசெய்யா மன்னவன்
  நாடொறும் நாடு கெடும்.

commentary: The king, who, without reflecting (on its evil consequences), perverts justice, will lose at once both his wealth and his subjects.
en:
Whose rod from right deflects, who counsel doth refuse,
At once his wealth and people utterly shall lose.
ta:
கூழுங் குடியும் ஒருங்கிழக்கும் கோல்கோடிச்
  சூழாது செய்யும் அரசு.

commentary: Will not the tears, shed by a people who cannot endure the oppression which they suffer (from their king), become a saw to waste away his wealth ?
en:
His people's tears of sorrow past endurance, are not they
Sharp instruments to wear the monarch's wealth away?
ta:
அல்லற்பட்டு ஆற்றாது அழுதகண் ணீரன்றே
  செல்வத்தைத் தேய்க்கும் படை

commentary: Righteous government gives permanence to (the fame of) kings; without that their fame will have no endurance.
en:
To rulers' rule stability is sceptre right;
When this is not, quenched is the rulers' light.
ta:
மன்னர்க்கு மன்னுதல் செங்கோன்மை அஃதின்றேல்
  மன்னாவாம் மன்னர்க் கொளி.

commentary: As is the world without rain, so live a people whose king is without kindness.
en:
As lack of rain to thirsty lands beneath,
Is lack of grace in kings to all that breathe.
ta:
துளியின்மை ஞாலத்திற்கு எற்றற்றே வேந்தன்
  அளியின்மை வாழும் உயிர்க்கு.

commentary: Property gives more sorrow than poverty, to those who live under the sceptre of a king without justice.
en:
To poverty it adds a sharper sting,
To live beneath the sway of unjust king.
ta:
இன்மையின் இன்னாது உடைமை முறைசெய்யா
  மன்னவன் கோற்கீழ்ப் படின்.

commentary: If the king acts contrary to justice, rain will become unseasonable, and the heavens will withhold their showers.
en:
Where king from right deflecting, makes unrighteous gain,
The seasons change, the clouds pour down no rain.
ta:
முறைகோடி மன்னவன் செய்யின் உறைகோடி
  ஒல்லாது வானம் பெயல்.

commentary: If the guardian (of the country) neglects to guard it, the produce of the cows will fail, and the men of six duties viz., the Brahmins will forget the vedas.
en:
Where guardian guardeth not, udder of kine grows dry,
And Brahmans' sacred lore will all forgotten lie.
ta:
ஆபயன் குன்றும் அறுதொழிலோர் நூல்மறப்பர்
  காவலன் காவான் எனின்.

commentary: He is a king who having equitably examined (any injustice which has been brought to his notice), suitably punishes it, so that it may not be again committed.
en:
Who punishes, investigation made in due degree,
So as to stay advance of crime, a king is he.
ta:
தக்காங்கு நாடித் தலைச்செல்லா வண்ணத்தால்
  ஒத்தாங்கு ஒறுப்பது வேந்து.

commentary: Let the king, who desires that his prosperity may long remain, commence his preliminary enquires with strictness, and then punish with mildness.
en:
For length of days with still increasing joys on Heav'n who call,
Should raise the rod with brow severe, but let it gently fall.
ta:
கடிதோச்சி மெல்ல எறிக நெடிதாக்கம்
  நீங்காமை வேண்டு பவர்.

commentary: The cruel-sceptred king, who acts so as to put his subjects in fear, will certainly and quickly come to ruin.
en:
Where subjects dread of cruel wrongs endure,
Ruin to unjust king is swift and sure.
ta:
வெருவந்த செய்தொழுகும் வெங்கோல னாயின்
  ஒருவந்தம் ஒல்லைக் கெடும்.

commentary: The king who is spoken of as cruel will quickly perish; his life becoming shortened.
en:
'Ah! cruel is our king', where subjects sadly say,
His age shall dwindle, swift his joy of life decay.
ta:
இறைகடியன் என்றுரைக்கும் இன்னாச்சொல் வேந்தன்
  உறைகடுகி ஒல்லைக் கெடும்.

commentary: The great wealth of him who is difficult of access and possesses a sternness of countenance, is like that which has been obtained by a devil.
en:
Whom subjects scarce may see, of harsh forbidding countenance;
His ample wealth shall waste, blasted by demon's glance.
ta:
அருஞ்செவ்வி இன்னா முகத்தான் பெருஞ்செல்வம்
  பேஎய்கண் டன்னது உடைத்து.

commentary: The abundant wealth of the king whose words are harsh and whose looks are void of kindness, will instantly perish instead of abiding long, with him.
en:
The tyrant, harsh in speach and hard of eye,
His ample joy, swift fading, soon shall die.
ta:
கடுஞ்சொல்லன் கண்ணிலன் ஆயின் நெடுஞ்செல்வம்
  நீடின்றி ஆங்கே கெடும்.

commentary: Severe words and excessive punishments will be a file to waste away a king's power for destroying (his enemies).
en:
Harsh words and punishments severe beyond the right,
Are file that wears away the monarch's conquering might.
ta:
கடுமொழியும் கையிகந்த தண்டமும் வேந்தன்
  அடுமுரண் தேய்க்கும் அரம்.

commentary: The prosperity of that king will waste away, who without reflecting (on his affairs himself), commits them to his ministers, and (when a failure occurs) gives way to anger, and rages against them.
en:
Who leaves the work to those around, and thinks of it no more;
If he in wrathful mood reprove, his prosperous days are o'er!
ta:
இனத்தாற்றி எண்ணாத வேந்தன் சினத்தாற்றிச்
  சீறிற் சிறுகும் திரு.

commentary: The king who has not provided himself with a place of defence, will in times of war be seized with fear and quickly perish.
en:
Who builds no fort whence he may foe defy,
In time of war shall fear and swiftly die.
ta:
செருவந்த போழ்திற் சிறைசெய்யா வேந்தன்
  வெருவந்து வெய்து கெடும்.

commentary: The earth bears up no greater burden than ignorant men whom a cruel sceptre attaches to itself (as the ministers of its evil deeds).
en:
Tyrants with fools their counsels share:
Earth can no heavier burthen bear!
ta:
கல்லார்ப் பிணிக்கும் கடுங்கோல் அதுவல்லது
  இல்லை நிலக்குப் பொறை.

commentary: The world exists through that greatest ornament (of princes), a gracious demeanour.
en:
Since true benignity, that grace exceeding great, resides
In kingly souls, world in happy state abides.
ta:
கண்ணோட்டம் என்னும் கழிபெருங் காரிகை
  உண்மையான் உண்டிவ் வுலகு.

commentary: The prosperity of the world springs from the kindliness, the existence of those who have no (kindliness) is a burden to the earth.
en:
The world goes on its wonted way, since grace benign is there;
All other men are burthen for the earth to bear.
ta:
கண்ணோட்டத் துள்ளது உலகியல் அஃதிலார்
  உண்மை நிலக்குப் பொறை.

commentary: Of what avail is a song if it be inconsistent with harmony ? what is the use of eyes which possess no kindliness.
en:
Where not accordant with the song, what use of sounding chords?
What gain of eye that no benignant light affords?
ta:
பண்என்னாம் பாடற்கு இயைபின்றேல் கண்என்னாம்
  கண்ணோட்டம் இல்லாத கண்.

commentary: Beyond appearing to be in the face, what good do they do, those eyes in which is no well-regulated kindness ?
en:
The seeming eye of face gives no expressive light,
When not with duly meted kindness bright.
ta:
உளபோல் முகத்தெவன் செய்யும் அளவினால்
  கண்ணோட்டம் இல்லாத கண்.

commentary: Kind looks are the ornaments of the eyes; without these they will be considered (by the wise) to be merely two sores.
en:
Benignity is eyes' adorning grace;
Without it eyes are wounds disfiguring face.
ta:
கண்ணிற்கு அணிகலம் கண்ணோட்டம் அஃதின்றேல்
  புண்ணென்று உணரப் படும்

commentary: They resemble the trees of the earth, who although they have eyes, never look kindly (on others).
en:
Whose eyes 'neath brow infixed diffuse no ray
Of grace; like tree in earth infixed are they.
ta:
மண்ணோ டியைந்த மரத்தனையர் கண்ணோ
  டியைந்துகண் ணோடா தவர்.

commentary: Men without kind looks are men without eyes; those who (really) have eyes are also not devoid of kind looks.
en:
Eyeless are they whose eyes with no benignant lustre shine;
Who've eyes can never lack the light of grace benign.
ta:
கண்ணோட்டம் இல்லவர் கண்ணிலர் கண்ணுடையார்
  கண்ணோட்டம் இன்மையும் இல்.

commentary: The world is theirs (kings) who are able to show kindness, without injury to their affairs, (administration of justice).
en:
Who can benignant smile, yet leave no work undone;
By them as very own may all the earth be won.
ta:
கருமம் சிதையாமல் கண்ணோட வல்லார்க்கு
  உரிமை உடைத்திவ் வுலகு.

commentary: Patiently to bear with, and show kindness to those who grieve us, is the most excellent of all dispositions.
en:
To smile on those that vex, with kindly face,
Enduring long, is most excelling grace.
ta:
ஒறுத்தாற்றும் பண்பினார் கண்ணும்கண் ணோடிப்
  பொறுத்தாற்றும் பண்பே தலை.

commentary: Those who desire (to cultivate that degree of) urbanity which all shall love, even after swallowing the poison served to them by their friends, will be friendly with them.
en:
They drink with smiling grace, though poison interfused they see,
Who seek the praise of all-esteemed courtesy.
ta:
பெயக்கண்டும் நஞ்சுண் டமைவர் நயத்தக்க
  நாகரிகம் வேண்டு பவர்.

commentary: Let a king consider as his eyes these two things, a spy and a book (of laws) universally esteemed.
en:
These two: the code renowned and spies,
In these let king confide as eyes.
ta:
ஒற்றும் உரைசான்ற நூலும் இவையிரண்டும்
  தெற்றென்க மன்னவன் கண்.

commentary: It is the duty of a king to know quickly (by a spy) what all happens, daily, amongst all men.
en:
Each day, of every subject every deed,
'Tis duty of the king to learn with speed.
ta:
எல்லார்க்கும் எல்லாம் நிகழ்பவை எஞ்ஞான்றும்
  வல்லறிதல் வேந்தன் தொழில்.

commentary: There is no way for a king to obtain conquests, who knows not the advantage of discoveries made by a spy.
en:
By spies who spies, not weighing things they bring,
Nothing can victory give to that unwary king.
ta:
ஒற்றினான் ஒற்றிப் பொருள்தெரியா மன்னவன்
  கொற்றங் கொளக்கிடந்தது இல்.

commentary: He is a spy who watches all men, to wit, those who are in the king's employment, his relatives, and his enemies.
en:
His officers, his friends, his enemies,
All these who watch are trusty spies.
ta:
வினைசெய்வார் தம்சுற்றம் வேண்டாதார் என்றாங்கு
  அனைவரையும் ஆராய்வது ஒற்று.

commentary: A spy is one who is able to assume an appearance which may create no suspicion (in the minds of others), who fears no man's face, and who never reveals (his purpose).
en:
Of unsuspected mien and all-unfearing eyes,
Who let no secret out, are trusty spies.
ta:
கடாஅ உருவொடு கண்ணஞ்சாது யாண்டும்
  உகாஅமை வல்லதே ஒற்று.

commentary: He is a spy who, assuming the appearance of an ascetic, goes into (whatever place he wishes), examines into (all, that is needful), and never discovers himself, whatever may be done to him.
en:
As monk or devotee, through every hindrance making way,
A spy, whate'er men do, must watchful mind display.
ta:
துறந்தார் படிவத்த ராகி இறந்தாராய்ந்து
  என்செயினும் சோர்விலது ஒற்று.

commentary: A spy is one who is able to discover what is hidden and who retains no doubt concerning what he has known.
en:
A spy must search each hidden matter out,
And full report must render, free from doubt.
ta:
மறைந்தவை கேட்கவற் றாகி அறிந்தவை
  ஐயப்பாடு இல்லதே ஒற்று.

commentary: Let not a king receive the information which a spy has discovered and made known to him, until he has examined it by another spy.
en:
Spying by spies, the things they tell
To test by other spies is well.
ta:
ஒற்றொற்றித் தந்த பொருளையும் மற்றுமோர்
  ஒற்றினால் ஒற்றிக் கொளல்.

commentary: Let a king employ spies so that one may have no knowledge of the other; and when the information of three agrees together, let him receive it.
en:
One spy must not another see: contrive it so;
And things by three confirmed as truth you know.
ta:
ஒற்றெற் றுணராமை ஆள்க உடன்மூவர்
  சொற்றொக்க தேறப் படும்.

commentary: Let not a king publicly confer on a spy any marks of his favour; if he does, he will divulge his own secret.
en:
Reward not trusty spy in others' sight,
Or all the mystery will come to light.
ta:
சிறப்பறிய ஒற்ற஧ன்கண் செய்யற்க செய்யின்
  புறப்படுத்தான் ஆகும் மறை.

commentary: Energy makes out the man of property; as for those who are destitute of it, do they (really) possess what they possess ?
en:
'Tis energy gives men o'er that they own a true control;
They nothing own who own not energy of soul.
ta:
உடையர் எனப்படுவது ஊக்கம் அஃதில்லார்
  உடையது உடையரோ மற்று.

commentary: The possession of (energy of) mind is true property; the possession of wealth passes away and abides not.
en:
The wealth of mind man owns a real worth imparts,
Material wealth man owns endures not, utterly departs.
ta:
உள்ளம் உடைமை உடைமை பொருளுடைமை
  நில்லாது நீங்கி விடும்.

commentary: They who are possessed of enduring energy will not trouble themselves, saying, "we have lost our property."
en:
'Lost is our wealth,' they utter not this cry distressed,
The men of firm concentred energy of soul possessed.
ta:
க்கம் இழந்தேமென்று அல்லாவார் ஊக்கம்
  ஒருவந்தம் கைத்துடை யார்.

commentary: Wealth will find its own way to the man of unfailing energy.
en:
The man of energy of soul inflexible,
Good fortune seeks him out and comes a friend to dwell.
ta:
க்கம் அதர்வினாய்ச் செல்லும் அசைவிலா
  ஊக்க முடையா னுழை.

commentary: The stalks of water-flowers are proportionate to the depth of water; so is men's greatness proportionate to their minds.
en:
With rising flood the rising lotus flower its stem unwinds;
The dignity of men is measured by their minds.
ta:
வெள்ளத் தனைய மலர்நீட்டம் மாந்தர்தம்
  உள்ளத் தனையது உயர்வு.

commentary: In all that a king thinks of, let him think of his greatness; and if it should be thrust from him (by fate), it will have the nature of not being thrust from him.
en:
Whate'er you ponder, let your aim be loftly still,
Fate cannot hinder always, thwart you as it will.
ta:
உள்ளுவ தெல்லாம் உயர்வுள்ளல் மற்றது
  தள்ளினுந் தள்ளாமை நீர்த்து.

commentary: The strong minded will not faint, even when all is lost; the elephant stands firm, even when wounded by a shower of arrows.
en:
The men of lofty mind quail not in ruin's fateful hour,
The elephant retains his dignity mind arrows' deadly shower.
ta:
சிதைவிடத்து ஒல்கார் உரவோர் புதையம்பிற்
  பட்டுப்பா டூன்றுங் களிறு.

commentary: Those who have no (greatness of) mind, will not acquire the joy of saying in the world, "we have excercised liaberality".
en:
The soulless man can never gain
Th' ennobling sense of power with men.
ta:
உள்ளம் இலாதவர் எய்தார் உலகத்து
  வள்ளியம் என்னுஞ் செருக்கு.

commentary: Although the elephant has a large body, and a sharp tusk, yet it fears the attack of the tiger.
en:
Huge bulk of elephant with pointed tusk all armed,
When tiger threatens shrinks away alarmed!
ta:
பரியது கூர்ங்கோட்டது ஆயினும் யானை
  ஦வ்ருஉம் புலிதாக் குறின்.

commentary: Energy is mental wealth; those men who are destitute of it are only trees in the form of men.
en:
Firmness of soul in man is real excellance;
Others are trees, their human form a mere pretence.
ta:
உரமொருவற்கு உள்ள வெறுக்கைஅஃ தில்லார்
  மரம்மக்க ளாதலே வேறு.

commentary: By the darkness, of idleness, the indestructible lamp of family (rank) will be extinguished.
en:
Of household dignity the lustre beaming bright,
Flickers and dies when sluggish foulness dims its light.
ta:
குடியென்னும் குன்றா விளக்கம் மடியென்னும்
  மாசூர மாய்ந்து கெடும்.

commentary: Let those, who desire that their family may be illustrious, put away all idleness from their conduct.
en:
Let indolence, the death of effort, die,
If you'd uphold your household's dignity.
ta:
மடியை மடியா ஒழுகல் குடியைக்
  குடியாக வேண்டு பவர்.

commentary: The (lustre of the) family of the ignorant man, who acts under the influence of destructive laziness will perish, even before he is dead.
en:
Who fosters indolence within his breast, the silly elf!
The house from which he springs shall perish ere himself.
ta:
மடிமடிக் கொண்டொழுகும் பேதை பிறந்த
  குடிமடியும் தன்னினும் முந்து.

commentary: Family (greatness) will be destroyed, and faults will increase, in those men who give way to laziness, and put forth no dignified exertions.
en:
His family decays, and faults unheeded thrive,
Who, sunk in sloth, for noble objects doth not strive.
ta:
குடிமடிந்து குற்றம் பெருகும் மடிமடிந்து
  மாண்ட உஞற்றி லவர்க்கு.

commentary: Procrastination, forgetfulness, idleness, and sleep, these four things, form the vessel which is desired by those destined to destruction.
en:
Delay, oblivion, sloth, and sleep: these four
Are pleasure-boat to bear the doomed to ruin's shore.
ta:
நெடுநீர் மறவி மடிதுயில் நான்கும்
  கெடுநீரார் காமக் கலன்.

commentary: It is a rare thing for the idle, even when possessed of the riches of kings who ruled over the whole earth, to derive any great benefit from it.
en:
Though lords of earth unearned possessions gain,
The slothful ones no yield of good obtain.
ta:
படியுடையார் பற்றமைந்தக் கண்ணும் மடியுடையார்
  மாண்பயன் எய்தல் அரிது.

commentary: Those who through idleness, and do not engage themselves in dignified exertion, will subject themselves to rebukes and reproaches.
en:
Who hug their sloth, nor noble works attempt,
Shall bear reproofs and words of just contempt.
ta:
இடிபுரிந்து எள்ளுஞ்சொல் கேட்பர் மடிபுரிந்து
  மாண்ட உஞற்றி லவர்.

commentary: If idleness take up its abode in a king of high birth, it will make him a slave of his enemies.
en:
If sloth a dwelling find mid noble family,
Bondsmen to them that hate them shall they be.
ta:
மடிமை குடிமைக்கண் தங்கின்தன் ஒன்னார்க்கு
  அடிமை புகுத்தி விடும்.

commentary: When a man puts away idleness, the reproach which has come upon himself and his family will disappear.
en:
Who changes slothful habits saves
Himself from all that household rule depraves.
ta:
குடியாண்மை யுள்வந்த குற்றம் ஒருவன்
  மடியாண்மை மாற்றக் கெடும்.

commentary: The king who never gives way to idleness will obtain entire possession of (the whole earth) passed over by him who measured (the worlds) with His foot.
en:
The king whose life from sluggishness is rid,
Shall rule o'er all by foot of mighty god bestrid.
ta:
மடியிலா மன்னவன் எய்தும் அடியளந்தான்
  தாஅய தெல்லாம் ஒருங்கு.

commentary: Yield not to the feebleness which says, "this is too difficult to be done"; labour will give the greatness (of mind) which is necessary (to do it).
en:
Say not, 'Tis hard', in weak, desponding hour,
For strenuous effort gives prevailing power.
ta:
அருமை உடைத்தென்று அசாவாமை வேண்டும்
  பெருமை முயற்சி தரும்.

commentary: Take care not to give up exertion in the midst of a work; the world will abandon those who abandon their unfinished work.
en:
In action be thou, 'ware of act's defeat;
The world leaves those who work leave incomplete!
ta:
வினைக்கண் வினைகெடல் ஓம்பல் வினைக்குறை
  தீர்ந்தாரின் தீர்ந்தன்று உலகு.

commentary: The lustre of munificence will dwell only with the dignity of laboriousness or efforts.
en:
In strenuous effort doth reside
The power of helping others: noble pride!
ta:
தாளாண்மை என்னும் தகைமைக்கண் தங்கிற்றே
  வேளாண்மை என்னுஞ் செருக்கு.

commentary: The liberality of him, who does not labour, will fail, like the manliness of a hermaphrodite, who has a sword in its hand.
en:
Beneficent intent in men by whom no strenuous work is wrought,
Like battle-axe in sexless being's hand availeth nought.
ta:
தாளாண்மை இல்லாதான் வேளாண்மை பேடிகை
  வாளாண்மை போலக் கெடும்.

commentary: He who desires not pleasure, but desires labour, will be a pillar to sustain his relations, wiping away their sorrows.
en:
Whose heart delighteth not in pleasure, but in action finds delight,
He wipes away his kinsmen's grief and stands the pillar of their might.
ta:
இன்பம் விழையான் வினைவிழைவான் தன்கேளிர்
  துன்பம் துடைத்தூன்றும் தூண்.

commentary: Labour will produce wealth; idleness will bring poverty.
en:
Effort brings fortune's sure increase,
Its absence brings to nothingness.
ta:
முயற்சி திருவினை ஆக்கும் முயற்றின்மை
  இன்மை புகுத்தி விடும்.

commentary: They say that the black Mudevi (the goddess of adversity) dwells with laziness, and the Latchmi (the goddess of prosperity) dwells with the labour of the industrious.
en:
In sluggishness is seen misfortune's lurid form, the wise declare;
Where man unslothful toils, she of the lotus flower is there!
ta:
மடியுளாள் மாமுகடி என்ப மடியிலான்
  தாளுளான் தாமரையி னாள்.

commentary: Adverse fate is no disgrace to any one; to be without exertion and without knowing what should be known, is disgrace.
en:
'Tis no reproach unpropitious fate should ban;
But not to do man's work is foul disgrace to man!
ta:
பொறியின்மை யார்க்கும் பழியன்று அறிவறிந்து
  ஆள்வினை இன்மை பழி.

commentary: Although it be said that, through fate, it cannot be attained, yet labour, with bodily exertion, will yield its reward.
en:
Though fate-divine should make your labour vain;
Effort its labour's sure reward will gain.
ta:
தெய்வத்தான் ஆகா தெனினும் முயற்சிதன்
  மெய்வருத்தக் கூலி தரும்.

commentary: They who labour on, without fear and without fainting will see even fate (put) behind their back.
en:
Who strive with undismayed, unfaltering mind,
At length shall leave opposing fate behind.
ta:
ஊழையும் உப்பக்கம் காண்பர் உலைவின்றித்
  தாழாது உஞற்று பவர்.

commentary: If troubles come, laugh; there is nothing like that, to press upon and drive away sorrow.
en:
Smile, with patient, hopeful heart, in troublous hour;
Meet and so vanquish grief; nothing hath equal power.
ta:
இடுக்கண் வருங்கால் நகுக அதனை
  அடுத்தூர்வது அஃதொப்ப தில்.

commentary: A flood of troubles will be overcome by the (courageous) thought which the minds of the wise will entertain, even in sorrow.
en:
Though sorrow, like a flood, comes rolling on,
When wise men's mind regards it,- it is gone.
ta:
வெள்ளத் தனைய இடும்பை அறிவுடையான்
  உள்ளத்தின் உள்ளக் கெடும்.

commentary: They give sorrow to sorrow, who in sorrow do not suffer sorrow.
en:
Who griefs confront with meek, ungrieving heart,
From them griefs, put to grief, depart.
ta:
இடும்பைக்கு இடும்பை படுப்பர் இடும்பைக்கு
  இடும்பை படாஅ தவர்.

commentary: Troubles will vanish (i.e., will be troubled) before the man who (struggles against difficulties) as a buffalo (drawing a cart) through deep mire.
en:
Like bullock struggle on through each obstructed way;
From such an one will troubles, troubled, roll away.
ta:
மடுத்தவா யெல்லாம் பகடன்னான் உற்ற
  இடுக்கண் இடர்ப்பாடு உடைத்து.

commentary: The troubles of that man will be troubled (and disappear) who, however thickly they may come upon him, does not abandon (his purpose).
en:
When griefs press on, but fail to crush the patient heart,
Then griefs defeated, put to grief, depart.
ta:
அடுக்கி வரினும் அழிவிலான் உற்ற
  இடுக்கண் இடுக்கட் படும்.

commentary: Will those men ever cry out in sorrow, "we are destitute" who, (in their prosperity), give not way to (undue desire) to keep their wealth.
en:
Who boasted not of wealth, nor gave it all their heart,
Will not bemoan the loss, when prosperous days depart.
ta:
அற்றேமென்று அல்லற் படுபவோ பெற்றேமென்று
  ஓம்புதல் தேற்றா தவர்.

commentary: The great will not regard trouble as trouble, knowing that the body is the butt of trouble.
en:
'Man's frame is sorrow's target', the noble mind reflects,
Nor meets with troubled mind the sorrows it expects.
ta:
இலக்கம் உடம்பிடும்பைக் கென்று கலக்கத்தைக்
  கையாறாக் கொள்ளாதாம் மேல்.

commentary: That man never experiences sorrow, who does not seek for pleasure, and who considers distress to be natural (to man).
en:
He seeks not joy, to sorrow man is born, he knows;
Such man will walk unharmed by touch of human woes.
ta:
இன்பம் விழையான் இடும்பை இயல்பென்பான்
  துன்பம் உறுதல் இலன்.

commentary: He does not suffer sorrow, in sorrow who does not look for pleasure in pleasure.
en:
Mid joys he yields not heart to joys' control.
Mid sorrows, sorrow cannot touch his soul.
ta:
இன்பத்துள் இன்பம் விழையாதான் துன்பத்துள்
  துன்பம் உறுதல் இலன்.

commentary: The elevation, which even his enemies will esteem, will be gained by him, who regards pain as pleasure.
en:
Who pain as pleasure takes, he shall acquire
The bliss to which his foes in vain aspire.
ta:
இன்னாமை இன்பம் எனக்கொளின் ஆகுந்தன்
  ஒன்னார் விழையுஞ் சிறப்பு.

commentary: The minister is one who can make an excellent choice of means, time, manner of execution, and the difficult undertaking (itself).
en:
A minister is he who grasps, with wisdom large,
Means, time, work's mode, and functions rare he must discharge.
ta:
கருவியும் காலமும் செய்கையும் செய்யும்
  அருவினையும் மாண்டது அமைச்சு.

commentary: The minister is one who in addition to the aforesaid five things excels in the possession of firmness, protection of subjects, clearness by learning, and perseverance.
en:
A minister must greatness own of guardian power, determined mind,
Learn'd wisdom, manly effort with the former five combined.
ta:
வன்கண் குடிகாத்தல் கற்றறிதல் ஆள்வினையோடு
  ஐந்துடன் மாண்டது அமைச்சு.

commentary: The minister is one who can effect discord (among foes), maintain the good-will of his friends and restore to friendship those who have seceded (from him).
en:
A minister is he whose power can foes divide,
Attach more firmly friends, of severed ones can heal the breaches wide.
ta:
பிரித்தலும் பேணிக் கொளலும் பிரிந்தார்ப்
  பொருத்தலும் வல்ல தமைச்சு.

commentary: The minister is one who is able to comprehend (the whole nature of an undertaking), execute it in the best manner possible, and offer assuring advice (in time of necessity).
en:
A minister has power to see the methods help afford,
To ponder long, then utter calm conclusive word.
ta:
தெரிதலும் தேர்ந்து செயலும் ஒருதலையாச்
  சொல்லலும் வல்லது அமைச்சு.

commentary: He is the best helper (of the king) who understanding the duties, of the latter, is by his special learning, able to tender the fullest advice, and at all times conversant with the best method (of performing actions).
en:
The man who virtue knows, has use of wise and pleasant words.
With plans for every season apt, in counsel aid affords.
ta:
அறனறிந்து ஆன்றமைந்த சொல்லான்எஞ் ஞான்றுந்
  திறனறிந்தான் தேர்ச்சித் துணை.

commentary: What (contrivances) are there so acute as to resist those who possess natural acuteness in addition to learning ?.
en:
When native subtilty combines with sound scholastic lore,
'Tis subtilty surpassing all, which nothing stands before.
ta:
மதிநுட்பம் நூலோடு உடையார்க்கு அதிநுட்பம்
  யாவுள முன்நிற் பவை.

commentary: Though you are acquainted with the (theoretical) methods (of performing an act), understand the ways of the world and act accordingly.
en:
Though knowing all that books can teach, 'tis truest tact
To follow common sense of men in act.
ta:
செயற்கை அற஧ந்தக் கடைத்தும் உலகத்து
  இயற்கை அறிந்து செயல்.

commentary: Although the king be utterly ignorant, it is the duty of the minister to give (him) sound advice.
en:
'Tis duty of the man in place aloud to say
The very truth, though unwise king may cast his words away.
ta:
அறிகொன்று அறியான் எனினும் உறுதி
  உழையிருந்தான் கூறல் கடன்.

commentary: Far better are seventy crores of enemies (for a king) than a minister at his side who intends (his) ruin.
en:
A minister who by king's side plots evil things
Worse woes than countless foemen brings.
ta:
பழுதெண்ணும் மந்திரியின் பக்கததுள் தெவ்வோர்
  எழுபது கோடி உறும்.

commentary: Those ministers who are destitute of (executive) ability will fail to carry out their projects, although they may have contrived aright.
en:
For gain of end desired just counsel nought avails
To minister, when tact in execution fails.
ta:
முறைப்படச் சூழ்ந்தும் முடிவிலவே செய்வர்
  திறப்பாடு இலாஅ தவர்.

commentary: The possession of that goodness which is called the goodness of speech is (even to others) better than any other goodness.
en:
A tongue that rightly speaks the right is greatest gain,
It stands alone midst goodly things that men obtain.
ta:
நாநலம் என்னும் நலனுடைமை அந்நலம்
  யாநலத்து உள்ளதூஉம் அன்று.

commentary: Since (both) wealth and evil result from (their) speech, ministers should most carefully guard themselves against faultiness therein.
en:
Since gain and loss in life on speech depend,
From careless slip in speech thyself defend.
ta:
ஆக்கமுங் கேடும் அதனால் வருதலால்
  காத்தோம்பல் சொல்லின்கட் சோர்வு.

commentary: The (minister's) speech is that which seeks (to express) elements as bind his friends (to himself) and is so delivered as to make even his enemies desire (his friendship).
en:
'Tis speech that spell-bound holds the listening ear,
While those who have not heard desire to hear.
ta:
கேட்டார்ப் பிணிக்கும் தகையவாய்க் கேளாரும்
  வேட்ப மொழிவதாம் சொல்.

commentary: Understand the qualities (of your hearers) and (then) make your speech; for superior to it, there is neither virtue nor wealth.
en:
Speak words adapted well to various hearers' state;
No higher virtue lives, no gain more surely great.
ta:
திறனறிந்து சொல்லுக சொல்லை அறனும்
  பொருளும் அதனினூஉங்கு இல்.

commentary: Deliver your speech, after assuring yourself that no counter speech can defeat your own.
en:
Speak out your speech, when once 'tis past dispute
That none can utter speech that shall your speech refute.
ta:
சொல்லுக சொல்லைப் பிறிதோர்சொல் அச்சொல்லை
  வெல்லுஞ்சொல் இன்மை அறிந்து.

commentary: It is the opinion of those who are free from defects in diplomacy that the minister should speak so as to make his hearers desire (to hear more) and grasp the meaning of what he hears himself.
en:
Charming each hearer's ear, of others' words to seize the sense,
Is method wise of men of spotless excellence.
ta:
வேட்பத்தாஞ் சொல்லிப் பிறர்சொல் பயன்கோடல்
  மாட்சியின் மாசற்றார் கோள்.

commentary: It is impossible for any one to conquer him by intrique who possesses power of speech, and is neither faulty nor timid.
en:
Mighty in word, of unforgetful mind, of fearless speech,
'Tis hard for hostile power such man to overreach.
ta:
சொலல்வல்லன் சோர்விலன் அஞ்சான் அவனை
  இகல்வெல்லல் யார்க்கும் அரிது.

commentary: If there be those who can speak on various subjects in their proper order and in a pleasing manner, the world would readily accept them.
en:
Swiftly the listening world will gather round,
When men of mighty speech the weighty theme propound.
ta:
விரைந்து தொழில்கேட்கும் ஞாலம் நிரந்தினிது
  சொல்லுதல் வல்லார்ப் பெறின்.

commentary: They will desire to utter many words, who do not know how to speak a few faultless ones.
en:
Who have not skill ten faultless words to utter plain,
Their tongues will itch with thousand words man's ears to pain.
ta:
பலசொல்லக் காமுறுவர் மன்றமா சற்ற
  சிலசொல்லல் தேற்றா தவர்.

commentary: Those who are unable to set forth their acquirements (before others) are like flowers blossoming in a cluster and yet without fragrance.
en:
Like scentless flower in blooming garland bound
Are men who can't their lore acquired to other's ears expound.
ta:
இண்ருழ்த்தும் நாறா மலரனையர் கற்றது
  உணர விரித்துரையா தார்.

commentary: The efficacy of support will yield (only) wealth; (but) the efficacy of action will yield all that is desired.
en:
The good external help confers is worldly gain;
By action good men every needed gift obtain.
ta:
துணைநலம் ஆக்கம் த்ருஉம் வினைநலம்
  வேண்டிய எல்லாந் தரும்.

commentary: Ministers should at all times avoid acts which, in addition to fame, yield no benefit (for the future).
en:
From action evermore thyself restrain
Of glory and of good that yields no gain.
ta:
என்றும் ஒருவுதல் வேண்டும் புகழொடு
  நன்றி பயவா வினை.

commentary: Those who say, "we will become (better)" should avoid the performance of acts that would destroy (their fame).
en:
Who tell themselves that nobler things shall yet be won
All deeds that dim the light of glory must they shun.
ta:
ஒஓதல் வேண்டும் ஒளிமாழ்கும் செய்வினை
  ஆஅதும் என்னு மவர்.

commentary: Those who have infallible judgement though threatened with peril will not do acts which have brought disgrace (on former ministers).
en:
Though troubles press, no shameful deed they do,
Whose eyes the ever-during vision view.
ta:
இடுக்கண் படினும் இளிவந்த செய்யார்
  நடுக்கற்ற காட்சி யவர்.

commentary: Let a minister never do acts of which he would have to grieve saying, "what is this I have done"; (but) should he do (them), it were good that he grieved not.
en:
Do nought that soul repenting must deplore,
If thou hast sinned, 'tis well if thou dost sin no more.
ta:
எற்றென்று இரங்குவ செய்யற்க செய்வானேல்
  மற்றன்ன செய்யாமை நன்று.

commentary: Though a minister may see his mother starve; let him do not act which the wise would (treat with contempt).
en:
Though her that bore thee hung'ring thou behold, no deed
Do thou, that men of perfect soul have crime decreed.
ta:
ஈன்றாள் பசிகாண்பான் ஆயினுஞ் செய்யற்க
  சான்றோர் பழிக்கும் வினை.

commentary: Far more excellent is the extreme poverty of the wise than wealth obtained by heaping up of sinful deeds.
en:
Than store of wealth guilt-laden souls obtain,
The sorest poverty of perfect soul is richer gain.
ta:
பழிமலைந்து எய்திய ஆக்கத்தின் சான்றோர்
  கழிநல் குரவே தலை.

commentary: The actions of those, who have not desisted from doing deeds forbidden (by the great), will, even if they succeed, cause them sorrow.
en:
To those who hate reproof and do forbidden thing.
What prospers now, in after days shall anguish bring.
ta:
கடிந்த கடிந்தொரார் செய்தார்க்கு அவைதாம்
  முடிந்தாலும் பீழை தரும்.

commentary: All that has been obtained with tears (to the victim) will depart with tears (to himself); but what has been by fair means; though with loss at first, will afterwards yield fruit.
en:
What's gained through tears with tears shall go;
From loss good deeds entail harvests of blessings grow.
ta:
அழக்கொண்ட எல்லாம் அழப்போம் இழப்பினும்
  பிற்பயக்கும் நற்பா லவை.

commentary: (For a minister) to protect (his king) with wealth obtained by foul means is like preserving a vessel of wet clay by filling it with water.
en:
In pot of clay unburnt he water pours and would retain,
Who seeks by wrong the realm in wealth and safety to maintain.
ta:
சலத்தால் பொருள்செய்தே மார்த்தல் பசுமண்
  கலத்துள்நீர் பெய்திரீஇ யற்று.

commentary: Firmness in action is (simply) one's firmness of mind; all other (abilities) are not of this nature.
en:
What men call 'power in action' know for 'power of mind'
Externe to man all other aids you find.
ta:
வினைத்திட்பம் என்பது ஒருவன் மனத்திட்பம்
  மற்றைய எல்லாம் பிற.

commentary: Not to perform a ruinous act, and not to be discouraged by the ruinous termination of an act, are the two maxims which, the wise say, from the principles of those who have investigated the subject.
en:
'Each hindrance shun', 'unyielding onward press, If obstacle be there,'
These two define your way, so those that search out truth declare.
ta:
ஊறொரால் உற்றபின் ஒல்காமை இவ்விரண்டின்
  ஆறென்பர் ஆய்ந்தவர் கோள்.

commentary: So to perform an act as to publish it (only) at its termination is (true) manliness; for to announce it beforehand, will cause irremediable sorrow.
en:
Man's fitting work is known but by success achieved;
In midst the plan revealed brings ruin ne'er to be retrieved.
ta:
கடைக்கொட்கச் செய்தக்க தாண்மை இடைக்கொட்கின்
  எற்றா விழுமந் தரும்.

commentary: To say (how an act is to be performed) is (indeed) easy for any one; but far difficult it is to do according to what has been said.
en:
Easy to every man the speech that shows the way;
Hard thing to shape one's life by words they say!
ta:
சொல்லுதல் யார்க்கும் எளிய அரியவாம்
  சொல்லிய வண்ணம் செயல்.

commentary: The firmness in action of those who have become great by the excellence (of their counsel) will, by attaining its fulfilment in the person of the king, be esteemed (by all).
en:
The power in act of men renowned and great,
With king acceptance finds and fame through all the state.
ta:
வீறெய்தி மாண்டார் வினைத்திட்பம் வேந்தன்கண்
  ஊறெய்தி உள்ளப் படும்.

commentary: If those who have planned (an undertaking) possess firmness (in executing it) they will obtain what they have desired even as they have desired it.
en:
Whate'er men think, ev'n as they think, may men obtain,
If those who think can steadfastness of will retain.
ta:
எண்ணிய எண்ணியாங்கு எய்து எண்ணியார்
  திண்ணியர் ஆகப் பெறின்.

commentary: Let none be despised for (their) size; (for) the world has those who resemble the linch-pin of the big rolling car.
en:
Despise not men of modest bearing; Look not at form, but what men are:
For some there live, high functions sharing, Like linch-pin of the mighty car!
ta:
உருவுகண்டு எள்ளாமை வேண்டும் உருள்பெருந்தேர்க்கு
  அச்சாணி அன்னார் உடைத்து.

commentary: An act that has been firmly resolved on must be as firmly carried out without delay.
en:
What clearly eye discerns as right, with steadfast will,
And mind unslumbering, that should man fulfil.
ta:
கலங்காது கண்ட வினைக்கண் துளங்காது
  தூக்கங் கடிந்து செயல்.

commentary: Though it should cause increasing sorrow (at the outset), do with firmness the act that yield bliss (in the end).
en:
Though toil and trouble face thee, firm resolve hold fast,
And do the deeds that pleasure yield at last.
ta:
துன்பம் உறவரினும் செய்க துணிவாற்றி
  இன்பம் பயக்கும் வினை.

commentary: The great will not esteem those who esteem not firmness of action, whatever other abilities the latter may possess.
en:
The world desires not men of every power possessed,
Who power in act desire not,- crown of all the rest.
ta:
எனைத்திட்பம் எய்தியக் கண்ணும் வினைத்திட்பம்
  வேண்டாரை வேண்டாது உலகு.

commentary: Consultation ends in forming a resolution (to act); (but) delay in the execution of that resolve is an evil.
en:
Resolve is counsel's end, If resolutions halt
In weak delays, still unfulfilled, 'tis grievous fault.
ta:
சூழ்ச்சி முடிவு துணிவெய்தல் அத்துணிவு
  தாழ்ச்சியுள் தங்குதல் தீது.

commentary: Sleep over such (actions) as may be slept over; (but) never over such as may not be slept over.
en:
Slumber when sleepy work's in hand: beware
Thou slumber not when action calls for sleepless care!
ta:
தூங்குக தூங்கிச் செயற்பால தூங்கற்க
  தூங்காது செய்யும் வினை.

commentary: Whenever it is possible (to overcome your enemy) the act (of fighting) is certainly good; if not, endeavour to employ some more successful method.
en:
When way is clear, prompt let your action be;
When not, watch till some open path you see.
ta:
ஙல்லும்வா யெல்லாம் வினைநன்றே ஒல்லாக்கால்
  செல்லும்வாய் நோக்கிச் செயல்.

commentary: When duly considered, the incomplete execution of an undertaking and hostility will grow and destroy one like the (unextinguished) remnant of a fire.
en:
With work or foe, when you neglect some little thing,
If you reflect, like smouldering fire, 'twill ruin bring.
ta:
வினைபகை என்றிரண்டின் எச்சம் நினையுங்கால்
  தீயெச்சம் போலத் தெறும்.

commentary: Do an act after a due consideration of the (following) five, viz. money, means, time, execution and place.
en:
Treasure and instrument and time and deed and place of act:
These five, till every doubt remove, think o'er with care exact.
ta:
பொருள்கருவி காலம் வினையிடனொடு ஐந்தும்
  இருள்தீர எண்ணிச் செயல்.

commentary: An act is to be performed after considering the exertion required, the obstacles to be encountered, and the great profit to be gained (on its completion).
en:
Accomplishment, the hindrances, large profits won
By effort: these compare,- then let the work be done.
ta:
முடிவும் இடையூறும் முற்றியாங்கு எய்தும்
  படுபயனும் பார்த்துச் செயல்.

commentary: The method of performance for one who has begun an act is to ascertain the mind of him who knows the secret thereof.
en:
Who would succeed must thus begin: first let him ask
The thoughts of them who thoroughly know the task.
ta:
செய்வினை செய்வான் செயன்முறை அவ்வினை
  உள்ளறிவான் உள்ளம் கொளல்.

commentary: To make one undertaking the means of accomplishing another (similar to it) is like making one rutting elephant the means of capturing another.
en:
By one thing done you reach a second work's accomplishment;
So furious elephant to snare its fellow brute is sent.
ta:
வினையான் வினையாக்கிக் கோடல் நனைகவுள்
  யானையால் யானையாத் தற்று.

commentary: One should rather hasten to secure the alliance of the foes (of one's foes) than perform good offices to one's friends.
en:
Than kindly acts to friends more urgent thing to do,
Is making foes to cling as friends attached to you.
ta:
நட்டார்க்கு நல்ல செயலின் விரைந்ததே
  ஒட்டாரை ஒட்டிக் கொளல்.

commentary: Ministers of small states, afraid of their people being frightened, will yield to and acknowledge their superior foes, if the latter offer them a chance of reconciliation.
en:
The men of lesser realm, fearing the people's inward dread,
Accepting granted terms, to mightier ruler bow the head.
ta:
உறைசிறியார் உள்நடுங்கல் அஞ்சிக் குறைபெறின்
  கொள்வர் பெரியார்ப் பணிந்து.

commentary: The qualification of an ambassador are affection (for his relations) a fitting birth, and the possession of attributes pleasing to royalty.
en:
Benevolence high birth, the courtesy kings love:-
These qualities the envoy of a king approve.
ta:
அன்புடைமை ஆன்ற குடிப்பிறத்தல் வேந்தவாம்
  பண்புடைமை தூதுரைப்பான் பண்பு.

commentary: Love (to his sovereign), knowledge (of his affairs), and a discriminating power of speech (before other sovereigns) are the three sine qua non qualifications of an ambassador.
en:
Love, knowledge, power of chosen words, three things,
Should he possess who speaks the words of kings.
ta:
அன்பறிவு ஆராய்ந்த சொல்வன்மை தூதுரைப்பார்க்கு
  இன்றி யமையாத மூன்று.

commentary: To be powerful in politics among those who are learned (in ethics) is the character of him who speaks to lance-bearing kings on matters of triumph (to his own sovereign).
en:
Mighty in lore amongst the learned must he be,
Midst jav'lin-bearing kings who speaks the words of victory.
ta:
நூலாருள் நூல்வல்லன் ஆகுதல் வேலாருள்
  வென்றி வினையுரைப்பான் பண்பு.

commentary: He may go on a mission (to foreign rulers) who has combined in him all these three. viz., (natural) sense, an attractive bearing and well-tried learning.
en:
Sense, goodly grace, and knowledge exquisite.
Who hath these three for envoy's task is fit.
ta:
அறிவுரு வாராய்ந்த கல்விஇம் மூன்றன்
  செறிவுடையான் செல்க வினைக்கு.

commentary: He is an ambassador who (in the presence of foreign rulers) speaks briefly, avoids harshness, talks so as to make them smile, and thus brings good (to his own sovereign).
en:
In terms concise, avoiding wrathful speech, who utters pleasant word,
An envoy he who gains advantage for his lord.
ta:
தொகச்சொல்லித் தூவாத நீக்கி நகச்சொல்லி
  நன்றி பயப்பதாந் தூது.

commentary: He is an ambassador who having studied (politics) talks impressively, is not afraid of angry looks, and knows (to employ) the art suited to the time.
en:
An envoy meet is he, well-learned, of fearless eye
Who speaks right home, prepared for each emergency.
ta:
கற்றுக்கண் அஞ்சான் செலச்சொல்லிக் காலத்தால்
  தக்கது அறிவதாம் தூது.

commentary: He is chief (among ambassadors) who understands the proper decorum (before foreign princes), seeks the (proper) occasion, knows the (most suitable) place, and delivers his message after (due) consideration.
en:
He is the best who knows what's due, the time considered well,
The place selects, then ponders long ere he his errand tell.
ta:
கடனறிந்து காலங் கருதி இடனறிந்து
  எண்ணி உரைப்பான் தலை.

commentary: The qualifications of him who faithfully delivers his (sovereign's) message are purity, the support (of foreign ministers), and boldness, with truthfulness in addition to the (aforesaid) three.
en:
Integrity, resources, soul determined, truthfulness.
Who rightly speaks his message must these marks possess.
ta:
தூய்மை துணைமை துணிவுடைமை இம்மூன்றின்
  வாய்மை வழியுரைப்பான் பண்பு.

commentary: He alone is fit to communicate (his sovereign's) reply, who possesses the firmness not to utter even inadvertently what may reflect discredit (on the latter).
en:
His faltering lips must utter no unworthy thing,
Who stands, with steady eye, to speak the mandates of his king.
ta:
விடுமாற்றம் வேந்தர்க்கு உரைப்பான் வடுமாற்றம்
  வாய்சேரா வன்கணவன்.

commentary: He is the ambassador who fearlessly seeks his sovereign's good though it should cost him his life (to deliver his message).
en:
Death to the faithful one his embassy may bring;
To envoy gains assured advantage for his king.
ta:
இறுதி பயப்பினும் எஞ்சாது இறைவற்கு
  உறுதி பயப்பதாம் தூது.

commentary: Ministers who serve under fickle-minded monarchs should, like those who warm themselves at the fire, be neither (too) far, nor (too) near.
en:
Who warm them at the fire draw not too near, nor keep too much aloof;
Thus let them act who dwell beneath of warlike kings the palace-roof.
ta:
அகலாது அணுகாது தீக்காய்வார் போல்க
  இகல்வேந்தர்ச் சேர்ந்தொழுகு வார்.

commentary: For ministers not to cover the things desired by their kings will through the kings themselves yield them everlasting wealth.
en:
To those who prize not state that kings are wont to prize,
The king himself abundant wealth supplies.
ta:
மன்னர் விழைப விழையாமை மன்னரால்
  மன்னிய ஆக்கந் தரும்.

commentary: Ministers who would save themselves should avoid (the commission of) serious errors for if the king's suspicion is once roused, no one can remove it.
en:
Who would walk warily, let him of greater faults beware;
To clear suspicions once aroused is an achievement rare.
ta:
போற்றின் அரியவை போற்றல் கடுத்தபின்
  தேற்றுதல் யார்க்கும் அரிது.

commentary: While in the presence of the sovereign, ministers should neither whisper to nor smile at others.
en:
All whispered words and interchange of smiles repress,
In presence of the men who kingly power possess.
ta:
செவிச்சொல்லும் சேர்ந்த நகையும் அவித்தொழுகல்
  ஆன்ற பெரியா ரகத்து.

commentary: (When the king is engaged) in secret counsel (with others), ministers should neither over-hear anything whatever nor pry into it with inquisitive questions, but (wait to) listen when it is divulged (by the king himself).
en:
Seek not, ask not, the secret of the king to hear;
But if he lets the matter forth, give ear!
ta:
எப்பொருளும் ஓரார் தொடரார்மற் றப்பொருளை
  விட்டக்கால் கேட்க மறை.

commentary: Knowing the (king's disposition and seeking the right time, (the minister) should in a pleasing manner suggest things such as are desirable and not disagreeable.
en:
Knowing the signs, waiting for fitting time, with courteous care,
Things not displeasing, needful things, declare.
ta:
குறிப்பறிந்து காலங் கருதி வெறுப்பில
  வேண்டுப வேட்பச் சொலல்.

commentary: Ministers should (always) give agreeable advice but on no occasion recommend useless actions, though requested (to do so).
en:
Speak pleasant things, but never utter idle word;
Not though by monarch's ears with pleasure heard.
ta:
வேட்பன சொல்லி வினையில எஞ்ஞான்றும்
  கேட்பினும் சொல்லா விடல்.

commentary: Ministers should behave in accordance with the (Divine) light in the person of kings and not despise them saying, "He is our junior (in age) and connected with our family!".
en:
Say not, 'He's young, my kinsman,' despising thus your king;
But reverence the glory kingly state doth bring.
ta:
இளையர் இனமுறையர் என்றிகழார் நின்ற
  ஒளியோடு ஒழுகப் படும்.

commentary: Those whose judgement is firm will not do what is disagreeable (to the sovereign) saying (within themselves) "We are esteemed by the king".
en:
'We've gained his grace, boots nought what graceless acts we do',
So deem not sages who the changeless vision view.
ta:
கொளப்பட்டேம் என்றெண்ணிக் கொள்ளாத செய்யார்
  துளக்கற்ற காட்சி யவர்.

commentary: The (foolish) claim with which a minister does unbecoming acts because of his (long) familiarity (with the king) will ensure his ruin.
en:
Who think 'We're ancient friends' and do unseemly things;
To these familiarity sure ruin brings.
ta:
பழையம் எனக்கருதிப் பண்பல்ல செய்யும்
  கெழுதகைமை கேடு தரும்.

commentary: The minister who by looking (at the king) understands his mind without being told (of it), will be a perpetual ornament to the world which is surrounded by a never-drying sea.
en:
Who knows the sign, and reads unuttered thought, the gem is he,
Of earth round traversed by the changeless sea.
ta:
கூறாமை நோக்க஧க் குறிப்பறிவான் எஞ்ஞான்றும்
  மாறாநீர் வையக் கணி.

commentary: He is to be esteemed a god who is able to ascertain without a doubt what is within (one's mind).
en:
Undoubting, who the minds of men can scan,
As deity regard that gifted man.
ta:
ஐயப் படாஅது அகத்தது உணர்வானைத்
  தெய்வத்தோ டொப்பக் கொளல்.

commentary: The king should ever give whatever (is asked) of his belongings and secure him who, by the indications (of his own mind) is able to read those of another.
en:
Who by the sign the signs interprets plain,
Give any member up his aid to gain.
ta:
குறிப்பிற் குறிப்புணர் வாரை உறுப்பினுள்
  யாது கொடுத்தும் கொளல்.

commentary: Those who understand one's thoughts without being informed (thereof) and those who do not, may (indeed) resemble one another bodily; still are they different (mentally).
en:
Who reads what's shown by signs, though words unspoken be,
In form may seem as other men, in function nobler far is he.
ta:
குறித்தது கூறாமைக் கொள்வாரோ டேனை
  உறுப்போ ரனையரால் வேறு.

commentary: Of what use are the eyes amongst one's members, if they cannot by their own indications dive those of another ?.
en:
By sign who knows not sings to comprehend, what gain,
'Mid all his members, from his eyes does he obtain?
ta:
குறிப்பிற் குறிப்புணரா வாயின் உறுப்பினுள்
  என்ன பயத்தவோ கண்.

commentary: As the mirror reflects what is near so does the face show what is uppermost in the mind.
en:
As forms around in crystal mirrored clear we find,
The face will show what's throbbing in the mind.
ta:
அடுத்தது காட்டும் பளிங்குபோல் நெஞ்சம்
  கடுத்தது காட்டும் முகம்.

commentary: Is there anything so full of knowledge as the face ? (No.) it precedes the mind, whether (the latter is) pleased or vexed.
en:
Than speaking countenance hath aught more prescient skill?
Rejoice or burn with rage, 'tis the first herald still!
ta:
முகத்தின் முதுக்குறைந்தது உண்டோ உவப்பினும்
  காயினும் தான்முந் துறும்.

commentary: If the king gets those who by looking into his mind can understand (and remove) what has occurred (to him) it is enough that he stand looking at their face.
en:
To see the face is quite enough, in presence brought,
When men can look within and know the lurking thought.
ta:
முகம்நோக்கி நிற்க அமையும் அகம்நோக்கி
  உற்ற துணர்வார்ப் பெறின்.

commentary: If a king gets ministers who can read the movements of the eye, the eyes (of foreign kings) will (themselves) reveal (to him) their hatred or friendship.
en:
The eye speaks out the hate or friendly soul of man;
To those who know the eye's swift varying moods to scan.
ta:
பகைமையும் கேண்மையும் கண்ணுரைக்கும் கண்ணின்
  வகைமை உணர்வார்ப் பெறின்.

commentary: The measuring-rod of those (ministers) who say "we are acute" will on inquiry be found to be their (own) eyes and nothing else.
en:
The men of keen discerning soul no other test apply
(When you their secret ask) than man's revealing eye.
ta:
நுண்ணியம் என்பார் அளக்குங்கோல் காணுங்கால்
  கண்ணல்லது இல்லை பிற.

commentary: Let the pure who know the arrangement of words speak with deliberation after ascertaining (the nature of) the court (then assembled).
en:
Men pure in heart, who know of words the varied force,
Should to their audience known adapt their well-arranged discourse.
ta:
அவையறிநது ஆராய்ந்து சொல்லுக சொல்லின்
  தொகையறிந்த தூய்மை யவர்.

commentary: Let the good who know the uses of words speak with a clear knowledge after ascertaining the time (suited to the court).
en:
Good men to whom the arts of eloquence are known,
Should seek occasion meet, and say what well they've made their own.
ta:
இடைதெரிந்து நன்குணர்ந்து சொல்லுக சொல்லின்
  நடைதெரிந்த நன்மை யவர்.

commentary: Those who undertake to speak without knowing the (nature of the) court are ignorant of the quality of words as well as devoid of the power (of learning).
en:
Unversed in councils, who essays to speak.
Knows not the way of suasive words,- and all is weak.
ta:
அவையறியார் சொல்லல்மேற் கொள்பவர் சொல்லின்
  வகையறியார் வல்லதூஉம் இல்.

commentary: Ministers should be lights in the assembly of the enlightned, but assume the pure whiteness of mortar (ignorance) in that of fools.
en:
Before the bright ones shine as doth the light!
Before the dull ones be as purest stucco white!
ta:
ஒளியார்முன் ஒள்ளிய ராதல் வெளியார்முன்
  வான்சுதை வண்ணம் கொளல்.

commentary: The modesty by which one does not rush forward and speak in (an assembly of) superiors is the best among all (one's) good qualities.
en:
Midst all good things the best is modest grace,
That speaks not first before the elders' face.
ta:
நன்றென்ற வற்றுள்ளும் நன்றே முதுவருள்
  முந்து கிளவாச் செறிவு.

commentary: (For a minister) to blunder in the presence of those who have acquired a vast store of learning and know (the value thereof) is like a good man stumbling (and falling away) from the path (of virtue).
en:
As in the way one tottering falls, is slip before
The men whose minds are filled with varied lore.
ta:
ஆற்றின் நிலைதளர்ந் தற்றே வியன்புலம்
  ஏற்றுணர்வார் முன்னர் இழுக்கு.

commentary: The learning of those who have read and understood (much) will shine in the assembly of those who faultlessly examine (the nature of) words.
en:
The learning of the learned sage shines bright
To those whose faultless skill can value it aright.
ta:
கற்றறிந்தார் கல்வி விளங்கும் கசடறச்
  சொல்தெரிதல் வல்லார் அகத்து.

commentary: Lecturing to those who have the ability to understand (for themselves) is like watering a bed of plants that are growing (of themselves).
en:
To speak where understanding hearers you obtain,
Is sprinkling water on the fields of growing grain!
ta:
உணர்வ துடையார்முன் சொல்லல் வளர்வதன்
  பாத்தியுள் நீர்சொரிந் தற்று.

commentary: Those who are able to speak good things impressively in an assembly of the good should not even forgetfully speak them in that of the low.
en:
In councils of the good, who speak good things with penetrating power,
In councils of the mean, let them say nought, e'en in oblivious hour.
ta:
புல்லவையுள் பொச்சாந்தும் சொல்லற்க நல்லவையுள்
  நன்குசலச் சொல்லு வார்.

commentary: To utter (a good word) in the assembly of those who are of inferior rank is like dropping nectar on the ground.
en:
Ambrosia in the sewer spilt, is word
Spoken in presence of the alien herd.
ta:
அங்கணத்துள் உக்க அமிழ்தற்றால் தங்கணத்தார்
  அல்லார்முன் கோட்டி கொளல்.

commentary: The pure who know the classification of words having first ascertained the nature (of the court) will not (through fear) falter in their speech before the powerful body.
en:
Men, pure in heart, who know of words the varied force,
The mighty council's moods discern, nor fail in their discourse.
ta:
வகையறிந்து வல்லவை வாய்சோரார் சொல்லின்
  தொகையறிந்த தூய்மை யவர்.

commentary: Those who can agreeably set forth their acquirements before the learned will be regarded as the most learned among the learned.
en:
Who what they've learned, in penetrating words heve learned to say,
Before the learn'd among the learn'd most learn'd are they.
ta:
கற்றாருள் கற்றார் எனப்படுவர் கற்றார்முன்
  கற்ற செலச்சொல்லு வார்.

commentary: Many indeed may (fearlessly) die in the presence of (their) foes; (but) few are those who are fearless in the assembly (of the learned).
en:
Many encountering death in face of foe will hold their ground;
Who speak undaunted in the council hall are rarely found.
ta:
பகையகத்துச் சாவார் எளியர் அரியர்
  அவையகத்து அஞ்சா தவர்.

commentary: (Ministers) should agreeably set forth their acquirements before the learned and acquire more (knowledge) from their superiors (in learning).
en:
What you have learned, in penetrating words speak out before
The learn'd; but learn what men more learn'd can teach you more.
ta:
கற்றார்முன் கற்ற செலச்சொல்லித் தாம்கற்ற
  மிக்காருள் மிக்க கொளல்.

commentary: In order to reply fearlessly before a foreign court, (ministers) should learn logic according to the rules (of grammar).
en:
By rule, to dialectic art your mind apply,
That in the council fearless you may make an apt reply.
ta:
ஆற்றின் அளவறிந்து கற்க அவையஞ்சா
  மாற்றங் கொடுத்தற் பொருட்டு.

commentary: What have they to do with a sword who are not valiant, or they with learning who are afraid of an intelligent assembly ?
en:
To those who lack the hero's eye what can the sword avail?
Or science what, to those before the council keen who quail?
ta:
வாளொடென் வன்கண்ணர் அல்லார்க்கு நூலொடென்
  நுண்ணவை அஞ்சு பவர்க்கு.

commentary: The learning of him who is diffident before an assembly is like the shining sword of an hermaphrodite in the presence of his foes.
en:
As shining sword before the foe which 'sexless being' bears,
Is science learned by him the council's face who fears.
ta:
பகையகத்துப் பேடிகை ஒள்வாள் அவையகத்து
  அஞ்சு மவன்கற்ற நூல்.

commentary: Those who cannot agreeably speak good things before a good assembly are indeed unprofitable persons inspite of all their various acquirements.
en:
Though many things they've learned, yet useless are they all,
To man who cannot well and strongly speak in council hall.
ta:
பல்லவை கற்றும் பயமிலரே நல்லவையுள்
  நன்கு செலச்சொல்லா தார்.

commentary: They who, though they have learned and understood, are yet afraid of the assembly of the good, are said to be inferior (even) to the illiterate.
en:
Who, though they've learned, before the council of the good men quake,
Than men unlearn'd a lower place must take.
ta:
கல்லா தவரின் கடையென்ப கற்றறிந்தும்
  நல்லா ரவையஞ்சு வார்.

commentary: Those who through fear of the assembly are unable to set forth their learning in an interesting manner, though alive, are yet like the dead.
en:
Who what they've learned, in penetrating words know not to say,
The council fearing, though they live, as dead are they.
ta:
உளரெனினும் இல்லாரொடு ஒப்பர் களன்அஞ்சிக்
  கற்ற செலச்சொல்லா தார்.

commentary: A kingdom is that in which (those who carry on) a complete cultivation, virtuous persons, and merchants with inexhaustible wealth, dwell together.
en:
Where spreads fertility unfailing, where resides a band,
Of virtuous men, and those of ample wealth, call that a 'land'
ta:
தள்ளா விளையுளும் தக்காரும் தாழ்விலாச்
  செல்வரும் சேர்வது நாடு.

commentary: A kingdom is that which is desire for its immense wealth, and which grows greatly in prosperity, being free from destructive causes.
en:
That is a 'land' which men desire for wealth's abundant share,
Yielding rich increase, where calamities are rare.
ta:
பெரும்பொருளால் பெட்டக்க தாகி அருங்கேட்டால்
  ஆற்ற விளைவது நாடு.

commentary: A kingdom is that which can bear any burden that may be pressed on it (from adjoining kingdoms) and (yet) pay the full tribute to its sovereign.
en:
When burthens press, it bears; Yet, With unfailing hand
To king due tribute pays: that is the 'land'
ta:
பொறையொருங்கு மேல்வருங்கால் தாங்கி இறைவற்கு
  இறையொருங்கு நேர்வது நாடு.

commentary: A kingdom is that which continues to be free from excessive starvation, irremediable epidemics, and destructive foes.
en:
That is a 'land' whose peaceful annals know,
Nor famine fierce, nor wasting plague, nor ravage of the foe.
ta:
உறுபசியும் ஓவாப் பிணியும் செறுபகையும்
  சேரா தியல்வது நாடு.

commentary: A kingdom is that which is without various (irregular) associations, destructive internal enemies, and murderous savages who (sometimes) harass the sovereign.
en:
From factions free, and desolating civil strife, and band
Of lurking murderers that king afflict, that is the 'land'.
ta:
பல்குழுவும் பாழ்செய்யும் உட்பகையும் வேந்தலைக்கும்
  கொல்குறும்பும் இல்லத

commentary: The learned say that the best kingdom is that which knows no evil (from its foes), and, if injured (at all), suffers no diminution in its fruitfulness.
en:
Chief of all lands is that, where nought disturbs its peace;
Or, if invaders come, still yields its rich increase.
ta:
கேடறியாக் கெட்ட இடத்தும் வளங்குன்றா
  நாடென்ப நாட்டின் தலை.

commentary: The constituents of a kingdom are the two waters (from above and below), well situated hills and an undestructible fort.
en:
Waters from rains and springs, a mountain near, and waters thence;
These make a land, with fortress' sure defence.
ta:
இருபுனலும் வாய்ந்த மலையும் வருபுனலும்
  வல்லரணும் நாட்டிற்கு உறுப்பு.

commentary: Freedom from epidemics, wealth, produce, happiness and protection (to subjects); these five, the learned, say, are the ornaments of a kingdom.
en:
A country's jewels are these five: unfailing health,
Fertility, and joy, a sure defence, and wealth.
ta:
பிணியின்மை செல்வம் விளைவின்பம் ஏமம்
  அணியென்ப நாட்டிவ் வைந்து.

commentary: The learned say that those are kingdom whose wealth is not laboured for, and those not, whose wealth is only obtained through labour.
en:
That is a land that yields increase unsought,
That is no land whose gifts with toil are bought.
ta:
நாடென்ப நாடா வளத்தன நாடல்ல
  நாட வளந்தரு நாடு.

commentary: Although in possession of all the above mentioned excellences, these are indeed of no use to a country, in the absence of harmony between the sovereign and the sujects.
en:
Though blest with all these varied gifts' increase,
A land gains nought that is not with its king at peace.
ta:
ஆங்கமை வெய்தியக் கண்ணும் பயமின்றே
  வேந்தமை வில்லாத நாடு.

commentary: A fort is an object of importance to those who march (against their foes) as well as to those who through fear (of pursuers) would seek it for shelter.
en:
A fort is wealth to those who act against their foes;
Is wealth to them who, fearing, guard themselves from woes.
ta:
ஆற்று பவர்க்கும் அரண்பொருள் அஞ்சித்தற்
  போற்று பவர்க்கும் பொருள்.

commentary: A fort is that which has everlasting water, plains, mountains and cool shady forests.
en:
A fort is that which owns fount of waters crystal clear,
An open space, a hill, and shade of beauteous forest near.
ta:
மணிநீரும் மண்ணும் மலையும் அணிநிழற்
  காடும் உடைய தரண்.

commentary: The learned say that a fortress is an enclosure having these four (qualities) viz., height, breadth, strength and inaccessibility.
en:
Height, breadth, strength, difficult access:
Science declares a fort must these possess.
ta:
உயர்வகலம் திண்மை அருமைஇந் நான்கின்
  அமைவரண் என்றுரைக்கும் நூல்.

commentary: A fort is that which has an extensive space within, but only small places to be guarded, and such as can destroy the courage of besieging foes.
en:
A fort must need but slight defence, yet ample be,
Defying all the foeman's energy.
ta:
சிறுகாப்பிற் பேரிடத்த தாகி உறுபகை
  ஊக்கம் அழிப்ப தரண்.

commentary: A fort is that which cannot be captured, which abounds in suitable provisions, and affords a position of easy defence to its inmates.
en:
Impregnable, containing ample stores of food,
A fort for those within, must be a warlike station good.
ta:
கொளற்கரிதாய்க் கொண்டகூழ்த் தாகி அகத்தார்
  நிலைக்கெளிதாம் நீரது அரண்.

commentary: A fort is that which has all (needful) things, and excellent heroes that can help it against destruction (by foes).
en:
A fort, with all munitions amply stored,
In time of need should good reserves afford.
ta:
எல்லாப் பொருளும் உடைத்தாய் இடத்துதவும்
  நல்லாள் உடையது அரண்.

commentary: A fort is that which cannot be captured by blockading, assaulting, or undermining it.
en:
A fort should be impregnable to foes who gird it round,
Or aim there darts from far, or mine beneath the ground.
ta:
முற்றியும் முற்றா தெறிந்தும் அறைப்படுத்தும்
  பற்றற் கரியது அரண்.

commentary: That is a fort whose inmates are able to overcome without losing their ground, even abler men who have besieged it.
en:
Howe'er the circling foe may strive access to win,
A fort should give the victory to those who guard within.
ta:
முற்றாற்றி முற்றி யவரையும் பற்றாற்றிப்
  பற்றியார் வெல்வது அரண்.

commentary: A fort is that which derives excellence from the stratagems made (by its inmates) to defeat their enemies in the battlefield.
en:
At outset of the strife a fort should foes dismay;
And greatness gain by deeds in every glorious day.
ta:
முனைமுகத்து மாற்றலர் சாய வினைமுகத்து
  வீறெய்தி மாண்ட தரண்.

commentary: Although a fort may possess all (the above-said) excellence, it is, as it were without these, if its inmates possess not the excellence of action.
en:
Howe'er majestic castled walls may rise,
To craven souls no fortress strength supplies.
ta:
எனைமாட்சித் தாகியக் கண்ணும் வினைமாட்சி
  இல்லார்கண் இல்லது அரண்.

commentary: Besides wealth there is nothing that can change people of no importance into those of (some) importance.
en:
Nothing exists save wealth, that can
Change man of nought to worthy man.
ta:
பொருளல் லவரைப் பொருளாகச் செய்யும்
  பொருளல்லது இல்லை பொருள்.

commentary: All despise the poor; (but) all praise the rich.
en:
Those who have nought all will despise;
All raise the wealthy to the skies.
ta:
இல்லாரை எல்லாரும் எள்ளுவர் செல்வரை
  எல்லாரும் செய்வர் சிறப்பு.

commentary: The imperishable light of wealth goes into regions desired (by its owner) and destroys the darkness (of enmity therein).
en:
Wealth, the lamp unfailing, speeds to every land,
Dispersing darkness at its lord's command.
ta:
பொருளென்னும் பொய்யா விளக்கம் இருளறுக்கும்
  எண்ணிய தேயத்துச் சென்று.

commentary: The wealth acquired with a knowledge of the proper means and without foul practices will yield virtue and happiness.
en:
Their wealth, who blameless means can use aright,
Is source of virtue and of choice delight.
ta:
அறன்ஈனும் இன்பமும் ஈனும் திறனறிந்து
  தீதின்றி வந்த பொருள்.

commentary: (Kings) should rather avoid than seek the accumulation of wealth which does not flow in with mercy and love.
en:
Wealth gained by loss of love and grace,
Let man cast off from his embrace.
ta:
அருளொடும் அன்பொடும் வாராப் பொருளாக்கம்
  புல்லார் புரள விடல்.

commentary: Unclaimed wealth, wealth acquired by taxes, and wealth (got) by conquest of foes are (all) the wealth of the king.
en:
Wealth that falls to him as heir, wealth from the kingdom's dues,
The spoils of slaughtered foes; these are the royal revenues.
ta:
உறுபொருளும் உல்கு பொருளும்தன் ஒன்னார்த்
  தெறுபொருளும் வேந்தன் பொருள்.

commentary: The child mercy which is borne by love grows under the care of the rich nurse of wealth.
en:
'Tis love that kindliness as offspring bears:
And wealth as bounteous nurse the infant rears.
ta:
அருளென்னும் அன்பீன் குழவி பொருளென்னும்
  செல்வச் செவிலியால் உண்டு.

commentary: An undertaking of one who has wealth in one's hands is like viewing an elephant-fight from a hill-top.
en:
As one to view the strife of elephants who takes his stand,
On hill he's climbed, is he who works with money in his hand.
ta:
குன்றேறி யானைப்போர் கண்டற்றால் தன்கைத்தொன்று
  உண்டாகச் செய்வான் வினை.

commentary: Accumulate wealth; it will destroy the arrogance of (your) foes; there is no weapon sharper than it.
en:
Make money! Foeman's insolence o'ergrown
To lop away no keener steel is known.
ta:
செய்க பொருளைச் செறுநர் செருக்கறுக்கும்
  எஃகதனிற் கூரிய தில்.

commentary: To those who have honestly acquired an abundance of riches, the other two, (virtue and pleasure) are things easy (of acquisition).
en:
Who plenteous store of glorious wealth have gained,
By them the other two are easily obtained.
ta:
ஒண்பொருள் காழ்ப்ப இயற்றியார்க்கு எண்பொருள்
  ஏனை இரண்டும் ஒருங்கு.

commentary: The army which is complete in (its) parts and conquers without fear of wounds is the chief wealth of the king.
en:
A conquering host, complete in all its limbs, that fears no wound,
Mid treasures of the king is chiefest found.
ta:
உறுப்பமைந்து ஊறஞ்சா வெல்படை வேந்தன்
  வெறுக்கையுள் எல்லாம் தலை.

commentary: Ancient army can alone have the valour which makes it stand by its king at the time of defeat, fearless of wounds and unmindful of its reduced strength.
en:
In adverse hour, to face undaunted might of conquering foe,
Is bravery that only veteran host can show.
ta:
உலைவிடத்து ஊறஞ்சா வன்கண் தொலைவிடத்துத்
  தொல்படைக் கல்லால் அரிது.

commentary: What if (a host of) hostile rats roar like the sea ? They will perish at the mere breath of the cobra.
en:
Though, like the sea, the angry mice send forth their battle cry;
What then? The dragon breathes upon them, and they die!
ta:
ஒலித்தக்கால் என்னாம் உவரி எலிப்பகை
  நாகம் உயிர்ப்பக் கெடும்.

commentary: That indeed is an army which has stood firm of old without suffering destruction or deserting (to the enemy).
en:
That is a host, by no defeats, by no desertions shamed,
For old hereditary courage famed.
ta:
அழிவின்றி அறைபோகா தாகி வழிவந்த
  வன்க ணதுவே படை.

commentary: That indeed is an army which is capable of offering a united resistance, even if Yama advances against it with fury.
en:
That is a 'host' that joins its ranks, and mightily withstands,
Though death with sudden wrath should fall upon its bands.
ta:
கூற்றுடன்று மேல்வரினும் கூடி எதிர்நிற்கும்
  ஆற்ற லதுவே படை.

commentary: Valour, honour, following in the excellent-footsteps (of its predecessors) and trust-worthiness; these four alone constitute the safeguard of an army.
en:
Valour with honour, sure advance in glory's path, with confidence;
To warlike host these four are sure defence.
ta:
மறமானம் மாண்ட வழிச்செலவு தேற்றம்
  எனநான்கே ஏமம் படைக்கு.

commentary: That is an army which knowing the art of warding off an impending struggle, can bear against the dust-van (of a hostile force).
en:
A valiant army bears the onslaught, onward goes,
Well taught with marshalled ranks to meet their coming foes.
ta:
தார்தாங்கிச் செல்வது தானை தலைவந்த
  போர்தாங்கும் தன்மை அறிந்து.

commentary: Though destitute of courage to fight and strength (to endure), an army may yet gain renown by the splendour of its appearance.
en:
Though not in war offensive or defensive skilled;
An army gains applause when well equipped and drilled.
ta:
அடல்தகையும் ஆற்றலும் இல்லெனினும் தானை
  படைத்தகையால் பாடு பெறும்.

commentary: An army can triumph (over its foes) if it is free from diminution; irremediable aversion and poverty.
en:
Where weakness, clinging fear and poverty
Are not, the host will gain the victory.
ta:
சிறுமையும் செல்லாத் துனியும் வறுமையும்
  இல்லாயின் வெல்லும் படை.

commentary: Though an army may contain a large number of permanent soldiers, it cannot last if it has no generals.
en:
Though men abound, all ready for the war,
No army is where no fit leaders are.
ta:
நிலைமக்கள் சால உடைத்தெனினும் தானை
  தலைமக்கள் இல்வழி இல்.

commentary: O my foes, stand not before my leader; (for) many are those who did so but afterwards stood (in the shape of) statues.
en:
Ye foes! stand not before my lord! for many a one
Who did my lord withstand, now stands in stone!
ta:
