என்னைமுன் நில்லன்மின் தெவ்விர் பலரென்னை
  முன்நின்று கல்நின் றவர்.

commentary: It is more pleasant to hold the dart that has missed an elephant than that which has hit hare in the forest.
en:
Who aims at elephant, though dart should fail, has greater praise.
Than he who woodland hare with winged arrow slays.
ta:
கான முயலெய்த அம்பினில் யானை
  பிழைத்தவேல் ஏந்தல் இனிது.

commentary: The learned say that fierceness (incontest with a foe) is indeed great valour; but to become a benefactor in case of accident (to a foe) is the extreme (limit) of that valour.
en:
Fierceness in hour of strife heroic greatness shows;
Its edge is kindness to our suffering foes.
ta:
பேராண்மை என்ப தறுகண்ஒன் றுற்றக்கால்
  ஊராண்மை மற்றதன் எஃகு.

commentary: The hero who after casting the lance in his hand on an elephant, comes (in search of another) will pluck the one (that sticks) in his body and laugh (exultingly).
en:
At elephant he hurls the dart in hand; for weapon pressed,
He laughs and plucks the javelin from his wounded breast.
ta:
கைவேல் களிற்றொடு போக்கி வருபவன்
  மெய்வேல் பறியா நகும்.

commentary: Is it not a defeat to the valiant to wink and destroy their ferocious look when a lance in cast at them (by their foe) ?
en:
To hero fearless must it not defeat appear,
If he but wink his eye when foemen hurls his spear.
ta:
விழித்தகண் வேல்கொண டெறிய அழித்திமைப்பின்
  ஒட்டன்றோ வன்க ணவர்க்கு.

commentary: The hero will reckon among wasted days all those on which he had not received severe wounds.
en:
The heroes, counting up their days, set down as vain
Each day when they no glorious wound sustain.
ta:
விழுப்புண் படாதநாள் எல்லாம் வழுக்கினுள்
  வைக்கும்தன் நாளை எடுத்து.

commentary: The fastening of ankle-ring by those who disire a world-wide renown and not (the safety of) their lives is like adorning (themselves).
en:
Who seek for world-wide fame, regardless of their life,
The glorious clasp adorns, sign of heroic strife.
ta:
சுழலும் இசைவேண்டி வேண்டா உயிரார்
  கழல்யாப்புக் காரிகை நீர்த்து.

commentary: The heroes who are not afraid of losing their life in a contest will not cool their ardour, even if the king prohibits (their fighting).
en:
Fearless they rush where'er 'the tide of battle rolls';
The king's reproof damps not the ardour of their eager souls.
ta:
உறின்உயிர் அஞ்சா மறவர் இறைவன்
  செறினும் சீர்குன்றல் இலர்.

commentary: Who would reproach with failure those who seal their oath with their death ?
en:
Who says they err, and visits them scorn,
Who die and faithful guard the vow they've sworn?
ta:
இழைத்தது இகவாமைச் சாவாரை யாரே
  பிழைத்தது ஒறுக்கிற் பவர்.

commentary: If (heroes) can so die as to fill with tears the eyes of their rulers, such a death deserves to be obtained even by begging.
en:
If monarch's eyes o'erflow with tears for hero slain,
Who would not beg such boon of glorious death to gain?
ta:
புரந்தார்கண் நீர்மல்கச் சாகிற்பின் சாக்காடு
  இரந்துகோள் தக்கது உடைத்து.

commentary: What things are there so difficult to acquire as friendship ? What guards are there so difficult to break through by the efforts (of one's foes) ?
en:
What so hard for men to gain as friendship true?
What so sure defence 'gainst all that foe can do?
ta:
செயற்கரிய யாவுள நட்பின் அதுபோல்
  வினைக்கரிய யாவுள காப்பு.

commentary: The friendship of the wise waxes like the new moon; (but) that of fools wanes like the full moon.
en:
Friendship with men fulfilled of good Waxes like the crescent moon;
Friendship with men of foolish mood, Like the full orb, waneth soon.
ta:
நிறைநீர நீரவர் கேண்மை பிறைமதிப்
  பின்னீர பேதையார் நட்பு.

commentary: Like learning, the friendship of the noble, the more it is cultivated, the more delightful does it become.
en:
Learned scroll the more you ponder, Sweeter grows the mental food;
So the heart by use grows fonder, Bound in friendship with the good.
ta:
நவில்தொறும் நூல்நயம் போலும் பயில்தொறும்
  பண்புடை யாளர் தொடர்பு.

commentary: Friendship is to be practised not for the purpose of laughing but for that of being beforehand in giving one another sharp rebukes in case of transgression.
en:
Nor for laughter only friendship all the pleasant day,
But for strokes of sharp reproving, when from right you stray.
ta:
நகுதற் பொருட்டன்று நட்டல் மிகுதிக்கண்
  மேற்செனறு இடித்தற் பொருட்டு.

commentary: Living together and holding frequent intercourse are not necessary (for friendship); (mutual) understanding can alone create a claim for it.
en:
Not association constant, not affection's token bind;
'Tis the unison of feeling friends unites of kindred mind.
ta:
புணர்ச்சி பழகுதல் வேண்டா உணர்ச்சிதான்
  நட்பாங் கிழமை தரும்.

commentary: The love that dwells (merely in the smiles of the face is not friendship; (but) that which dwells deep in the smiles of the heart is true friendship.
en:
Not the face's smile of welcome shows the friend sincere,
But the heart's rejoicing gladness when the friend is near.
ta:
முகநக நட்பது நட்பன்று நெஞ்சத்து
  அகநக நட்பது நட்பு.

commentary: (True) friendship turns aside from evil (ways) makes (him) walk in the (good) way, and, in case of loss if shares his sorrow (with him).
en:
Friendship from ruin saves, in way of virtue keeps;
In troublous time, it weeps with him who weeps.
ta:
அழிவி னவைநீக்கி ஆறுய்த்து அழிவின்கண்
  அல்லல் உழப்பதாம் நட்பு.

commentary: (True) friendship hastens to the rescue of the afflicted (as readily) as the hand of one whose garment is loosened (before an assembly).
en:
As hand of him whose vesture slips away,
Friendship at once the coming grief will stay.
ta:
உடுக்கை இழந்தவன் கைபோல ஆங்கே
  இடுக்கண் களைவதாம் நட்பு.

commentary: Friendship may be said to be on its throne when it possesses the power of supporting one at all times and under all circumstances, (in the practice or virtue and wealth).
en:
And where is friendship's royal seat? In stable mind,
Where friend in every time of need support may find.
ta:
நட்பிற்கு வீற்றிருக்கை யாதெனின் கொட்பின்றி
  ஒல்லும்வாய் ஊன்றும் நிலை.

commentary: Though friends may praise one another saying, "He is so intimate with us, and we so much (with him)"; (still) such friendship will appear mean.
en:
Mean is the friendship that men blazon forth,
'He's thus to me' and 'such to him my worth'.
ta:
இனையர் இவரெமக்கு இன்னம்யாம் என்று
  புனையினும் புல்லென்னும் நட்பு.

commentary: As those who are of a friendly nature will not forsake (a friend) after once loving (him), there is no evil so great as contracting a friendship without due inquiry.
en:
To make an untried man your friend is ruin sure;
For friendship formed unbroken must endure.
ta:
நாடாது நட்டலிற் கேடில்லை நட்டபின்
  வீடில்லை நட்பாள் பவர்க்கு.

commentary: The friendship contracted by him who has not made repeated inquiry will in the end grieve (him) to death.
en:
Alliance with the man you have not proved and proved again,
In length of days will give you mortal pain.
ta:
ஆய்ந்தாய்ந்து கொள்ளாதான் கேண்மை கடைமுறை
  தான்சாம் துயரம் தரும்.

commentary: Make friendship (with one) after ascertaining (his) character, birth, defects and the whole of one's relations.
en:
Temper, descent, defects, associations free
From blame: know these, then let the man be friend to thee.
ta:
குணமும் குடிமையும் குற்றமும் குன்றா
  இனனும் அறிந்தியாக்க நட்பு.

commentary: The friendship of one who belongs to a (good) family and is afraid of (being charged with) guilt, is worth even purchasing.
en:
Who, born of noble race, from guilt would shrink with shame,
Pay any price so you as friend that man may claim.
ta:
குடிப்பிறந்து தன்கண் பழிநாணு வானைக்
  கொடுத்தும் கொளல்வேண்டும் நட்பு.

commentary: You should examine and secure the friendship of those who can speak so as to make you weep over a crime (before its commission) or rebuke you severely (after you have done it) and are able to teach you (the ways of) the world.
en:
Make them your chosen friend whose words repentance move,
With power prescription's path to show, while evil they reprove.
ta:
அழச்சொல்லி அல்லது இடித்து வழக்கறிய
  வல்லார்நடபு ஆய்ந்து கொளல்.

commentary: Even in ruin there is some good; (for) it is a rod by which one may measure fully (the affection of one's) relations.
en:
Ruin itself one blessing lends:
'Tis staff that measures out one's friends.
ta:
கேட்டினும் உண்டோர் உறுதி கிளைஞரை
  நீட்டி அளப்பதோர் கோல்.

commentary: It is indead a gain for one to renounce the friendship of fools.
en:
'Tis gain to any man, the sages say,
Friendship of fools to put away.
ta:
ஊதியம் என்பது ஒருவற்குப் பேதையார்
  கேண்மை ஒரீஇ விடல்.

commentary: Do not think of things that discourage your mind, nor contract friendship with those who would forsake you in adversity.
en:
Think not the thoughts that dwarf the soul; nor take
For friends the men who friends in time of grief forsake.
ta:
உள்ளற்க உள்ளம் சிறுகுவ கொள்ளற்க
  அல்லற்கண் ஆற்றறுப்பார் நட்பு.

commentary: The very thought of the friendship of those who have deserted one at the approach of adversity will burn one's mind at the time of death.
en:
Of friends deserting us on ruin's brink,
'Tis torture e'en in life's last hour to think.
ta:
கெடுங்காலைக் கைவிடுவார் கேண்மை அடுங்காலை
  உள்ளினும் உள்ளஞ் சுடும்.

commentary: Continue to enjoy the friendship of the pure; (but) renounce even with a gift, the friendship of those who do not agree (with the world).
en:
Cling to the friendship of the spotless one's; whate'er you pay.
Renounce alliance with the men of evil way.
ta:
மருவுக மாசற்றார் கேண்மைஒன் றீத்தும்
  ஒருவுக ஒப்பிலார் நட்பு.

commentary: Imtimate friendship is that which cannot in the least be injured by (things done through the) right (of longstanding intimacy).
en:
Familiarity is friendship's silent pact,
That puts restraint on no familiar act.
ta:
பழைமை எனப்படுவது யாதெனின் யாதும்
  கிழமையைக் கீழ்ந்திடா நட்பு.

commentary: The constituents of friendship are (things done through) the right of intimacy; to be pleased with such a right is the duty of the wise.
en:
Familiar freedom friendship's very frame supplies;
To be its savour sweet is duty of the wise.
ta:
நட்பிற் குறுப்புக் கெழுதகைமை மற்றதற்கு
  உப்பாதல் சான்றோர் கடன்.

commentary: Of what avail is long-standing friendship, if friends do not admit as their own actions done through the right of intimacy ?
en:
When to familiar acts men kind response refuse,
What fruit from ancient friendship's use?
ta:
பழகிய நட்பெவன் செய்யுங் கெழுதகைமை
  செய்தாங்கு அமையாக் கடை.

commentary: If friends, through the right of friendship, do (anything) without being asked, the wise will be pleased with them on account of its desirability.
en:
When friends unbidden do familiar acts with loving heart,
Friends take the kindly deed in friendly part.
ta:
விழைதகையான் வேண்டி இருப்பர் கெழுதகையாற்
  கேளாது நட்டார் செயின்.

commentary: If friends should perform what is painful, understand that it is owing not only to ignorance, but also to the strong claims of intimacy.
en:
Not folly merely, but familiar carelessness,
Esteem it, when your friends cause you distress.
ta:
பேதைமை ஒன்றோ பெருங்கிழமை என்றுணர்க
  நோதக்க நட்டார் செயின்.

commentary: Those who stand within the limits (of true friendship) will not even in adversity give up the intimacy of long-standing friends.
en:
Who stand within the bounds quit not, though loss impends,
Association with the old familiar friends.
ta:
எல்லைக்கண் நின்றார் துறவார் தொலைவிடத்தும்
  தொல்லைக்கண் நின்றார் தொடர்பு.

commentary: Those who have (long) stood in the path of affection will not give it up even if their friends cause (them) their ruin.
en:
True friends, well versed in loving ways,
Cease not to love, when friend their love betrays.
ta:
அழிவந்த செய்யினும் அன்பறார் அன்பின்
  வழிவந்த கேண்மை யவர்.

commentary: To those who understand that by which they should not listen to (tales about) the faults of their friends, that is a (profitable) day on which the latter may commit a fault.
en:
In strength of friendship rare of friend's disgrace who will not hear,
The day his friend offends will day of grace to him appear.
ta:
கேளிழுக்கம் கேளாக் கெழுதகைமை வல்லார்க்கு
  நாளிழுக்கம் நட்டார் செயின்.

commentary: They will be loved by the world, who have not forsaken the friendship of those with whom they have kept up an unbroken long-standing intimacy.
en:
Friendship of old and faithful friends,
Who ne'er forsake, the world commends.
ta:
கெடாஅ வழிவந்த கேண்மையார் கேண்மை
  விடாஅர் விழையும் உலகு.

commentary: Even enemies will love those who have never changed in their affection to their long-standing friends.
en:
Ill-wishers even wish them well, who guard.
For ancient friends, their wonted kind regard.
ta:
விழையார் விழையப் படுப பழையார்கண்
  பண்பின் தலைப்பிரியா தார்.

commentary: The decrease of friendship with those who look as if they would eat you up (through excess of love) while they are really destitute of goodness is far better than its increase.
en:
Though evil men should all-absorbing friendship show,
Their love had better die away than grow.
ta:
பருகுவார் போலினும் பண்பிலார் கேண்மை
  பெருகலிற் குன்றல் இனிது.

commentary: Of what avail is it to get or lose the friendship of those who love when there is gain and leave when there is none ?
en:
What though you gain or lose friendship of men of alien heart,
Who when you thrive are friends, and when you fail depart?
ta:
உறின்நட்டு அறின்ஙருஉம் ஒப்பிலார் கேண்மை
  பெறினும் இழப்பினும் என்.

commentary: Friendship who calculate the profits (of their friendship), prostitutes who are bent on obtaining their gains, and thieves are (all) of the same character.
en:
These are alike: the friends who ponder friendship's gain
Those who accept whate'er you give, and all the plundering train.
ta:
உறுவது சீர்தூக்கும் நட்பும் பெறுவது
  கொள்வாரும் கள்வரும் நேர்.

commentary: Solitude is more to be desired than the society of those who resemble the untrained horses which throw down (their riders) in the fields of battle.
en:
A steed untrained will leave you in the tug of war;
Than friends like that to dwell alone is better far.
ta:
அமரகத்து ஆற்றறுக்கும் கல்லாமா அன்னார்
  தமரின் தனிமை தலை.

commentary: It is far better to avoid that to contract the evil friendship of the base who cannot protect (their friends) even when appointed to do so.
en:
'Tis better not to gain than gain the friendship profitless
Of men of little minds, who succour fails when dangers press.
ta:
செய்தேமஞ் சாராச் சிறியவர் புன்கேண்மை
  எய்தலின் எய்தாமை நன்று.

commentary: The hatred of the wise is ten-million times more profitable than the excessive intimacy of the fool.
en:
Better ten million times incur the wise man's hate,
Than form with foolish men a friendship intimate.
ta:
பேதை பெருங்கெழீஇ நட்பின் அறிவுடையார்
  ஏதின்மை கோடி உறும்.

commentary: What comes from enemies is a hundred million times more profitable than what comes from the friendship of those who cause only laughter.
en:
From foes ten million fold a greater good you gain,
Than friendship yields that's formed with laughers vain.
ta:
நகைவகைய ராகிய நட்பின் பகைவரால்
  பத்தடுத்த கோடி உறும்.

commentary: Gradually abandon without revealing (beforehand) the friendship of those who pretend inability to carry out what they (really) could do.
en:
Those men who make a grievous toil of what they do
On your behalf, their friendship silently eschew.
ta:
ஒல்லும் கருமம் உடற்று பவர்கேண்மை
  சொல்லாடார் சோர விடல்.

commentary: The friendship of those whose actions do not agree with their words will distress (one) even in (one's) dreams.
en:
E'en in a dream the intercourse is bitterness
With men whose deeds are other than their words profess.
ta:
கனவினும் இன்னாது மன்னோ வினைவேறு
  சொல்வேறு பட்டார் தொடர்பு.

commentary: Avoid even the least approach to a contraction of friendship with those who would love you in private but ridicule you in public.
en:
In anywise maintain not intercourse with those,
Who in the house are friends, in hall are slandering foes.
ta:
எனைத்தும் குறுகுதல் ஓம்பல் மனைக்கெழீஇ
  மன்றில் பழிப்பார் தொடர்பு.

commentary: The friendship of those who behave like friends without inward affection is a weapon that may be thrown when a favourable opportunity presents itself.
en:
Anvil where thou shalt smitten be, when men occasion find,
Is friendship's form without consenting mind.
ta:
சீரிடம் காணின் எறிதற்குப் பட்டடை
  நேரா நிரந்தவர் நட்பு.

commentary: The friendship of those who seem to be friends while they are not, will change like the love of women.
en:
Friendship of those who seem our kin, but are not really kind.
Will change from hour to hour like woman's mind.
ta:
இனம்போன்று இனமல்லார் கேண்மை மகளிர்
  மனம்போல வேறு படும்.

commentary: Though (one's) enemies may have mastered many good books, it will be impossible for them to become truly loving at heart.
en:
To heartfelt goodness men ignoble hardly may attain,
Although abundant stores of goodly lore they gain.
ta:
பலநல்ல கற்றக் கடைத்து மனநல்லர்
  ஆகுதல் மாணார்க் கரிது.

commentary: One should fear the deceitful who smile sweetly with their face but never love with their heart.
en:
'Tis fitting you should dread dissemblers' guile,
Whose hearts are bitter while their faces smile.
ta:
முகத்தின் இனிய நகாஅ அகத்தின்னா
  வஞ்சரை அஞ்சப் படும்.

commentary: In nothing whatever is it proper to rely on the words of those who do not love with their heart.
en:
When minds are not in unison, 'its never; just,
In any words men speak to put your trust.
ta:
மனத்தின் அமையா தவரை எனைத்தொன்றும்
  சொல்லினால் தேறற்பாற்று அன்று.

commentary: Though (one's) foes may utter good things as though they were friends, once will at once understand (their evil, import).
en:
Though many goodly words they speak in friendly tone,
The words of foes will speedily be known.
ta:
நட்டார்போல் நல்லவை சொல்லினும் ஒட்டார்சொல்
  ஒல்லை உணரப் படும்.

commentary: Since the bending of the bow bespeaks evil, one should not accept (as good) the humiliating speeches of one's foes.
en:
To pliant speech from hostile lips give thou no ear;
'Tis pliant bow that show the deadly peril near!
ta:
சொல்வணக்கம் ஒன்னார்கண் கொள்ளற்க வில்வணக்கம்
  தீங்கு குறித்தமை யான்.

commentary: A weapon may be hid in the very hands with which (one's) foes adore (him) (and) the tears they shed are of the same nature.
en:
In hands that worship weapon ten hidden lies;
Such are the tears that fall from foeman's eyes.
ta:
தொழுதகை யுள்ளும் படையொடுங்கும் ஒன்னார்
  அழுதகண் ணீரும் அனைத்து.

commentary: It is the duty of kings to affect great love but make it die (inwardly); as regard those foes who shew them great friendship but despise them (in their heart).
en:
'Tis just, when men make much of you, and then despise,
To make them smile, and slap in friendship's guise.
ta:
மிகச்செய்து தம்மெள்ளு வாரை நகச்செய்து
  நட்பினுள் சாப்புல்லற் பாற்று.

commentary: When one's foes begin to affect friendship, one should love them with one's looks, and, cherishing no love in the heart, give up (even the former).
en:
When time shall come that foes as friends appear,
Then thou, to hide a hostile heart, a smiling face may'st wear.
ta:
பகைநட்பாம் காலம் வருங்கால் முகநட்டு
  அகநட்பு ஒரீஇ விடல்.

commentary: Folly is one (of the chief defects); it is that which (makes one) incur loss and forego gain.
en:
What one thing merits folly's special name.
Letting gain go, loss for one's own to claim!
ta:
பேதைமை என்பதொன்று யாதெனின் ஏதங்கொண்டு
  ஊதியம் போக விடல்.

commentary: The greatest folly is that which leads one to take delight in doing what is forbidden.
en:
'Mid follies chiefest folly is to fix your love
On deeds which to your station unbefitting prove.
ta:
பேதைமையுள் எல்லாம் பேதைமை காதன்மை
  கையல்ல தன்கட் செயல்.

commentary: Shamelessness indifference (to what must be sought after), harshness, and aversion for everything (that ought to be desired) are the qualities of the fool.
en:
Ashamed of nothing, searching nothing out, of loveless heart,
Nought cherishing, 'tis thus the fool will play his part.
ta:
நாணாமை நாடாமை நாரின்மை யாதொன்றும்
  பேணாமை பேதை தொழில்

commentary: There are no greater fools than he who, though he has read and understood (a great deal) and even taught it to others, does not walk according to his own teaching.
en:
The sacred law he reads and learns, to other men expounds,-
Himself obeys not; where can greater fool be found?
ta:
ஓதி உணர்ந்தும் பிறர்க்குரைத்தும் தானடங்காப்
  பேதையின் பேதையார் இல்.

commentary: A fool can procure in a single birth a hell into which he may enter and suffer through all the seven births.
en:
The fool will merit hell in one brief life on earth,
In which he entering sinks through sevenfold round of birth.
ta:
ஒருமைச் செயலாற்றும் பேதை எழுமையும்
  தான்புக் கழுந்தும் அளறு.

commentary: If the fool, who knows not how to act undertakes a work, he will (certainly) fail. (But) is it all ? He will even adorn himself with fetters.
en:
When fool some task attempts with uninstructed pains,
It fails; nor that alone, himself he binds with chains.
ta:
பொய்படும் ஒன்றோ புனைபூணும் கையறியாப்
  பேதை வினைமேற் கொளின்.

commentary: If a fool happens to get an immense fortune, his neighbours will enjoy it while his relations starve.
en:
When fools are blessed with fortune's bounteous store,
Their foes feed full, their friends are prey to hunger sore.
ta:
ஏதிலார் ஆரத் தமர்பசிப்பர் பேதை
  பெருஞ்செல்வம் உற்றக் கடை.

commentary: A fool happening to possess something is like the intoxication of one who is (already) giddy.
en:
When folly's hand grasps wealth's increase, 'twill be
As when a mad man raves in drunken glee.
ta:
மையல் ஒருவன் களித்தற்றால் பேதைதன்
  கையொன்று உடைமை பெறின்.

commentary: The friendship between fools is exceedingly delightful (to each other): for at parting there will be nothing to cause them pain.
en:
Friendship of fools is very pleasant thing,
Parting with them will leave behind no sting.
ta:
பெரிதினிது பேதையார் கேண்மை பிரிவின்கண்
  பீழை தருவதொன் றில்.

commentary: The appearance of a fool in an assembly of the learned is like placing (one's) unwashed feet on a bed.
en:
Like him who seeks his couch with unwashed feet,
Is fool whose foot intrudes where wise men meet.
ta:
கழாஅக்கால் பள்ளியுள் வைத்தற்றால் சான்றோர்
  குழாஅத்துப் பேதை புகல்.

commentary: The want of wisdom is the greatest of all wants; but that of wealth the world will not regard as such.
en:
Want of knowledge, 'mid all wants the sorest want we deem;
Want of other things the world will not as want esteem.
ta:
அறிவின்மை இன்மையுள் இன்மை பிறிதின்மை
  இன்மையா வையா துலகு.

commentary: (The cause of) a fool cheerfully giving (something) is nothing else but the receiver's merit (in a former birth).
en:
The gift of foolish man, with willing heart bestowed, is nought,
But blessing by receiver's penance bought.
ta:
அறிவிலான் நெஞ்சுவந்து ஈதல் பிறிதியாதும்
  இல்லை பெறுவான் தவம்.

commentary: The suffering that fools inflict upon themselves is hardly possible even to foes.
en:
With keener anguish foolish men their own hearts wring,
Than aught that even malice of their foes can bring.
ta:
அறிவிலார் தாந்தம்மைப் பீழிக்கும் பீழை
  செறுவார்க்கும் செய்தல் அரிது.

commentary: What is called want of wisdom is the vanity which says, "We are wise".
en:
What is stupidity? The arrogance that cries,
'Behold, we claim the glory of the wise.'
ta:
வெண்மை எனப்படுவ தியாதெனின் ஒண்மை
  உடையம்யாம் என்னும் செருக்கு.

commentary: Fools pretending to know what has not been read (by them) will rouse suspicion even as to what they have thoroughly mastered.
en:
If men what they have never learned assume to know,
Upon their real learning's power a doubt 'twill throw.
ta:
கல்லாத மேற்கொண் டொழுகல் கசடற
  வல்லதூஉம் ஐயம் தரும்.

commentary: Even to cover one's nakedness would be folly, if (one's) faults were not covered (by forsaking them).
en:
Fools are they who their nakedness conceal,
And yet their faults unveiled reveal.
ta:
அற்றம் மறைத்தலோ புல்லறிவு தம்வயின்
  குற்றம் மறையா வழி.

commentary: The fool who neglects precious counsel does, of his own accord, a great injury to himself.
en:
From out his soul who lets the mystic teachings die,
Entails upon himself abiding misery.
ta:
அருமறை சோரும் அறிவிலான் செய்யும்
  பெருமிறை தானே தனக்கு.

commentary: The fool will not perform (his duties) even when advised nor ascertain them himself; such a soul is a burden (to the earth) till it departs (from the body).
en:
Advised, he heeds not; of himself knows nothing wise;
This man's whole life is all one plague until he dies.
ta:
ஏவவும் செய்கலான் தான்தேறான் அவ்வுயிர்
  போஒம் அளவுமோர் நோய்.

commentary: One who would teach a fool will (simply) betray his folly; and the fool would (still) think himself "wise in his own conceit".
en:
That man is blind to eyes that will not see who knowledge shows;-
The blind man still in his blind fashion knows.
ta:
காணாதான் காட்டுவான் தான்காணான் காணாதான்
  கண்டானாம் தான்கண்ட வாறு.

commentary: He who denies the existence of what the world believes in will be regarded as a demon on earth.
en:
Who what the world affirms as false proclaim,
O'er all the earth receive a demon's name.
ta:
உலகத்தார் உண்டென்பது இல்லென்பான் வையத்து
  அலகையா வைக்கப் படும்.

commentary: The disease which fosters the evil of disunion among all creatures is termed hatred by the wise.
en:
Hostility disunion's plague will bring,
That evil quality, to every living thing.
ta:
இகலென்ப எல்லா உயிர்க்கும் பகலென்னும்
  பண்பின்மை பார஧க்கும் நோய்.

commentary: Though disagreeable things may be done from (a feeling of) disunion, it is far better that nothing painful be done from (that of) hatred.
en:
Though men disunion plan, and do thee much despite
'Tis best no enmity to plan, nor evil deeds requite.
ta:
பகல்கருதிப் பற்றா செயினும் இகல்கருதி
  இன்னாசெய் யாமை தலை.

commentary: To rid one-self of the distressing dtsease of hatred will bestow (on one) a never-decreasing imperishable fame.
en:
If enmity, that grievous plague, you shun,
Endless undying praises shall be won.
ta:
இகலென்னும் எவ்வநோய் நீக்கின் தவலில்லாத்
  தாவில் விளக்கம் தரும்.

commentary: If hatred which is the greatest misery is destroyed, it will yield the greatest delight.
en:
Joy of joys abundant grows,
When malice dies that woe of woes.
ta:
இன்பத்துள் இன்பம் பயக்கும் இகலென்னும்
  துன்பத்துள் துன்பங் கெடின்.

commentary: Who indeed would think of conquering those who naturally shrink back from hatred ?
en:
If men from enmity can keep their spirits free,
Who over them shall gain the victory?
ta:
இகலெதிர் சாய்ந்தொழுக வல்லாரை யாரே
  மிக்லூக்கும் தன்மை யவர்.

commentary: Failure and ruin are not far from him who says it is sweet to excel in hatred.
en:
The life of those who cherished enmity hold dear,
To grievous fault and utter death is near.
ta:
இகலின் மிகலினிது என்பவன் வாழ்க்கை
  தவலும் கெடலும் நணித்து.

commentary: Those whose judgement brings misery through its connection with hatred cannot understand the triumphant nature of truth.
en:
The very truth that greatness gives their eyes can never see,
Who only know to work men woe, fulfilled of enmity.
ta:
மிகல்மேவல் மெய்ப்பொருள் காணார் இகல்மேவல்
  இன்னா அறிவி னவர்.

commentary: Shrinking back from hatred will yield wealth; indulging in its increase will hasten ruin.
en:
'Tis gain to turn the soul from enmity;
Ruin reigns where this hath mastery.
ta:
இகலிற்கு எதிர்சாய்தல் ஆக்கம் அதனை
  மிக்லூக்கின் ஊக்குமாம் கேடு.

commentary: At the approach of wealth one will not think of hatred (but) to secure one's ruin, one will look to its increase.
en:
Men think not hostile thought in fortune's favouring hour,
They cherish enmity when in misfortune's power.
ta:
இகல்காணான் ஆக்கம் வருங்கால் அதனை
  மிகல்காணும் கேடு தரற்கு.

commentary: All calamities are caused by hatred; but by the delight (of friendship) is caused the great wealth of good virtues.
en:
From enmity do all afflictive evils flow;
But friendliness doth wealth of kindly good bestow.
ta:
இகலானாம் இன்னாத எல்லாம் நகலானாம்
  நன்னயம் என்னும் செருக்கு.

commentary: Avoid offering resistance to the strong; (but) never fail to cherish enmity towards the weak.
en:
With stronger than thyself, turn from the strife away;
With weaker shun not, rather court the fray.
ta:
வலியார்க்கு மாறேற்றல் ஓம்புக ஓம்பா
  மெலியார்மேல் மேக பகை.

commentary: How can he who is unloving, destitute of powerful aids, and himself without strength overcome the might of his foe ?
en:
No kinsman's love, no strength of friends has he;
How can he bear his foeman's enmity?
ta:
அன்பிலன் ஆன்ற துணையிலன் தான்துவ்வான்
  என்பரியும் ஏதிலான் துப்பு.

commentary: In the estimation of foes miserably weak is he, who is timid, ignorant, unsociable and niggardly.
en:
A craven thing! knows nought, accords with none, gives nought away;
To wrath of any foe he falls an easy prey.
ta:
அஞ்சும் அறியான் அமைவிலன் ஈகலான்
  தஞ்சம் எளியன் பகைக்கு.

commentary: He who neither refrains from anger nor keeps his secrets will at all times and in all places be easily conquered by all.
en:
His wrath still blazes, every secret told; each day
This man's in every place to every foe an easy prey.
ta:
நீங்கான் வெகுளி நிறையிலன் எஞ்ஞான்றும்
  யாங்கணும் யார்க்கும் எளிது.

commentary: (A) pleasing (object) to his foes is he who reads not moral works, does nothing that is enjoined by them cares not for reproach and is not possessed of good qualities.
en:
No way of right he scans, no precepts bind, no crimes affright,
No grace of good he owns; such man's his foes' delight.
ta:
வழிநோக்கான் வாய்ப்பன செய்யான் பழிநோக்கான்
  பண்பிலன் பற்றார்க்கு இனிது.

commentary: Highly to be desired is the hatred of him whose anger is blind, and whose lust increases beyond measure.
en:
Blind in his rage, his lustful passions rage and swell;
If such a man mislikes you, like it well.
ta:
காணாச் சினத்தான் கழிபெருங் காமத்தான்
  பேணாமை பேணப் படும்.

commentary: It is indeed necessary to obtain even by purchase the hatred of him who having begun (a work) does what is not conductive (to its accomplishment).
en:
Unseemly are his deeds, yet proffering aid, the man draws nigh:
His hate- 'tis cheap at any price- be sure to buy!
ta:
கொடுத்தும் கொளல்வேண்டும் மன்ற அடுத்திருந்து
  மாணாத செய்வான் பகை.

commentary: He will become friendless who is without (any good) qualities. and whose faults are many; (such a character) is a help to (his) foes.
en:
No gracious gifts he owns, faults many cloud his fame;
His foes rejoice, for none with kindred claim.
ta:
குணனிலனாய்க் குற்றம் பலவாயின் மாற்றார்க்கு
  இனனிலனாம் ஏமாப் புடைத்து.

commentary: There will be no end of lofty delights to the victorious, if their foes are (both) ignorant and timid.
en:
The joy of victory is never far removed from those
Who've luck to meet with ignorant and timid foes.
ta:
செறுவார்க்குச் சேணிகவா இன்பம் அறிவிலா
  அஞ்சும் பகைவர்ப் பெறின்.

commentary: The light (of fame) will never be gained by him who gains not the trifling reputation of having fought an unlearned (foe).
en:
The task of angry war with men unlearned in virtue's lore
Who will not meet, glory shall meet him never more.
ta:
கல்லான் வெகுளும் சிறுபொருள் எஞ்ஞான்றும்
  ஒல்லானை ஒல்லா தொளி.

commentary: The evil of hatred is not of a nature to be desired by one even in sport.
en:
For Hate, that ill-conditioned thing not e'en in jest.
Let any evil longing rule your breast.
ta:
பகைஎன்னும் பண்பி லதனை ஒருவன்
  நகையேயும் வேண்டற்பாற்று அன்று.

commentary: Though you may incur the hatred of warriors whose ploughs are bows, incur not that of ministers whose ploughs are words.
en:
Although you hate incur of those whose ploughs are bows,
Make not the men whose ploughs are words your foes!
ta:
வில்லேர் உழவர் பகைகொளினும் கொள்ளற்க
  சொல்லேர் உழவர் பகை.

commentary: He who being alone, incurs the hatred of many is more infatuated than even mad men.
en:
Than men of mind diseased, a wretch more utterly forlorn,
Is he who stands alone, object of many foeman's scorn.
ta:
ஏமுற் றவரினும் ஏழை தமியனாய்ப்
  பல்லார் பகைகொள் பவன்.

commentary: The world abides in the greatness of that good-natured man who behaves so as to turn hatred into friendship.
en:
The world secure on his dexterity depends,
Whose worthy rule can change his foes to friends.
ta:
பகைநட்பாக் கொண்டொழுகும் பண்புடை யாளன்
  தகைமைக்கண் தங்கிற்று உலகு.

commentary: He who is alone and helpless while his foes are two should secure one of them as an agreeable help (to himself).
en:
Without ally, who fights with twofold enemy o'ermatched,
Must render one of these a friend attached.
ta:
தன்துணை இன்றால் பகையிரண்டால் தான்ஒருவன்
  இன்துணையாக் கொள்கவற்றின் ஒன்று.

commentary: Though (one's foe is) aware or not of one's misfortune one should act so as neither to join nor separate (from him).
en:
Whether you trust or not, in time of sore distress,
Questions of diff'rence or agreement cease to press.
ta:
தேற஧னும் தேறா விடினும் அழிவின்கண்
  தேறான் பகாஅன் விடல்.

commentary: Relate not your suffering even to friends who are ignorant of it, nor refer to your weakness in the presence of your foes.
en:
To those who know them not, complain not of your woes;
Nor to your foeman's eyes infirmities disclose.
ta:
நோவற்க நொந்தது அறியார்க்கு மேவற்க
  மென்மை பகைவர் அகத்து.

commentary: The joy of one's foes will be destroyed if one guards oneself by knowing the way (of acting) and securing assistance.
en:
Know thou the way, then do thy part, thyself defend;
Thus shall the pride of those that hate thee have an end.
ta:
வகையறிந்து தற்செய்து தற்காப்ப மாயும்
  பகைவர்கண் பட்ட செருக்கு.

commentary: A thorny tree should be felled while young, (for) when it is grown it will destroy the hand of the feller.
en:
Destroy the thorn, while tender point can work thee no offence;
Matured by time, 'twill pierce the hand that plucks it thence.
ta:
இளைதாக முள்மரம் கொல்க களையுநர்
  கைகொல்லும் காழ்த்த இடத்து.

commentary: Those who do not destroy the pride of those who hate (them) will certainly not exist even to breathe.
en:
But breathe upon them, and they surely die,
Who fail to tame the pride of angry enemy.
ta:
உயிர்ப்ப உளரல்லர் மன்ற செயிர்ப்பவர்
  செம்மல் சிதைக்கலா தார்.

commentary: Shade and water are not pleasant, (if) they cause disease; so are the qualities of (one's) relations not agreeable, (if) they cause pain.
en:
Water and shade, if they unwholesome prove, will bring you pain.
And qualities of friends who treacherous act, will be your bane.
ta:
நிழல்நீரும் இன்னாத இன்னா தமர்நீரும்
  இன்னாவாம் இன்னா செயின்.

commentary: Fear not foes (who say they would cut) like a sword; (but) fear the friendship of foes (who seemingly act) like relations.
en:
Dread not the foes that as drawn swords appear;
Friendship of foes, who seem like kinsmen, fear!
ta:
வாள்போல பகைவரை அஞ்சற்க அஞ்சுக
  கேள்போல் பகைவர் தொடர்பு.

commentary: Fear internal enmity and guard yourself; (if not) it will destroy (you) in an evil hour, as surely as the tool which cuts the potter's clay.
en:
Of hidden hate beware, and guard thy life;
In troublous time 'twill deeper wound than potter's knife.
ta:
உட்பகை அஞ்சித்தற் காக்க உலைவிடத்து
  மட்பகையின் மாணத் தெறும்.

commentary: The secret enmity of a person whose mind in unreformed will lead to many evils causing disaffection among (one's) relations.
en:
If secret enmities arise that minds pervert,
Then even kin unkind will work thee grievous hurt.
ta:
மனமாணா உட்பகை தோன்றின் இனமாணா
  ஏதம் பலவும் தரும்.

commentary: If there appears internal hatred in a (king's) family; it will lead to many a fatal crime.
en:
Amid one's relatives if hidden hath arise,
'Twill hurt inflict in deadly wise.
ta:
உறல்முறையான் உட்பகை தோன்றின் இறல்முறையான்
  ஏதம் பலவும் தரும்.

commentary: If hatred arises among (one's) own people, it will be hardly possible (for one) to escape death.
en:
If discord finds a place midst those who dwelt at one before,
'Tis ever hard to keep destruction from the door.
ta:
ஒன்றாமை ஒன்றியார் கட்படின் எஞ்ஞான்றும்
  பொன்றாமை ஒன்றல் அரிது.

commentary: Never indeed will a family subject to internal hatred unite (really) though it may present an apparent union like that of a casket and its lid.
en:
As casket with its cover, though in one they live alway,
No union to the house where hate concealed hath sway.
ta:
செப்பின் புணர்ச்சிபோல் கூடினும் கூடாதே
  உட்பகை உற்ற குடி.

commentary: A family subject to internal hatred will wear out and lose its strength like iron that has been filed away.
en:
As gold with which the file contends is worn away,
So strength of house declines where hate concealed hath sway.
ta:
அரம்பொருத பொன்போலத் தேயும் உரம்பொருது
  உட்பகை உற்ற குடி.

commentary: Although internal hatred be as small as the fragment of the sesamum (seed), still does destruction dwell in it.
en:
Though slight as shred of 'seasame' seed it be,
Destruction lurks in hidden enmity.
ta:
எட்பக வன்ன சிறுமைத்தே ஆயினும்
  உட்பகை உள்ளதாங் கேடு.

commentary: Living with those who do not agree (with one) is like dwelling with a cobra (in the same) hut.
en:
Domestic life with those who don't agree,
Is dwelling in a shed with snake for company.
ta:
உடம்பாடு இலாதவர் வாழ்க்கை குடங்கருள்
  பாம்போடு உடனுறைந் தற்று.

commentary: Not to disregard the power of those who can carry out (their wishes) is more important than all the watchfulness of those who guard (themselves against evil).
en:
The chiefest care of those who guard themselves from ill,
Is not to slight the powers of those who work their mighty will.
ta:
ஆற்றுவார் ஆற்றல் இகழாமை போற்றுவார்
  போற்றலுள் எல்லாம் தலை.

commentary: To behave without respect for the great (rulers) will make them do (us) irremediable evils.
en:
If men will lead their lives reckless of great men's will,
Such life, through great men's powers, will bring perpetual ill.
ta:
பெரியாரைப் பேணாது ஒழுகிற் பெரியாரால்
  பேரா இடும்பை தரும்.

commentary: If a person desires ruin, let him not listen to the righteous dictates of law, but commit crimes against those who are able to slay (other sovereigns).
en:
Who ruin covet let them shut their ears, and do despite
To those who, where they list to ruin have the might.
ta:
கெடல்வேண்டின் கேளாது செய்க அடல்வேண்டின்
  ஆற்று பவர்கண் இழுக்கு.

commentary: The weak doing evil to the strong is like beckoning Yama to come (and destroy them).
en:
When powerless man 'gainst men of power will evil deeds essay,
Tis beck'ning with the hand for Death to seize them for its prey.
ta:
கூற்றத்தைக் கையால் விளித்தற்றால் ஆற்றுவார்க்கு
  ஆற்றாதார் இன்னா செயல்.

commentary: Those who have incurred the wrath of a cruel and mighty potentate will not prosper wherever they may go.
en:
Who dare the fiery wrath of monarchs dread,
Where'er they flee, are numbered with the dead.
ta:
யாண்டுச்சென்று யாண்டும் உளராகார் வெந்துப்பின்
  வேந்து செறப்பட் டவர்.

commentary: Though burnt by a fire (from a forest), one may perhaps live; (but) never will he live who has shown disrespect to the great (devotees).
en:
Though in the conflagration caught, he may escape from thence:
He 'scapes not who in life to great ones gives offence.
ta:
எரியால் சுடப்படினும் உய்வுண்டாம் உய்யார்
  பெரியார்ப் பிழைத்தொழுகு வார்.

commentary: If a king incurs the wrath of the righteous great, what will become of his government with its splendid auxiliaries and (all) its untold wealth ?
en:
Though every royal gift, and stores of wealth your life should crown,
What are they, if the worthy men of mighty virtue frown?
ta:
வகைமாண்ட வாழ்க்கையும் வான்பொருளும் என்னாம்
  தகைமாண்ட தக்கார் செறின்.

commentary: If (the) hill-like (devotees) resolve on destruction, those who seemed to be everlasting will be destroyed root and branch from the earth.
en:
If they, whose virtues like a mountain rise, are light esteemed;
They die from earth who, with their households, ever-during seemed.
ta:
குன்றன்னார் குன்ற மதிப்பின் குடியொடு
  நின்றன்னார் மாய்வர் நிலத்து.

commentary: If those of exalted vows burst in a rage, even (Indra) the king will suffer a sudden loss and be entirely ruined.
en:
When blazes forth the wrath of men of lofty fame,
Kings even fall from high estate and perish in the flame.
ta:
ஏந்திய கொள்கையார் சீறின் இடைமுரிந்து
  வேந்தனும் வேந்து கெடும்.

commentary: Though in possession of numerous auxiliaries, they will perish who are-exposed to the wrath of the noble whose penance is boundless.
en:
Though all-surpassing wealth of aid the boast,
If men in glorious virtue great are wrath, they're lost.
ta:
இறந்தமைந்த சார்புடையர் ஆயினும் உய்யார்
  சிறந்தமைந்த சீரார் செறின்.

commentary: Those who lust after their wives will not attain the excellence of virtue; and it is just this that is not desired by those who are bent on acquiring wealth.
en:
Who give their soul to love of wife acquire not nobler gain;
Who give their soul to strenuous deeds such meaner joys disdain.
ta:
மனைவிழைவார் மாண்பயன் எய்தார் வினைவிழையார்
  வேண்டாப் பொருளும் அது.

commentary: The wealth of him who, regardless (of his manliness), devotes himself to his wife's feminine nature will cause great shame (to ali men) and to himself;
en:
Who gives himself to love of wife, careless of noble name
His wealth will clothe him with o'erwhelming shame.
ta:
பேணாது பெண்விழைவான் ஆக்கம் பெரியதோர்
  நாணாக நாணுத் தரும்.

commentary: The frailty that stoops to a wife will always make (her husband) feel ashamed among the good.
en:
Who to his wife submits, his strange, unmanly mood
Will daily bring him shame among the good.
ta:
இல்லாள்கண் தாழ்ந்த இயல்பின்மை எஞ்ஞான்றும்
  நல்லாருள் நாணுத் தரும்.

commentary: The undertaking of one, who fears his wife and is therefore destitute of (bliss), will never be applauded.
en:
No glory crowns e'en manly actions wrought
By him who dreads his wife, nor gives the other world a thought.
ta:
மனையாளை அஞ்சும் மறுமையி லாளன்
  வினையாண்மை வீறெய்த லின்று.

commentary: He that fears his wife will always be afraid of doing good deeds (even) to the good.
en:
Who quakes before his wife will ever tremble too,
Good deeds to men of good deserts to do.
ta:
இல்லாளை அஞ்சுவான் அஞ்சுமற் றெஞ்ஞான்றும்
  நல்லார்க்கு நல்ல செயல்.

commentary: They that fear the bamboo-like shoulders of their wives will be destitute of manliness though they may flourish like the Gods.
en:
Though, like the demi-gods, in bliss they dwell secure from harm,
Those have no dignity who fear the housewife's slender arm.
ta:
இமையாரின் வாழினும் பாடிலரே இல்லாள்
  அமையார்தோள் அஞ்சு பவர்.

commentary: Even shame faced womanhood is more to be esteemed than the shameless manhood that performs the behests of a wife.
en:
The dignity of modest womanhood excels
His manliness, obedient to a woman's law who dwells.
ta:
பெண்ணேவல் செய்தொழுகும் ஆண்மையின் நாணுடைப்
  பெண்ணே பெருமை உடைத்து.

commentary: Those who yield to the wishes of their wives will neither relieve the wants of (their) friends nor perform virtuous deeds.
en:
Who to the will of her with beauteous brow their lives conform,
Aid not their friends in need, nor acts of charity perform.
ta:
நட்டார் குறைமுடியார் நன்றாற்றார் நன்னுதலாள்
  பெட்டாங்கு ஒழுகு பவர்.

commentary: From those who obey the commands of their wives are to be expected neither deeds of virtue, nor those of wealth nor (even) those of pleasure.
en:
No virtuous deed, no seemly wealth, no pleasure, rests
With them who live obedient to their wives' behests.
ta:
அறவினையும் ஆன்ற பொருளும் பிறவினையும்
  பெண்ஏவல் செய்வார்கண் இல்.

commentary: The foolishness that results from devotion to a wife will never be found in those who possess a reflecting mind and a prosperity (flowing) therefrom.
en:
Where pleasures of the mind, that dwell in realms of thought, abound,
Folly, that springs from overweening woman's love, is never found.
ta:
எண்சேர்ந்த நெஞ்சத் திடனுடையார்க்கு எஞ்ஞான்றும்
  பெண்சேர்ந்தாம் பேதைமை இல்.

commentary: The sweet words of elegant braceleted (prostitutes) who desire (a man) not from affection but from avarice, will cause sorrow.
en:
Those that choice armlets wear who seek not thee with love,
But seek thy wealth, their pleasant words will ruin prove.
ta:
அன்பின் விழையார் பொருள்விழையும் ஆய்தொடியார்
  இன்சொல் இழுக்குத் தரும்.

commentary: One must ascertain the character of the ill-natured women who after ascertaining the wealth (of a man) speak (as if they were) good natured-ones, and avoid intercourse (with them).
en:
Who weigh the gain, and utter virtuous words with vicious heart,
Weighing such women's worth, from their society depart.
ta:
பயன்தூக்கிப் பண்புரைக்கும் பண்பின் மகளிர்
  நயன்தூக்கி நள்ளா விடல்.

commentary: The false embraces of wealth-loving women are like (hired men) embracing a strange corpse in a dark room.
en:
As one in darkened room, some stranger corpse inarms,
Is he who seeks delight in mercenary women's charms!
ta:
பொருட்பெண்டிர் பொய்ம்மை முயக்கம் இருட்டறையில்
  ஏத஧ல் பிணந்தழீஇ அற்று.

commentary: The wise who seek the wealth of grace will not desire the base favours of those who regard wealth (and not pleasure) as (their) riches.
en:
Their worthless charms, whose only weal is wealth of gain,
From touch of these the wise, who seek the wealth of grace, abstain.
ta:
பொருட்பொருளார் புன்னலந் தோயார் அருட்பொருள்
  ஆயும் அறிவி னவர்.

commentary: Those whose knowledge is made excellent by their (natural) sense will not covet the trffling delights of those whose favours are common (to all).
en:
From contact with their worthless charms, whose charms to all are free,
The men with sense of good and lofty wisdom blest will flee;
ta:
பொதுநலத்தார் புன்னலம் தோயார் மதிநலத்தின்
  மாண்ட அறிவி னவர்.

commentary: Those who would spread (the fame of) their own goodness will not desire the shoulders of those who rejoice in their accomplishments and bestow their despicable favours (on all who pay).
en:
From touch of those who worthless charms, with wanton arts, display,
The men who would their own true good maintain will turn away.
ta:
தந்நலம் பார஧ப்பார் தோயார் தகைசெருக்கிப்
  புன்னலம் பாரிப்பார் தோள்.

commentary: Those who are destitute of a perfectly (reformed) mind will covet the shoulders of those who embrace (them) while their hearts covet other things.
en:
Who cherish alien thoughts while folding in their feigned embrace,
These none approach save those devoid of virtue's grace.
ta:
நிறைநெஞ்சம் இல்லவர் தோய்வார் பிறநெஞ்சிற்
  பேணிப் புணர்பவர் தோள்.

commentary: The wise say that to such as are destitute of discerning sense the embraces of faithless women are (as ruinous as those of) the celestail female.
en:
As demoness who lures to ruin woman's treacherous love
To men devoid of wisdom's searching power will prove.
ta:
ஆயும் அறிவினர் அல்லார்க்கு அணங்கென்ப
  மாய மகளிர் முயக்கு.

commentary: The delicate shoulders of prostitutes with excellent jewels are a hell into which are plunged the ignorant base.
en:
The wanton's tender arm, with gleaming jewels decked,
Is hell, where sink degraded souls of men abject.
ta:
வரைவிலா மாணிழையார் மென்தோள் புரையிலாப்
  பூரியர்கள் ஆழும் அளறு.

commentary: Treacherous women, liquor, and gambling are the associates of such as have forsaken by Fortune.
en:
Women of double minds, strong drink, and dice; to these giv'n o'er,
Are those on whom the light of Fortune shines no more.
ta:
இருமனப் பெண்டிரும் கள்ளும் கவறும்
  திருநீக்கப் பட்டார் தொடர்பு.

commentary: Those who always thirst after drink will neither inspire fear (in others) nor retain the light (of their fame).
en:
Who love the palm's intoxicating juice, each day,
No rev'rence they command, their glory fades away.
ta:
உட்கப் படாஅர் ஒளியிழப்பர் எஞ்ஞான்றும்
  கட்காதல் கொண்டொழுகு வார்.

commentary: Let no liquor be drunk; if it is desired, let it be drunk by those who care not for esteem of the great.
en:
Drink not inebriating draught. Let him count well the cost.
Who drinks, by drinking, all good men's esteem is lost.
ta:
உண்ணற்க கள்ளை உணில்உண்க சான்றோரான்
  எண்ணப் படவேண்டா தார்.

commentary: Intoxication is painful even in the presence of (one's) mother; what will it not then be in that of the wise ?
en:
The drunkard's joy is sorrow to his mother's eyes;
What must it be in presence of the truly wise?
ta:
ஈன்றாள் முகத்தேயும் இன்னாதால் என்மற்றுச்
  சான்றோர் முகத்துக் களி.

commentary: The fair maid of modesty will turn her back on those who are guilty of the great and abominable crime of drunkenness.
en:
Shame, goodly maid, will turn her back for aye on them
Who sin the drunkard's grievous sin, that all condemn.
ta:
நாண்என்னும் நல்லாள் புறங்கொடுக்கும் கள்ளென்னும்
  பேணாப் பெருங்குற்றத் தார்க்கு.

commentary: To give money and purchase unconsciousness is the result of one's ignorance of (one's own actions).
en:
With gift of goods who self-oblivion buys,
Is ignorant of all that man should prize.
ta:
கையறி யாமை உடைத்தே பொருள்கொடுத்து
  மெய்யறி யாமை கொளல்.

commentary: They that sleep resemble the deed; (likewise) they that drink are no other than poison-eaters.
en:
Sleepers are as the dead, no otherwise they seem;
Who drink intoxicating draughts, they poison quaff, we deem.
ta:
துஞ்சினார் செத்தாரின் வேறல்லர் எஞ்ஞான்றும்
  நஞ்சுண்பார் கள்ளுண் பவர்.

commentary: Those who always intoxicate themselves by a private (indulgence in) drink; will have their secrets detected and laughed at by their fellow-townsmen.
en:
Who turn aside to drink, and droop their heavy eye,
Shall be their townsmen's jest, when they the fault espy.
ta:
உள்ளொற்றி உள்ளூர் நகப்படுவர் எஞ்ஞான்றும்
  கள்ளொற்றிக் கண்சாய் பவர்3

commentary: Let (the drunkard) give up saying "I have never drunk"; (for) the moment (he drinks) he will simply betray his former attempt to conceal.
en:
No more in secret drink, and then deny thy hidden fraud;
What in thy mind lies hid shall soon be known abroad.
ta:
களித்தறியேன் என்பது கைவிடுக நெஞ்சத்து
  ஒளித்ததூஉம் ஆங்கே மிகும்.

commentary: Reasoning with a drunkard is like going under water with a torch in search of a drowned man.
en:
Like him who, lamp in hand, would seek one sunk beneath the wave.
Is he who strives to sober drunken man with reasonings grave.
ta:
களித்தானைக் காரணம் காட்டுதல் கீழ்நீர்க்
  குளித்தானைத் தீத்துரீஇ அற்று.

commentary: When (a drunkard) who is sober sees one who is not, it looks as if he remembered not the evil effects of his (own) drink.
en:
When one, in sober interval, a drunken man espies,
Does he not think, 'Such is my folly in my revelries'?
ta:
கள்ளுண்ணாப் போழ்திற் களித்தானைக் காணுங்கால்
  உள்ளான்கொல் உண்டதன் சோர்வு.

commentary: Though able to win, let not one desire gambling; (for) even what is won is like a fish swallowing the iron in fish-hook.
en:
Seek not the gamester's play; though you should win,
Your gain is as the baited hook the fish takes in.
ta:
வேண்டற்க வென்றிடினும் சூதினை வென்றதூஉம்
  தூண்டிற்பொன் மீன்விழுங்கி அற்று.

commentary: Is there indeed a means of livelihood that can bestow happiness on gamblers who gain one and lose a hundred ?
en:
Is there for gamblers, too, that gaining one a hundred lose, some way
That they may good obtain, and see a prosperous day?
ta:
ஒன்றெய்தி நூறிழக்கும் சூதர்க்கும் உண்டாங்கொல்
  நன்றெய்தி வாழ்வதோர் ஆறு.

commentary: If the king is incessantly addicted to the rolling dice in the hope of gain, his wealth and the resources thereof will take their departure and fall into other's hands.
en:
If prince unceasing speak of nought but play,
Treasure and revenue will pass from him away.
ta:
உருளாயம் ஓவாது கூறின் பொருளாயம்
  போஒய்ப் புறமே படும்.

commentary: There is nothing else that brings (us) poverty like gambling which causes many a misery and destroys (one's) reputation.
en:
Gaming brings many woes, and ruins fair renown;
Nothing to want brings men so surely down.
ta:
சிறுமை பலசெய்து சீரழ஧க்கும் சூதின்
  வறுமை தருவதொன்று இல்.

commentary: Penniless are those who by reason of their attachment would never forsake gambling, the gambling-place and the handling (of dice).
en:
The dice, and gaming-hall, and gamester's art, they eager sought,
Thirsting for gain- the men in other days who came to nought.
ta:
கவறும் கழகமும் கையும் தருக்கி
  இவறியார் இல்லாகி யார்.

commentary: Those who are swallowed by the goddess called "gambling" will never have their hunger satisfied, but suffer the pangs of hell in the next world.
en:
Gambling's Misfortune's other name: o'er whom she casts her veil,
They suffer grievous want, and sorrows sore bewail.
ta:
அகடாரார் அல்லல் உழப்பர்சூ தென்னும்
  முகடியான் மூடப்பட் டார்.

commentary: To waste time at the place of gambling will destroy inherited wealth and goodness of character.
en:
Ancestral wealth and noble fame to ruin haste,
If men in gambler's halls their precious moments waste.
ta:
பழகிய செல்வமும் பண்பும் கெடுக்கும்
  கழகத்துக் காலை புகின்.

commentary: Gambling destroys property, teaches falsehood, puts an end to benevolence, and brings in misery (here and hereafter).
en:
Gambling wastes wealth, to falsehood bends the soul: it drives away
All grace, and leaves the man to utter misery a prey.
ta:
பொருள்கெடுத்துப் பொய்மேற் கொளீஇ அருள்கெடுத்து
  அல்லல் உழப்பிக்கும் சூது.

commentary: The habit of gambling prevents the attainment of these five: clothing, wealth, food, fame and learning.
en:
Clothes, wealth, food, praise, and learning, all depart
From him on gambler's gain who sets his heart.
ta:
உடைசெல்வம் ஊண்ஒளி கல்விஎன்று ஐந்தும்
  அடையாவாம் ஆயங் கொளின்.

commentary: As the gambler loves (his vice) the more he loses by it, so does the soul love (the body) the more it suffers through it.
en:
Howe'er he lose, the gambler's heart is ever in the play;
E'en so the soul, despite its griefs, would live on earth alway.
ta:
இழத்தொறூஉம் காதலிக்கும் சூதேபோல் துன்பம்
  உழத்தொறூஉம் காதற்று உயிர்.

commentary: If (food and work are either) excessive or deficient, the three things enumerated by (medical) writers, flatulence, biliousness, and phlegm, will cause (one) disease.
en:
The learned books count three, with wind as first; of these,
As any one prevail, or fail; 'twill cause disease.
ta:
மிகினும் குறையினும் நோய்செய்யும் நூலோர்
  வளிமுதலா எண்ணிய மூன்று.

commentary: No medicine is necessary for him who eats after assuring (himself) that what he has (already) eaten has been digested.
en:
No need of medicine to heal your body's pain,
If, what you ate before digested well, you eat again.
ta:
மருந்தென வேண்டாவாம் யாக்கைக்கு அருந்தியது
  அற்றது போற்றி உணின்.

commentary: If (one's food has been) digested let one eat with moderation; (for) that is the way to prolong the life of an embodied soul.
en:
Who has a body gained may long the gift retain,
If, food digested well, in measure due he eat again.
ta:
அற்றால் அறவறிந்து உண்க அஃதுடம்பு
  பெற்றான் நெடிதுய்க்கும் ஆறு.

commentary: (First) assure yourself that your food has been digested and never fail to eat, when very hungry, whatever is not disagreeable (to you).
en:
Knowing the food digested well, when hunger prompteth thee,
With constant care, the viands choose that well agree.
ta:
அற்றது அறிந்து கடைப்பிடித்து மாறல்ல
  துய்க்க துவரப் பசித்து.

commentary: There will be no disaster to one's life if one eats with moderation, food that is not disagreeable.
en:
With self-denial take the well-selected meal;
So shall thy frame no sudden sickness feel.
ta:
மாறுபாடு இல்லாத உண்டி மறுத்துண்ணின்
  ஊறுபாடு இல்லை உயிர்க்கு.

commentary: As pleasure dwells with him who eats moderately, so disease (dwells) with the glutton who eats voraciously.
en:
On modest temperance as pleasures pure,
So pain attends the greedy epicure.
ta:
இழிவறிந்து உண்பான்கண் இன்பம்போல் நிற்கும்
  கழிபேர் இரையான்கண் நோய்.

commentary: He will be afflicted with numberless diseases, who eats immoderately, ignorant (of the rules of health).
en:
Who largely feeds, nor measure of the fire within maintains,
That thoughtless man shall feel unmeasured pains.
ta:
தீயள வன்றித் தெரியான் பெரிதுண்ணின்
  நோயள வின்றிப் படும்.

commentary: Let the physician enquire into the (nature of the) disease, its cause and its method of cure and treat it faithfully according to (medical rule).
en:
Disease, its cause, what may abate the ill:
Let leech examine these, then use his skill.
ta:
நோய்நாடி நோய்முதல் நாடி அதுதணிக்கும்
  வாய்நாடி வாய்ப்பச் செயல்.

commentary: The learned (physician) should ascertain the condition of his patient; the nature of his disease, and the season (of the year) and (then) proceed (with his treatment).
en:
The habitudes of patient and disease, the crises of the ill
These must the learned leech think over well, then use his skill.
ta:
உற்றான் அளவும் பிணியளவும் காலமும்
  கற்றான் கருதிச் செயல்.

commentary: Medical science consists of four parts, viz., patient, physician, medicine and compounder; and each of these (again) contains four sub-divisions.
en:
For patient, leech, and remedies, and him who waits by patient's side,
The art of medicine must fourfold code of laws provide.
ta:
உற்றவன் தீர்ப்பான் மருந்துழைச் செல்வானென்று
  அப்பால் நாற்கூற்றே மருந்து.

commentary: Consistency (of thought, word and deed) and fear (of sin) are conjointly natural only to the high-born.
en:
Save in the scions of a noble house, you never find
Instinctive sense of right and virtuous shame combined.
ta:
இற்பிறந்தார் கண்அல்லது இல்லை இயல்பாகச்
  செப்பமும் நாணும் ஒருங்கு.

commentary: The high-born will never deviate from these three; good manners, truthfulness and modesty.
en:
In these three things the men of noble birth fail not:
In virtuous deed and truthful word, and chastened thought.
ta:
ஒழுக்கமும் வாய்மையும் நாணும்இம் மூன்றும்
  இழுக்கார் குடிப்பிறந் தார்.

commentary: A cheerful countenance, liberality, pleasant words, and an unreviling disposition, these four are said to be the proper qualities of the truly high-born.
en:
The smile, the gift, the pleasant word, unfailing courtesy
These are the signs, they say, of true nobility.
ta:
நகைஈகை இன்சொல் இகழாமை நான்கும்
  வகையென்ப வாய்மைக் குடிக்கு.

commentary: Though blessed with immense wealth, the noble will never do anything unbecoming.
en:
Millions on millions piled would never win
The men of noble race to soul-degrading sin.
ta:
அடுக்கிய கோடி பெறினும் குடிப்பிறந்தார்
  குன்றுவ செய்தல் இலர்.

commentary: Though their means fall off, those born in ancient families, will not lose their character (for liberality).
en:
Though stores for charity should fail within, the ancient race
Will never lose its old ancestral grace.
ta:
வழங்குவ துள்வீழ்ந்தக் கண்ணும் பழங்குடி
  பண்பில் தலைப்பிரிதல் இன்று.

commentary: Those who seek to preserve the irreproachable honour of their families will not viciously do what is detrimental thereto.
en:
Whose minds are set to live as fits their sire's unspotted fame,
Stooping to low deceit, commit no deeds that gender shame.
ta:
சலம்பற்றிச் சால்பில செய்யார்மா சற்ற
  குலம்பற்றி வாழ்தும்என் பார்.

commentary: The defects of the noble will be observed as clearly as the dark spots in the moon.
en:
The faults of men of noble race are seen by every eye,
As spots on her bright orb that walks sublime the evening sky.
ta:
குடிப்பிறந்தார் கண்விளங்கும் குற்றம் விசும்பின்
  மதிக்கண் மறுப்போல் உயர்ந்து.

commentary: If one of a good family betrays want of affection, his descent from it will be called in question.
en:
If lack of love appear in those who bear some goodly name,
'Twill make men doubt the ancestry they claim.
ta:
நலத்தின்கண் நாரின்மை தோன்றின் அவனைக்
  குலத்தின்கண் ஐயப் படும்.

commentary: As the sprout indicates the nature of the soil, (so) the speech of the noble indicates (that of one's birth).
en:
Of soil the plants that spring thereout will show the worth:
The words they speak declare the men of noble birth.
ta:
நிலத்தில் கிடந்தமை கால்காட்டும் காட்டும்
  குலத்தில் பிறந்தார்வாய்ச் சொல்.

commentary: He who desires a good name must desire modesty; and he who desires (the continuance of) a family greatness must be submissive to all.
en:
Who seek for good the grace of virtuous shame must know;
Who seek for noble name to all must reverence show.
ta:
நலம்வேண்டின் நாணுடைமை வேண்டும் குலம்
  வேண்டின் வேண்டுக யார்க்கும் பணிவு.

commentary: Actions that would degrade (one's) family should not be done; though they may be so important that not doing them would end in death.
en:
Though linked to splendours man no otherwise may gain,
Reject each act that may thine honour's clearness stain.
ta:
இன்றி அமையாச் சிறப்பின ஆயினும்
  குன்ற வருப விடல்.

commentary: Those who desire (to maintain their) honour, will surely do nothing dishonourable, even for the sake of fame.
en:
Who seek with glory to combine honour's untarnished fame,
Do no inglorious deeds, though men accord them glory's name.
ta:
சீரினும் சீரல்ல செய்யாரே சீரொடு
  பேராண்மை வேண்டு பவர்.

commentary: In great prosperity humility is becoming; dignity, in great adversity.
en:
Bow down thy soul, with increase blest, in happy hour;
Lift up thy heart, when stript of all by fortune's power.
ta:
பெருக்கத்து வேண்டும் பணிதல் சிறிய
  சுருக்கத்து வேண்டும் உயர்வு.

commentary: They who have fallen from their (high) position are like the hair which has fallen from the head.
en:
Like hairs from off the head that fall to earth,
When fall'n from high estate are men of noble birth.
ta:
தலையின் இழிந்த மயிரனையர் மாந்தர்
  நிலையின் இழிந்தக் கடை.

commentary: Even those who are exalted like a hill will be thought low, if they commit deeds that are debasing.
en:
If meanness, slight as 'abrus' grain, by men be wrought,
Though like a hill their high estate, they sink to nought.
ta:
குன்றின் அனையாரும் குன்றுவர் குன்றுவ
  குன்றி அனைய செயின்.

commentary: Of what good is it (for the high-born) to go and stand in vain before those who revile him ? it only brings him loss of honour and exclusion from heaven.
en:
It yields no praise, nor to the land of Gods throws wide the gate:
Why follow men who scorn, and at their bidding wait?
ta:
புகழ்இன்றால் புத்தேள்நாட்டு உய்யாதால் என்மற்று
  இகழ்வார்பின் சென்று நிலை.

commentary: It is better for a man to be said of him that he died in his usual state than that he eked out his life by following those who disgraced him.
en:
Better 'twere said, 'He's perished!' than to gain
The means to live, following in foeman's train.
ta:
ஒட்டார்பின் சென்றொருவன் வாழ்தலின் அந்நிலையே
  கெட்டான் எனப்படுதல் நன்று.

commentary: For the high-born to keep their body in life when their honour is gone will certainly not prove a remedy against death.
en:
When high estate has lost its pride of honour meet,
Is life, that nurses this poor flesh, as nectar sweet?
ta:
மருந்தோமற்று ஊன்ஓம்பும் வாழ்க்கை பெருந்தகைமை
  பீடழிய வந்த இடத்து.

commentary: Those who give up (their) life when (their) honour is at stake are like the yark which kills itself at the loss of (even one of) its hairs.
en:
Like the wild ox that, of its tuft bereft, will pine away,
Are those who, of their honour shorn, will quit the light of day.
ta:
மயிர்நீப்பின் வாழாக் கவரிமா அன்னார்
  உயிர்நீப்பர் மானம் வரின்.

commentary: The world will (always) praise and adore the fame of the honourable who would rather die than suffer indignity.
en:
Who, when dishonour comes, refuse to live, their honoured memory
Will live in worship and applause of all the world for aye!
ta:
இளிவரின் வாழாத மானம் உடையார்
  ஒளிதொழுது ஏத்தும் உலகு.

commentary: One's light is the abundance of one's courage; one's darkness is the desire to live destitute of such (a state of mind.)
en:
The light of life is mental energy; disgrace is his
Who says, 'I 'ill lead a happy life devoid of this.'
ta:
ஒளிஒருவற்கு உள்ள வெறுக்கை இளிஒருவற்கு
  அஃதிறந்து வாழ்தும் எனல்.

commentary: All human beings agree as regards their birth but differ as regards their characteristics, because of the different qualities of their actions.
en:
All men that live are one in circumstances of birth;
Diversities of works give each his special worth.
ta:
பிறப்பொக்கும் எல்லா உயிர்க்கும் சிறப்பொவ்வா
  செய்தொழில் வேற்றுமை யான்.

commentary: Though (raised) above, the base cannot become great; though (brought) low, the great cannot become base.
en:
The men of lofty line, whose souls are mean, are never great
The men of lowly birth, when high of soul, are not of low estate.
ta:
மேலிருந்தும் மேலல்லார் மேலல்லர் கீழிருந்தும்
  கீழல்லார் கீழல் லவர்.

commentary: Even greatness, like a woman's chastity, belongs only to him who guards himself.
en:
Like single-hearted women, greatness too,
Exists while to itself is true.
ta:
ஒருமை மகளிரே போலப் பெருமையும்
  தன்னைத்தான் கொண்டொழுகின் உண்டு.

commentary: (Though reduced) the great will be able to perform, in the proper way, deeds difficult (for others to do).
en:
The man endowed with greatness true,
Rare deeds in perfect wise will do.
ta:
பெருமை யுடையவர் ஆற்றுவார் ஆற்றின்
  அருமை உடைய செயல்.

commentary: It is never in the nature of the base to seek the society of the great and partake of their nature.
en:
'As votaries of the truly great we will ourselves enroll,'
Is thought that enters not the mind of men of little soul.
ta:
சிறியார் உணர்ச்சியுள் இல்லை பெரியாரைப்
  பேணிக் கொள் வேம் என்னும்
  நோக்கு.

commentary: Even nobility of birth, wealth and learning, if in (the possession of) the base, will (only) produce everincreasing pride.
en:
Whene'er distinction lights on some unworthy head,
Then deeds of haughty insolence are bred.
ta:
இறப்பே புரிந்த தொழிற்றாம் சிறப்புந்தான்
  சீரல் லவர்கண் படின்.

commentary: The great will always humble himself; but the mean will exalt himself in self-admiration.
en:
Greatness humbly bends, but littleness always
Spreads out its plumes, and loads itself with praise.
ta:
பணியுமாம் என்றும் பெருமை சிறுமை
  அணியுமாம் தன்னை வியந்து.

commentary: Freedom from conceit is (the nature of true) greatness; (while) obstinacy therein is (that of) meanness.
en:
Greatness is absence of conceit; meanness, we deem,
Riding on car of vanity supreme.
ta:
பெருமை பெருமிதம் இன்மை சிறுமை
  பெருமிதம் ஊர்ந்து விடல்.

commentary: The great hide the faults of others; the base only divulge them.
en:
Greatness will hide a neighbour's shame;
Meanness his faults to all the world proclaim.
ta:
அற்றம் மறைக்கும் பெருமை சிறுமைதான்
  குற்றமே கூறி விடும்.

commentary: It is said that those who are conscious of their duty and behave with a perfect goodness will regard as natural all that is good.
en:
All goodly things are duties to the men, they say
Who set themselves to walk in virtue's perfect way.
ta:
கடன்என்ப நல்லவை எல்லாம் கடன்அறிந்து
  சான்றாண்மை மேற்கொள் பவர்க்கு.

commentary: The only delight of the perfect is that of their goodness; all other (sensual) delights are not to be included among any (true) delights.
en:
The good of inward excellence they claim,
The perfect men; all other good is only good in name.
ta:
குணநலம் சான்றோர் நலனே பிறநலம்
  எந்நலத்து உள்ளதூஉம் அன்று.

commentary: Affection, fear (of sin), benevolence, favour and truthfulness; these are the five pillars on which perfect goodness rests.
en:
Love, modesty, beneficence, benignant grace,
With truth, are pillars five of perfect virtue's resting-place.
ta:
அன்புநாண் ஒப்புரவு கண்ணோட்டம் வாய்மையொடு
  ஐந்துசால் ஊன்றிய தூண்.

commentary: Penance consists in the goodness that kills not , and perfection in the goodness that tells not others' faults.
en:
The type of 'penitence' is virtuous good that nothing slays;
To speak no ill of other men is perfect virtue's praise.
ta:
கொல்லா நலத்தது நோன்மை பிறர்தீமை
  சொல்லா நலத்தது சால்பு.

commentary: Stooping (to inferiors) is the strength of those who can accomplish (an undertaking); and that is the weapon with which the great avert their foes.
en:
Submission is the might of men of mighty acts; the sage
With that same weapon stills his foeman's rage.
ta:
ஆற்றுவார் ஆற்றல் பணிதல் அதுசான்றோர்
  மாற்றாரை மாற்றும் படை.

commentary: The touch-stone of perfection is to receive a defeat even at the hands of one's inferiors.
en:
What is perfection's test? The equal mind.
To bear repulse from even meaner men resigned.
ta:
சால்பிற்குக் கட்டளை யாதெனின் தோல்வி
  துலையல்லார் கண்ணும் கொளல்.

commentary: Of what avail is perfect goodness if it cannot do pleasing things even to those who have pained (it) ?
en:
What fruit doth your perfection yield you, say!
Unless to men who work you ill good repay?
ta:
இன்னாசெய் தார்க்கும் இனியவே செய்யாக்கால்
  என்ன பயத்ததோ சால்பு.

commentary: Poverty is no disgrace to one who abounds in good qualities.
en:
To soul with perfect virtue's strength endued,
Brings no disgrace the lack of every earthly good.
ta:
இன்மை ஒருவற்கு இனிவன்று சால்பென்னும்
  திண்மைஉண் டாகப் பெறின்.

commentary: Those who are said to be the shore of the sea of perfection will never change, though ages may change.
en:
Call them of perfect virtue's sea the shore,
Who, though the fates should fail, fail not for evermore.
ta:
ஊழி பெயரினும் தாம்பெயரார் சான்றாண்மைக்கு
  ஆழி எனப்படு வார்.

commentary: If there is a defect in the character of the perfect, (even) the great world cannot bear (its) burden.
en:
The mighty earth its burthen to sustain must cease,
If perfect virtue of the perfect men decrease.
ta:
சான்றவர் சான்றாண்மை குன்றின் இருநிலந்தான்
  தாங்காது மன்னோ பொறை.

commentary: If one is easy of access to all, it will be easy for one to obtain the virtue called goodness.
en:
Who easy access give to every man, they say,
Of kindly courtesy will learn with ease the way.
ta:
எண்பதத்தால் எய்தல் எளிதென்ப யார்மாட்டும்
  பண்புடைமை என்னும் வழக்கு.

commentary: Affectionateness and birth in a good family, these two constitute what is called a proper behaviour to all.
en:
Benevolence and high born dignity,
These two are beaten paths of courtesy.
ta:
அன்புடைமை ஆன்ற குடிப்பிறத்தல் இவ்விரண்டும்
  பண்புடைமை என்னும் வழக்கு.

commentary: Resemblance of bodies is no resemblance of souls; true resemblance is the resemblance of qualities that attract.
en:
Men are not one because their members seem alike to outward view;
Similitude of kindred quality makes likeness true.
ta:
உறுப்பொத்தல் மக்களொப்பு அன்றால் வெறுத்தக்க
  பண்பொத்தல் ஒப்பதாம் ஒப்பு.

commentary: The world applauds the character of those whose usefulness results from their equity and charity.
en:
Of men of fruitful life, who kindly benefits dispense,
The world unites to praise the 'noble excellence.'
ta:
யனொடு நன்றி புரிந்த பயனுடையார்
  பண்புபா ராட்டும் உலகு.

commentary: Reproach is painful to one even in sport; those (therefore) who know the nature of others exhibit (pleasing) qualities even when they are hated.
en:
Contempt is evil though in sport. They who man's nature know,
E'en in their wrath, a courteous mind will show.
ta:
நகையுள்ளும் இன்னா திகழ்ச்சி பகையுள்ளும்
  பண்புள பாடறிவார் மாட்டு.

commentary: The (way of the) world subsists by contact with the good; if not, it would bury itself in the earth and perish.
en:
The world abides; for 'worthy' men its weight sustain.
Were it not so, 'twould fall to dust again.
ta:
பண்புடையார்ப் பட்டுண்டு உலகம் அதுஇன்றேல்
  மண்புக்கு மாய்வது மன்.

commentary: He who is destitute of (true) human qualities (only) resembles a tree, though he may possess the sharpness of a file.
en:
Though sharp their wit as file, as blocks they must remain,
Whose souls are void of 'courtesy humane'.
ta:
அரம்போலும் கூர்மைய ரேனும் மரம்போல்வர்
  மக்கட்பண்பு இல்லா தவர்.

commentary: It is wrong (for the wise) not to exhibit (good) qualities even towards those who bearing no friendship (for them) do only what is hateful.
en:
Though men with all unfriendly acts and wrongs assail,
'Tis uttermost disgrace in 'courtesy' to fail.
ta:
நண்பாற்றார் ஆகி நயமில செய்வார்க்கும்
  பண்பாற்றார் ஆதல் கடை.

commentary: To those who cannot rejoice, the wide world is buried darkness even in (broad) day light.
en:
To him who knows not how to smile in kindly mirth,
Darkness in daytime broods o'er all the vast and mighty earth.
ta:
நகல்வல்லர் அல்லார்க்கு மாயிரு ஞாலம்
  பகலும்பாற் பட்டன்று இருள்.

commentary: The great wealth obtained by one who has no goodness will perish like pure milk spoilt by the impurity of the vessel.
en:
Like sweet milk soured because in filthy vessel poured,
Is ample wealth in churlish man's unopened coffers stored.
ta:
பண்பிலான் பெற்ற பெருஞ்செல்வம் நன்பால்
  கலந்தீமை யால்திரிந் தற்று.

commentary: He who does not enjoy the immense riches he has heaped up in his house, is (to be reckoned as) dead, (for) there is nothing achieved (by him).
en:
Who fills his house with ample store, enjoying none,
Is dead. Nought with the useless heap is done.
ta:
வைத்தான்வாய் சான்ற பெரும்பொருள் அஃதுண்ணான்
  செத்தான் செயக்கிடந்தது இல்.

commentary: He who knows that wealth yields every pleasure and yet is so blind as to lead miserly life will be born a demon.
en:
Who giving nought, opines from wealth all blessing springs,
Degraded birth that doting miser's folly brings.
ta:
பொருளானாம் எல்லாமென்று ஈயாது இவறும்
  மருளானாம் மாணாப் பிறப்பு

commentary: A burden to the earth are men bent on the acquisition of riches and not (true) fame.
en:
Who lust to heap up wealth, but glory hold not dear,
It burthens earth when on the stage of being they appear.
ta:
ஈட்டம் இவறி இசைவேண்டா ஆடவர்
  தோற்றம் நிலக்குப் பொறை.

commentary: What will the miser who is not liked (by any one) regard as his own (in the world to come) ?
en:
Whom no one loves, when he shall pass away,
What doth he look to leave behind, I pray?
ta:
எச்சமென்று என்எண்ணுங் கொல்லோ ஒருவரால்
  நச்சப் படாஅ தவன்.

commentary: Those who neither give (to others) nor enjoy (their property) are (truly) destitute, though possessing immense riches.
en:
Amid accumulated millions they are poor,
Who nothing give and nought enjoy of all they store.
ta:
கொடுப்பதூஉம் துய்ப்பதூஉம் இல்லார்க்கு அடுக்கிய
  கோடியுண் டாயினும் இல்.

commentary: He who enjoys not (his riches) nor relieves the wants of the worthy is a disease to his wealth.
en:
Their ample wealth is misery to men of churlish heart,
Who nought themselves enjoy, and nought to worthy men impart.
ta:
ஏதம் பெருஞ்செல்வம் தான்துவ்வான் தக்கார்க்கொன்று
  ஈதல் இயல்பிலா தான்.

commentary: The wealth of him who never bestows anything on the destitute is like a woman of beauty growing old without a husband.
en:
Like woman fair in lonelihood who aged grows,
Is wealth of him on needy men who nought bestows.
ta:
அற்றார்க்கொன்று ஆற்றாதான் செல்வம் மிகநலம்
  பெற்றாள் தமியள்மூத் தற்று.

commentary: The wealth of him who is disliked (by all) is like the fruit-bearing of the etty tree in the midst of a town.
en:
When he whom no man loves exults in great prosperity,
'Tis as when fruits in midmost of the town some poisonous tree.
ta:
நச்சப் படாதவன் செல்வம் நடுவூருள்
  நச்சு மரம்பழுத் தற்று.

commentary: Strangers will inherit the riches that have been acquired without regard for friendship, comfort and charity.
en:
Who love abandon, self-afflict, and virtue's way forsake
To heap up glittering wealth, their hoards shall others take.
ta:
அன்பொரீஇத் தற்செற்று அறநோக்காது ஈட்டிய
  ஒண்பொருள் கொள்வார் பிறர்.

commentary: The short-lived poverty of those who are noble and rich is like the clouds becoming poor (for a while).
en:
'Tis as when rain cloud in the heaven grows day,
When generous wealthy man endures brief poverty.
ta:
சீருடைச் செல்வர் சிறுதுனி மாரி
  வறங்கூர்ந் தனையது உடைத்து.

commentary: True modesty is the fear of (evil) deeds; all other modesty is (simply) the bashfulness of virtuous maids.
en:
To shrink abashed from evil deed is 'generous shame';
Other is that of bright-browed one of virtuous fame.
ta:
கருமத்தால் நாணுதல் நாணுந் திருநுதல்
  நல்லவர் நாணுப் பிற.

commentary: Food, clothing and the like are common to all men but modesty is peculiar to the good.
en:
Food, clothing, and other things alike all beings own;
By sense of shame the excellence of men is known.
ta:
ஊணுடை எச்சம் உயிர்க்கெல்லாம் வேறல்ல
  நாணுடைமை மாந்தர் சிறப்பு.

commentary: As the body is the abode of the spirit, so the excellence of modesty is the abode of perfection.
en:
All spirits homes of flesh as habitation claim,
And perfect virtue ever dwells with shame.
ta:
ஊனைக் குறித்த உயிரெல்லாம் நாண்என்னும்
  நன்மை குறித்தது சால்பு.

commentary: Is not the modesty ornament of the noble ? Without it, their haughtiness would be a pain (to others).
en:
And is not shame an ornament to men of dignity?
Without it step of stately pride is piteous thing to see.
ta:
அணிஅன்றோ நாணுடைமை சான்றோர்க்கு அஃதின்றேல்
  பிணிஅன்றோ பீடு நடை.

commentary: The world regards as the abode of modesty him who fear his own and other's guilt.
en:
As home of virtuous shame by all the world the men are known,
Who feel ashamed for others, guilt as for their own.
ta:
பிறர்பழியும் தம்பழியும் நாணுவார் நாணுக்கு
  உறைபதி என்னும் உலகு.

commentary: The great make modesty their barrier (of defence) and not the wide world.
en:
Unless the hedge of shame inviolate remain,
For men of lofty soul the earth's vast realms no charms retain.
ta:
நாண்வேலி கொள்ளாது மன்னோ வியன்ஞாலம்
  பேணலர் மேலா யவர்.

commentary: The modest would rather lose their life for the sake of modesty than lose modesty for the sake of life.
en:
The men of modest soul for shame would life an offering make,
But ne'er abandon virtuous shame for life's dear sake.
ta:
நாணால் உயிரைத் துறப்பர் உயிர்ப்பொருட்டால்
  நாண்துறவார் நாணாள் பவர்.

commentary: Virtue is likely to forsake him who shamelessly does what others are ashamed of.
en:
Though know'st no shame, while all around asha med must be:
Virtue will shrink away ashamed of thee!
ta:
பிறர்நாணத் தக்கது தான்நாணா னாயின்
  அறம்நாணத் தக்கது உடைத்து.

commentary: Want of manners injures one's family; but want of modesty injures one's character.
en:
'Twill race consume if right observance fail;
'Twill every good consume if shamelessness prevail.
ta:
குலஞ்சுடும் கொள்கை பிழைப்பின் நலஞ்சுடும்
  நாணின்மை நின்றக் கடை.

commentary: The actions of those who are without modesty at heart are like those of puppet moved by a string.
en:
'Tis as with strings a wooden puppet apes life's functions, when
Those void of shame within hold intercourse with men.
ta:
நாண்அகத் தில்லார் இயக்கம் மரப்பாவை
  நாணால் உயிர்மருட்டி அற்று.

commentary: There is no higher greatness than that of one saying. I will not cease in my effort (to raise my family).
en:
Who says 'I'll do my work, nor slack my hand',
His greatness, clothed with dignity supreme, shall stand.
ta:
கருமம் செயஒருவன் கைதூவேன் என்னும்
  பெருமையின் பீடுடையது இல்.

commentary: One's family is raised by untiring perseverance in both effort and wise contrivances.
en:
The manly act and knowledge full, when these combine
In deed prolonged, then lengthens out the race's line.
ta:
ஆள்வினையும் ஆன்ற அறிவும் எனஇரண்டின்
  நீள்வினையால் நீளும் குடி.

commentary: The Deity will clothe itself and appear before him who resolves on raising his family.
en:
'I'll make my race renowned,' if man shall say,
With vest succinct the goddess leads the way.
ta:
குடிசெய்வல் என்னும் ஒருவற்குத் தெய்வம்
  மடிதற்றுத் தான்முந் துறும்.

commentary: Those who are prompt in their efforts (to better their family) need no deliberation, such efforts will of themselves succeed.
en:
Who labours for his race with unremitting pain,
Without a thought spontaneously, his end will gain.
ta:
சூழாமல் தானே முடிவெய்தும் தம்குடியைத்
  தாழாது உஞற்று பவர்க்கு.

commentary: People will eagerly seek the friendship of the prosperous soul who has raised his family without foul means.
en:
With blameless life who seeks to build his race's fame,
The world shall circle him, and kindred claim.
ta:
குற்றம் இலனாய்க் குடிசெய்து வாழ்வானைச்
  சுற்றமாச் சுற்றும் உலகு.

commentary: A man's true manliness consists in making himself the head and benefactor of his family.
en:
Of virtuous manliness the world accords the praise
To him who gives his powers, the house from which he sprang to raise.
ta:
நல்லாண்மை என்பது ஒருவற்குத் தான்பிறந்த
  இல்லாண்மை ஆக்கிக் கொளல்.

commentary: Like heroes in the battle-field, the burden (of protection etc.) is borne by those who are the most efficient in a family.
en:
The fearless hero bears the brunt amid the warrior throng;
Amid his kindred so the burthen rests upon the strong.
ta:
அமரகத்து வன்கண்ணர் போலத் தமரகத்தும்
  ஆற்றுவார் மேற்றே பொறை.

commentary: As a family suffers by (one's) indolence and false dignity there is to be so season (good or bad) to those who strive to raise their family.
en:
Wait for no season, when you would your house uprear;
'Twill perish, if you wait supine, or hold your honour dear.
ta:
குடிசெய்வார்க் கில்லை பருவம் மடிசெய்து
  மானங் கருதக் கெடும்.

commentary: Is it only to suffering that his body is exposed who undertakes to preserve his family from evil ?
en:
Is not his body vase that various sorrows fill,
Who would his household screen from every ill?
ta:
இடும்பைக்கே கொள்கலம் கொல்லோ குடும்பத்தைக்
  குற்ற மறைப்பான் உடம்பு.

commentary: If there are none to prop up and maintain a family (in distress), it will fall at the stroke of the axe of misfortune.
en:
When trouble the foundation saps the house must fall,
